episodes.titleZh: 139. 【Agent的综述】和苏煜聊Agent技术史、OpenClaw Moment、边界的消弭和社会的辐射
episodes.titleEn: 139. Agent Survey: Yu Su on the Technical History of Agents, the OpenClaw Moment, Vanishing Boundaries, and Social Spillover
episodes.summaryEn: Xiaojun frames agents as AI's 2026 high-frequency term and asks Yu Su to explain the technical lineage behind them. The episode follows agents from logical agents and neural agents through semantic parsing and language agents, then connects the OpenClaw moment, universal digital agents, continual learning, and the different ways technology radiates through China and the U.S.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
前面在福莉和广密的节目中，我们深入揭示了AI的进化从第一幕Chat走向第二幕Agent。可以说，Agent是2026年的高频词。
关于Agent，我一直非常希望深入技术原理层，给大家做一次技术讲解，能够让我们一起非常清晰地了解技术的脉络。
今天我邀请的是俄亥俄州立大学计算机系教授、也是创业公司NeoCognition创始人，苏煜。苏煜是少数见证过Agent演化史的学者，研究方向是Language Agent。我们从更长周期复盘了Agent的技术演进史，尤其是最近三年Language Agent的快速进化。此外，苏煜也是2025年“斯隆研究奖”得主。
接下来，是我们对Agent的技术综述。
祝大家五一假期学习快乐啦^。^

00:27:21 人类的进化史来说，语言非常晚发生，但对人类文明有了指数型发展

00:48:56 OpenClaw Moment和ChatGPT Moment有非常多相似的地方
00:55:10 中美科技辐射的pattern不同，中国更全民化，在应用层的动作更快

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: After earlier episodes with Luo Fuli and Guangmi, we traced AI's evolution from Act One, chat, toward Act Two, agents. Agent may be the defining buzzword of 2026. I wanted to go deeper into the technical principles and make the lineage clear. Today I invited Yu Su, professor of computer science at Ohio State and founder of NeoCognition. He is one of the few scholars who has witnessed the evolution of agents, with a research focus on language agents.

outline.textZh: 苏煜是谁
outline.textEn: Who is Yu Su?

outline.textZh: Agent的技术演进史：从Logical Agent（1960-90s） → Neural Agent（2000年以后，神经代理） → Semantic Parsing（另一边的故事，语义解析） → Language Agent（语言代理）
outline.textEn: The technical evolution of agents: from logical agents in the 1960s-1990s to neural agents after 2000, semantic parsing as a parallel story, and language agents

outline.textZh: 人类的进化史来说，语言非常晚发生，但对人类文明有了指数型发展
outline.textEn: In human evolution, language arrived very late, yet it drove exponential progress in civilization

outline.textZh: 过去三年发展速度比过去几十年都要快，复盘Language Agent上的关键工作
outline.textEn: The past three years moved faster than the previous decades: reviewing key work on language agents

outline.textZh: At the end of the day，大家想要的就是universal digital agent；边界的消弭和coding有关
outline.textEn: At the end of the day, what people want is a universal digital agent; coding is tied to vanishing boundaries

outline.textZh: 我是最早从Semantic Parsing转型做Language Agent的学者之一
outline.textEn: I was one of the first scholars to move from semantic parsing into language agents

outline.textZh: OpenClaw Moment和ChatGPT Moment有非常多相似的地方
outline.textEn: The OpenClaw Moment has many similarities to the ChatGPT Moment

outline.textZh: 中美科技辐射的pattern不同，中国更全民化，在应用层的动作更快
outline.textEn: China and the U.S. radiate technology differently: China is more mass-market and moves faster at the application layer

outline.textZh: 创业新公司NeoCognition，最近融完了一轮$40M的seed round
outline.textEn: New startup NeoCognition recently closed a $40M seed round

outline.textZh: 聊聊Continual Learning、世界模型、交互（GUI vs. CLI）
outline.textEn: Continual learning, world models, and interaction: GUI vs. CLI

outline.textZh: Agent现在最大的瓶颈是什么？对2026年Agent进展的预期？
outline.textEn: What is the biggest bottleneck for agents now, and what should we expect in 2026?

outline.textZh: 各个大厂都在Agent上bet什么，有什么有意思的bets？
outline.textEn: What are the major companies betting on in agents, and which bets are interesting?

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Universal Agents Move From Research Lineage To Application Shock
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Notes-derived case: the episode frames agents as a decades-old technical lineage that only becomes commercially explosive once language becomes the control surface. The hidden investable angle is not a single agent app, but the collapse of boundaries between semantic parsing, language agents, continual learning, and everyday GUI or CLI work.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The agent wave may reward companies that translate long academic agent history into mass-market workflows, not just teams with the largest base models.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor platforms that turn language agents into universal digital workers with continual-learning loops, especially where China-style fast application radiation can expose product-market fit earlier.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source calls agent the likely defining buzzword of 2026 and compares the OpenClaw moment to the ChatGPT moment, implying a new adoption window.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: AI is shifting from chat interfaces toward universal digital agents that operate across tools, interfaces, and social contexts.
evidenceSignals: Yu Su's lineage runs from logical agents to neural agents, semantic parsing, and language agents.
The episode says the past three years moved faster than prior decades of agent work.
OpenClaw is described as having similarities to the ChatGPT moment.
China is described as faster and more mass-market at the application layer.
Continual learning, world models, and GUI versus CLI interaction are explicit agenda items.
watchlist: OpenClaw-style agent benchmarks becoming product procurement criteria
NeoCognition translating language-agent research into commercial deployments
Chinese mass-market agent apps showing retention beyond novelty
Continual-learning features that persist user context across sessions
risks: The source is episode notes rather than a full transcript.
Universal digital agents may remain too broad to monetize cleanly.
Fast application-layer diffusion can create shallow copycats before durable moats form.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes explicitly compare OpenClaw with the ChatGPT moment.
thesisTable.implication: Adoption curves can accelerate faster than normal enterprise software cycles.

thesisTable.claim: Application speed may matter as much as model depth.

thesisTable.implication: Early winners may appear in consumer and workflow products before infrastructure settles.

episodes.titleZh: 138. 对罗福莉3.5小时访谈：AI范式已然巨变！OpenClaw、Agent范式很吃后训练、卡的分配、组织平权
episodes.titleEn: 138. A 3.5-Hour Interview with Luo Fuli: The AI Paradigm Has Already Changed, OpenClaw, Agent Post-Training, GPU Allocation, and Organizational Egalitarianism
episodes.summaryEn: Luo Fuli argues that large-model competition has entered an agent era dominated by post-training, especially RL scaling for agent behavior. The conversation links OpenClaw, Claude Opus 4.6, MiMo-V2, GPU allocation, research agility, and organizational changes inside AI labs responding to a fast paradigm shift.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
2026年，大模型战争全面升级，掀开了第二幕——从Pre-train（预训练）主导的Chat时代，转向Post-train（后训练）主导的Agent时代。
在AI范式巨变之际，我访谈了人工智能研究员罗福莉。罗福莉曾供职阿里达摩院、DeepSeek，目前是小米大模型团队负责人，主导研发了MiMo-V2系列模型。她在网络空间有很多标签，例如“AI天才少女”，但她不喜欢这个称呼。
这次是她的第一次访谈，也是她第一次进行长时间的技术访谈。
我们系统性地谈论了，2026年由Claude Opus 4.6、OpenClaw等技术变量所触发的AI巨震，以及后续结构性影响。
在这个生产力大爆炸的时代，人人都有危机感。哪怕是对于亲手训练模型的研究者来说。
“我之前认为我们自己做的工作已经足够有创造力、足够不会被Skill化、不会被Workflow化。但我现在发现，它竟然也能！那它可不可以训出更强的模型？自己左脚踩右脚就提升了？——这是这一两年会发生的事情。”
当人类的知识与智慧内化为模型能力，未来的人类去做什么？我们的社会如何消化这次剧烈的技术变革？——这些宏大的时代命题我们不得而知。
但无论如何，这仍然是一次信息密度极大的访谈——你能从中看见，当面临一次巨大技术范式转折时，一家AI Lab内部，在技术押注、资源调配、组织与人员等诸多方面的系列举措。而它应对巨变的根基是，文化与价值观的成型。
罗福莉对当下有一些关键的技术判断：
•  Anthropic的路径是正确的，这是当下共识。
•  在路径更清晰的情况下，国内大模型团队进入加速追赶的状态。现在大家在Pre-train上的代差是基本没有的，或者说非常接近。
•  现在至少跟23年要去追平Pre-train的差距一样，大家很all in，要去做好Agent的Post-train。更具体说，是在Agent上怎么做好RL的scaling。
•  系统从“以Rollout推理引擎为核心”，转变为“以Agent为核心”的一个更复杂系统。这对团队提出了更高的要求：必须具备足够敏捷性，能够快速开发出适配当前时代的RL Infra系统。
•  接下来两三个月，大家怎么发生变化，是考验团队整体研究水平、技术敏捷程度，以及怎么拥抱新的范式来做研究的关键。
•  一个for更长期的事情：我们不会在1T水平上走太久。如果要拿到下一个阶段的领先，就要寻求更大规模scaling。到底是去scaling模型的参数量，还是去scaling什么东西？以及要在什么样的芯片上去scaling？——这是当下立即需要去决策和判断的，这才决定了大半年过后谁更领先。在卡的

I interviewed AI researcher Luo Fuli, formerly of Alibaba DAMO Academy and DeepSeek and now head of Xiaomi's large model team, where she leads MiMo-V2. This is her first long-form technical interview. We discuss the AI shock triggered by Claude Opus 4.6, OpenClaw, and related technical variables, along with the structural effects inside an AI lab.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Post-Training Becomes The Agent Era's Control Point
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Notes-derived case: Luo Fuli's framing moves the center of model competition from pre-training scale to post-training behavior shaping. The investable wedge is that agent reliability, self-iteration, and GPU allocation become organizational capabilities, not just model-card metrics.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The next AI lab moat may be a post-training operating rhythm rather than raw pre-training budgets.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Back teams that can repeatedly improve agent behavior through RL scaling, self-evolution loops, and fast resource allocation, because those capabilities map directly to productivity transformation.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source says the large-model war has escalated from chat-era pre-training to agent-era post-training in 2026.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Competition is shifting from building models that answer to building agents that can iteratively act, learn, and coordinate.

episodes.titleZh: Luo Fuli: OpenClaw, Agent Frameworks — The AI Paradigm Has Already Changed Dramatically!
episodes.titleEn: Luo Fuli: OpenClaw and Agent Frameworks - The AI Paradigm Has Already Changed Dramatically!
episodes.summaryEn: This English-titled companion episode presents Luo Fuli's first long-form technical interview after OpenClaw and Xiaomi's MiMo-V2 releases. The discussion centers on why OpenClaw marks a turning point for agent frameworks, how post-training and GPU allocation are changing, and why environment can matter more than prior experience in fast-moving AI teams.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
In 2026, the large model war has fully escalated, unveiling Act Two—from the Chat era dominated by Pre-train to the Agent era dominated by Post-train.
At a time when the AI paradigm is undergoing dramatic change, I interviewed AI researcher Luo Fuli. Luo Fuli previously worked at Alibaba DAMO Academy and DeepSeek, and is currently the head of Xiaomi's large model team, leading the development of the MiMo-V2 series of models. She has many labels in cyberspace, such as "AI prodigy girl," but she dislikes this title.
This was her first interview, and also her first long-form technical interview.
We systematically discussed the AI earthquake triggered in 2026 by technical variables such as Claude Opus 4.6 and OpenClaw, as well as the subsequent structural impacts.
"The next two or three months will be very exciting," Luo Fuli said.
Next is my interview with Luo Fuli.
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: In 2026, the large-model war has fully escalated, unveiling Act Two: from the chat era dominated by pre-training to the agent era dominated by post-training. At this moment of dramatic paradigm change, I interviewed Luo Fuli, who previously worked at Alibaba DAMO Academy and DeepSeek and is now head of Xiaomi's large model team. She leads the MiMo-V2 series. We discussed the AI earthquake triggered by Claude Opus 4.6, OpenClaw, and other technical variables, as well as the structural impacts that follow.

alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The transcript-derived companion episode adds an operating insight: in fast agent paradigms, people can learn abilities within months if the environment is right. That implies frontier AI teams may compound through culture, allocation, and framework exposure faster than through traditional experience filters.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Agent labs may outperform by redesigning research environments around rapid learning rather than recruiting only for prior specialization.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Underwrite agent teams that combine strong post-training systems, flexible GPU allocation, and talent environments where newcomers can rapidly become productive.
alphaLens.whyNow: The episode is explicitly recorded after OpenClaw and MiMo-V2 releases, at a moment the source calls a dramatic 2026 paradigm change.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Agent competition is becoming a systems problem that blends model scale, post-training, frameworks, and team structure.

episodes.titleZh: A 4-hour Interview with Carina Hong: AI for Math, Lean, Proofs from The Book, and Intuition
episodes.titleEn: A 4-Hour Interview with Carina Hong: AI for Math, Lean, Proofs from The Book, and Intuition
episodes.summaryEn: Carina Hong discusses Axiom, AI for Math, and the strange public story around Ken Ono leaving a tenured role to join her startup. The interview moves from mathematical beauty and discovery to fundraising, formalizing mathematics in Lean, proof intuition, and the all-or-nothing nature of a moonshot.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
In 2026, a wave of Neo Labs emerged across the United States. Neo Labs is a concept that has gained traction in recent years, referring to a new breed of AI research organizations — founded by leading AI researchers, targeting foundation models or next-generation agentic systems, backed by substantial funding, and driven by a distinctly research-first ethos. Our guest for Episode 133 was Saining Xie from AMI Labs. Today, we welcome another.
She is the youngest guest ever featured on Zhang Xiaojun Podcast, a post-2000s Chinese woman whom corners of the internet have come to call “the math girl.” Her name is Carina Hong Letong — and this is her first interview conducted in Chinese.
Her focus is AI for Math. The company she founded, Axiom, has just closed a $200 million Series A.
What first drew widespread attention to her was a single headline: Ken Ono, a 57-year-old tenured American professor, abruptly resigned his position to go work for a 24-year-old Chinese woman.
We talked about many things — mathematics and beauty, whether math is created or discovered, proofs and axioms from *Pr
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: In 2026, a wave of Neo Labs emerged across the United States: AI research organizations founded by leading researchers, aimed at foundation models or next-generation agentic systems, backed by large funding rounds, and driven by a research-first ethos. Carina Hong Letong is the youngest guest ever featured on the show, a post-2000s Chinese woman sometimes called the math girl online. Her focus is AI for Math, and her company Axiom has just closed a $200 million Series A. This is her first Chinese-language interview.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Formal Math Turns Research Taste Into A Data Moat
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The transcript-derived source makes Axiom look less like a math assistant and more like a bet on converting mathematical intuition into formal, machine-verifiable substrate. The overlooked alpha is that Lean formalization and proof taste can create proprietary training terrain where generic internet-scale models are weak.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: AI for Math may be closer to a foundation-model category than a vertical productivity tool.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor AI-for-Math companies that can combine elite mathematician taste, Lean formalization, and proof-generation loops into a scarce corpus and evaluation system.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source places Axiom inside a 2026 wave of U.S. Neo Labs raising large rounds for foundation models or next-generation agentic systems.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Mathematical work is shifting from informal human proof culture toward formalized reasoning workflows that AI can verify and extend.

episodes.titleZh: 137. 对洪乐潼的4小时访谈：AI for Math、把数学变成Lean、数学天书中的证明、直觉、被创造与被发现的
episodes.titleEn: 137. A 4-Hour Interview with Carina Hong: AI for Math, Turning Math into Lean, Proofs from The Book, Intuition, and Created vs. Discovered
episodes.summaryEn: This Chinese episode introduces Carina Hong as the show's youngest guest and the founder of Axiom, an AI for Math company that recently raised a $200 million Series A. The conversation spans mathematical beauty, whether math is created or discovered, Ken Ono's email, fundraising, Lean formalization, and the intuition behind ambitious AI for Math work.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
2026年，美国诞生了一系列的Neo Labs（新型研究室）。Neo Labs是近年兴起的一个新概念，专指由顶级AI研究者创立、以基础模型或新一代智能体系为目标、融资规模巨大、研究导向很强的一类新型AI实验室。我们133集的嘉宾，来自AMI Labs的谢赛宁是，今天我们邀请的是另外一位。
她是《商业访谈录》至今最年轻的一位嘉宾，是一位00后华人女孩，网络空间有人会叫她“数学少女”——她的名字是洪乐潼Carina，这是她第一次接受中文访谈。
她探索的方向是AI for Math，所创办的公司Axiom（公理）刚完成2亿美元的A轮融资，估值16亿美金。
而她引起很多人的关注，来自于这样一条新闻：57岁美国终身教授小野肯（Ken Ono）突然辞职，去给24岁的华人女孩打工。
我们谈论了许多数学与美、被创造的与被发现的数学、数学天书中的证明与公理、最不可能的创业者的创业旅途，当然还有AI for Math。
接下来，是我对洪乐潼的访谈。

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: In 2026, a new category of Neo Labs appeared in the United States: research-oriented AI labs founded by top researchers, pursuing foundation models or next-generation intelligent systems and raising very large rounds. Today's guest is Carina Hong Letong, the youngest guest yet on Xiaojun's Podcast, a post-2000s Chinese woman some people online call the math girl. She works on AI for Math, and Axiom, the company she founded, has just completed a $200 million Series A at a $1.6 billion valuation.

episodes.titleZh: 136. 全球大模型季报第9集：和广密聊，Coding是AGI第二幕、硅谷御三家真相、模型正成为新一代OS
episodes.titleEn: 136. Global Large Model Quarterly, Episode 9: Guangmi on Coding as AGI's Second Act, the Truth About Silicon Valley's Big Three, and Models as the Next OS
episodes.summaryEn: Guangmi argues that coding has pushed AI beyond chatbots into agents that can do work, making coding models a new accelerator for AGI. The episode surveys Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, Meta, xAI, Chinese model teams, models as a next-generation operating system, and the social risks of white-collar deflation and unemployment.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
今天给大家带来的是全球大模型季报第9集，这一集的情绪十分复杂。
一方面，你会看到正在急速进化的AI革命。Coding把AI从聊天机器人Chatbot第一幕，推向了能够干活的Agent第二幕。研究员们已经不再亲自写代码。广密带来的核心判断是，Coding是新的”AI加速器"，正在加速AGI实现，领先的Coding模型就像领先的GPU。
另一方面，社会层面随之进入白领通缩与失业的窗口。整个社会准备好了应对这么剧烈变化的AI革命吗？
OUTLINE:
00:02:00 第9集季报的概览
00:03:28 硅谷体感与洞察
过去一个Q，智能水平进步幅度赶上2025全年，推背感非常强，AI奇点时刻应该很快就到
最关键转折点就是Opus 4.5 → Opus 4.6模型，算是GPT-3 → GPT-4跨代际提升的模型
今年6月之前可能还会再有一个从GPT-3→4水平幅度的跨越，OpenAI/Anthropic新模型都很强，Mythos/Spud下一代也开始训练了，继续加速
Coding会是头部的放大器，最顶尖1的人才能放大10-50倍的生产力
你是否相信Code可以表达数字世界的绝大多数任务？“语言即世界，代码即方案”
如果领先的模型公司不重视Coding，大概率会掉出第一梯队
Coding就像Amazon最早卖书一样，借助卖完书把所有仓储物流用户全都拉通了
Coding在整个AGI的历史进程中处在什么位置？
做好Coding的难点到底在哪里？不只是技术knowhow，更是战略组织和文化问题

All in Coding不是day1这个团队就想清楚的
创始人特别hands-on亲自看训练数据，重视数据和重视技术细节刻在基因里
偏好“underdog”而非大人物，文化面试非常严格，尤其是看重一个人的文化特质
00:33:35 OpenAI
在这个时代的过去胜利秘诀，可能是下个时代的毒药
过去OpenAI在ChatGPT的成功，让他们专注ToC忽视了Coding
重点说下OpenAI做的不好的地方，可以在构建组织的时候借鉴一下
OpenAI即将要发布的新模型，是真正意义上的GPT-5吧

Gemini 3被高估
Coding严重落后，Google最大的战略失误
Google是最领先的追随者，资源和布局上没啥绝对短板，但战略跟随OpenAI/Anthropic，掉队可能性很低

最有机会的挑战者，已经取代xAI，成为硅谷4号种子

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: This is the ninth Global Large Model Quarterly, and the mood is complicated. On one side is an AI revolution evolving at high speed: coding pushes AI from Act One, the chatbot, into Act Two, agents that can actually do work. Researchers are no longer writing code themselves. Guangmi's core judgment is that coding is the new AI accelerator, speeding up AGI, while leading coding models are becoming like leading GPUs. On the other side, society is entering a window of white-collar deflation and job loss.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Coding Models Become The New AI Accelerator Layer
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Notes-derived case: Guangmi's strongest claim is that coding pushed AI from chat into work-performing agents. The hidden investment implication is that leading coding models may function like strategic infrastructure, closer to GPUs or operating systems than developer utilities.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The most important coding AI companies may be priced like infrastructure primitives because they accelerate AGI itself.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor coding-agent platforms, harness engineering providers, and model layers that become the default operating substrate for AI-driven work.
alphaLens.whyNow: The episode says coding is AGI's second act and that researchers are no longer writing code themselves.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: AI moves from answering questions to generating, testing, and operating software as a labor substitute.

episodes.titleZh: 135. 和自然选择创始人Tristan聊，Elys、赛博分身、灵魂、Context的获取与流动和AI社交网络
episodes.titleEn: 135. A Conversation with Natural Selection Founder Tristan on Elys, Cyber Avatars, Soul, Context Flow, and AI Social Networks
episodes.summaryEn: Tristan explains Elys as an early AI-native social product built around cyber avatars that can proactively socialize and turn online context into real-world connections. The episode frames AI social networking around context flow, agency, privacy tradeoffs, and a product philosophy rooted in the idea that humans are lonely.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
春节节后，一款AI native产品Elys引起了小圈层人的关注，这是一款还在测试，尚且没有全面上线的AI社交产品。某种程度上，它展现了AI时代社交产品的一些雏形：打造赛博分身、分身替你去主动社交、为你转化成真实世界的连接。
今天我邀请了自然选择创始人Tristan（张筱帆）来聊聊。他们公司目前有两款产品，分别是AI游戏Eve和AI社交Elys，它们背后的精神内核都是：“人类太孤独了。”
接下来，就是我对Tristan的访谈。

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: After the Spring Festival, an AI-native product called Elys drew attention in a small circle. It was still in testing and had not fully launched, but in some ways it revealed an early shape of social products in the AI era: create a cyber avatar, let that avatar proactively socialize for you, and turn those interactions into real-world connections. Today I invited Tristan Zhang, founder of Natural Selection, whose company has two products: the AI game Eve and the AI social product Elys.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: AI Social Networks Compete On Context, Not Profiles
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Notes-derived case: Elys is framed around context flow, not matching, feeds, or user tags. The alpha is that AI-native social products may monetize by owning high-resolution personal context and delegating social agency to avatars.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The next social network may look less like a feed and more like a context broker that acts on the user's behalf.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor AI social products that can earn permission to hold context, create cyber avatars, and convert agent-mediated interactions into real-world connections.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source says Elys revealed an early shape of social products in the AI era while still in testing after Spring Festival.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Social networking shifts from low-dimensional profiles and tags to context-rich agents with delegated agency.

episodes.titleZh: Xie Chen : Data Survey — History, Landscape, Pyramid Structure, and Recipes for AI and Robotics Data
episodes.titleEn: Xie Chen: Data Survey - History, Landscape, Pyramid Structure, and Recipes for AI and Robotics Data
episodes.summaryEn: Xie Chen returns for an industry survey of data as one of AI's three pillars, alongside compute and algorithms. The episode contrasts LLMs hitting a data wall with robotics data remaining scarce, then discusses synthetic data, data pyramids, pricing, recipes, and the companies competing to build robot brains.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
In 2026, beyond guest interviews, we also hope to launch a series of industry-focused solo episodes featuring insiders. Today’s episode is one such attempt.
We know that data, compute, and algorithms are the three pillars driving artificial intelligence. In this episode, we invite back our returning guest, Xie Chen, to focus on one of these pillars—data—and to present a comprehensive industry overview.
Data for large language models is running into a “wall,” while data for robotics remains a vast desert. How does the data industry actually operate? And how might emerging approaches—such as synthetic data and data pyramids—reshape the landscape?
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: In 2026, beyond guest interviews, we hope to launch industry-focused solo episodes featuring insiders. This is one such attempt. Data, compute, and algorithms are the three pillars driving artificial intelligence, and today we invite back Steve Xie Chen, founder and CEO of Guanglun Intelligence, to focus on data. Large language model data is running into a wall, while robotics data remains a vast desert. How does the data industry operate, and how might synthetic data or data pyramids reshape it?

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Robotics Data Becomes The Scarce Side Of AI Infrastructure
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The transcript-derived source separates the mature LLM data problem from the still-barren robotics data problem. The hidden alpha is that robot-brain competition may be bottlenecked less by model ambition and more by data recipes, simulation, and pyramid construction.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The next data boom may be in robotics data, not in another tranche of web text.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor companies that can collect, synthesize, price, and package robotics data into reusable recipes for robot-brain builders.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source says LLM data is hitting a wall while robotics data remains a vast desert, making data a live 2026 infrastructure topic.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Data shifts from passive web-scale extraction to engineered, domain-specific, synthetic, and embodied datasets.

2026年，除了嘉宾访谈，我们也很希望推出一些内部人士的产业单集。今天就是这样的一次尝试。

alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Data companies may regain pricing power by selling structured recipes rather than undifferentiated datasets.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Track data platforms that can build pyramids for LLM and robotics customers, especially where simulation data fills expensive real-world gaps.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source frames 2026 as a moment when LLM data hits a wall while robotics data remains underbuilt.

episodes.titleZh: 133. 对谢赛宁的7小时马拉松访谈：世界模型、逃出硅谷、AMI Labs、两次拒绝Ilya、杨立昆、李飞飞和42
episodes.titleEn: 133. A 7-Hour Marathon Interview with Saining Xie: World Models, Escaping Silicon Valley, AMI Labs, Twice Turning Down Ilya, Yann LeCun, Fei-Fei Li, and 42
episodes.summaryEn: Saining Xie recounts his research path from vision to world models and the founding of AMI Labs with Yann LeCun. The interview combines biography, research taste, the history of representations, AMI's large seed round, and Xie's critique that Silicon Valley has become deeply LLM-pilled.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
2026年春节，在中国机器人登上春晚的喜乐时分，纽约刚下过一场暴雪——这是近几年以来纽约最凛冽的一个冬天。
2月雪后的一天，在布鲁克林一栋略显凌乱的楼房，我与谢赛宁开启了这场始料未及的马拉松式访谈。我们从下午2点开始，直到凌晨时分散去。
就在前不久，他与图灵奖得主杨立昆（Yann LeCun）等，踏上了一条关于“世界模型”的创业旅程。他们创立的AMI Labs（Advanced Machine Intelligence Labs），目前仅25人、在没有任何产品的情况下，完成10.3亿美元Seed轮融资，投前估值35亿美元。
“Silicon Valley is very LLM-pilled。”AMI联合创始人兼首席科学官谢赛宁说道，“硅谷已经深陷于LLM（大语言模型），完全被它催眠了。”
这位出生于1990年的华人科学家，毕业于上海交通大学与加利福尼亚大学圣地亚哥分校，现于纽约大学任教。创业之前，他曾在Google DeepMind担任研究科学家。更早之前，他在Meta的FAIR实验室担任研究科学家4年。他的论文总计引用数近10万次，曾共同提出Diffusion Transformers（DiT）。
这是谢赛宁第一次接受访谈。在黑暗浸透的霓虹纽约街头，空气中弥漫着没化完冰雪与难闻烟雾的混合味道。就像谢赛宁的表达，总是带着多重的混合感。

Not long before, he and Turing Award winner Yann LeCun had started a journey around world models. Their AMI Labs had only 25 people and no product, yet completed a $1.03 billion seed round at a $3.5 billion pre-money valuation.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: World Models Become The Anti-LLM Consensus Trade
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Notes-derived case: Saining Xie's story is framed as a conscious escape from a Silicon Valley that has become LLM-pilled. The hidden alpha is that representation-first world models can attract enormous capital precisely because they offer a credible alternative path after internet-scale language saturation.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: A 25-person lab with no product can be rationally valuable if it owns a differentiated research path away from LLM monoculture.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor world-model and representation-first labs where exceptional researchers can create a non-language-native foundation for embodied, visual, or physical intelligence.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source says AMI Labs raised a $1.03 billion seed round at a $3.5 billion pre-money valuation while pursuing world models.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Frontier AI broadens from downloading the internet to downloading humanity through richer representations of the world.

Customer or partner interest before product launch
Evidence that world models solve tasks LLMs struggle with

episodes.titleEn: A 7-Hour Marathon Interview with Saining Xie: World Models, AMI Labs, Yann LeCun, Fei-Fei Li, and 42
episodes.summaryEn: This companion version introduces Saining Xie as an ordinary rather than chosen figure, then follows his academic life and move into AMI Labs. It emphasizes world models, representations, research taste, the AMI financing story, and his claim that Silicon Valley is hypnotized by large language models.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
In the spring of 2026, as China celebrated the Chinese New Year with humanoid robots dancing on the national stage, New York was reeling from a brutal blizzard—the harshest winter the city had seen in years. In a cluttered apartment in Brooklyn, my conversation with Saining Xie began.
By March 2026, Xie, alongside Turing Award laureate Yann LeCun, had embarked on a high-stakes venture into "World Models." Their brainchild, AMI Labs (Advanced Machine Intelligence Labs), consists of just 25 people and zero products. Yet, it has already secured a $1.03 billion Seed round at a pre-money valuation of $3.5 billion.
This is the titan among 2026’s "AI Neo Labs," setting a record for the largest Seed round in European history. Statistically, its initial funding ranks second globally among Neo Labs, trailing only Thinking Machines Lab, founded by former OpenAI CTO Mira Murati ($2 billion Seed).
"Silicon Valley is very LLM-pilled," says Xie, AMI’s co-founder and Chief Science Officer. "The Valley is deep in the trenches of Large Language Models—it’s completely hypnotized."
Born in 1990, the Chi

episodes.transcriptZhExcerpt: 哈喽 大家好 我是小珺 今天这期节目我们来到了美国纽约 此时正是中国的春节 纽约刚刚下过了一场大雪 这是近几年以来 纽约最冷的一个冬天 街道上还有许多没有化开的冰雪 但是今天这场对话 让我感受到了一种 冰解冻封后的人间烟火气息 今天坐在我对面的 是华人青年科学家谢赛宁 他刚刚和图灵奖得主杨立昆一起 踏上了创业的旅程 他们的neo lab AMI Labs 刚刚完成了第一笔超大规模的融资 目前团队规模为25人 谢赛宁一直告诉我 他不是那个天选之子 他是普通的那一个 那接下来就是我对谢赛宁的访谈 Ilya给我打电话 就 我什么都没说 我就把OpenAI拒了 他们发给我一个offer 然后我说我不去 抱歉 但是有爱的同时一定就有恨 它是一体两面 （哈哈哈） 我今天早上我们在纽约 就是布鲁克林拍空镜 我觉得我很喜欢这里 因为我住在时代广场那边 我觉得那边 还是一个非常刻板印象的纽约 但是好像来到这里 就是一个充满了艺术气息 和生活气息的纽约 是呀 我觉得Dumbo这边当然非常艺术了 对吧 很多电影里面 之前有个韩国电影的叫 过往人生 他们那里面 你可能看到了 旋转木马 然后那边的Dumbo大桥 是吧 时代广场只有游客才会去啊 我就是游客 真正的纽约人是不会去的啊 但其实NYU附近其实也很好 那边叫做 Greenwich Village 然后那边也是一个村 然后那边也很有生活气息啊 你为什么会来纽约做学术呀 这好像不是一个很多人的选择 倒也不是吧 但是历史比较长了 确实是 各种原因吧 我觉得啊 当然 也是因为我确实比较向往这个城市 对 向往这个城市里面的很多的元素啊 它的一些人 然后包括

alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The next frontier lab winner may be the one that rejects language-first assumptions most coherently.

alphaLens.whyNow: The source places AMI Labs' launch and mega seed in March 2026, amid visible robot and world-model excitement.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: AI ambition shifts from internet text compression toward models that represent and predict the world more directly.

episodes.titleZh: GALAXEA Founder Gao Jiyang: Catfish Effect, Zeng Guofan, Waymo vs. Momenta, and Why Xu Huazhe Left
episodes.titleEn: GALAXEA Founder Gao Jiyang: Catfish Effect, Zeng Guofan, Waymo vs. Momenta, and Why Xu Huazhe Left
episodes.summaryEn: Gao Jiyang presents GALAXEA and embodied AI through a lens of efficiency, survival, and engineering decomposition rather than technological romanticism. The episode covers his upbringing, lessons from Zeng Guofan, Waymo and Momenta, supply-chain difficulty, robotics data recipes, robot brains, and Xu Huazhe's pending departure.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
I’ve long had a question: why is it that in China’s embodied AI industry, I have yet to meet someone with the kind of intense technological romanticism embodied by figures like Liang Wenfeng or Yang Zhilin? At times, that has left me feeling a little disappointed.
Then I met Gao Jiyang. He seems almost like the opposite of a tech romantic — relentlessly pursuing efficiency, survival, and rigorous engineering decomposition.
At the time of this interview, GALAXEA’s co-founder Xu Huazhe was about to depart. Perhaps that, too, is a signal of something.
Gao told me that robotics is an industry with an exceptionally long chain — to a large extent, you have to stick your head into the dirt.
What follows is my conversation with Gao Jiyang, Founder and CEO of GALAXEA.
Here’s to moving forward together with AI in 2026.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Embodied AI Rewards Supply-Chain Pragmatists
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The transcript-derived source makes Gao Jiyang compelling because he rejects technological romanticism in favor of efficiency, survival, and decomposition. The alpha is that embodied AI may be won by operators who treat robots as long-chain manufacturing systems, not as model demos.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The most durable robotics companies may look more like disciplined industrial integrators than charismatic AI labs.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor embodied-AI teams with full-device manufacturing discipline, data-recipe rigor, and robot-brain strategy grounded in supply-chain execution.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source presents GALAXEA during a moment of founder transition and heightened focus on China's embodied AI industry.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Robotics shifts from romantic model narratives toward integrated engineering across hardware, data, software, and supply chain.
evidenceSignals: Gao is described as pursuing efficiency, survival, and engineering decomposition.
Robotics is called an exceptionally long-chain industry.
The source contrasts Waymo and Momenta.
Supply-chain difficulty and robotics data recipes are included.
Xu Huazhe's pending departure is a major context signal.

Robot-brain performance tied to data recipes

risks: The caption source is translated and limited.

thesisTable.implication: Investors should diligence operations as deeply as algorithms.

episodes.titleZh: 132. 对星海图创始人高继扬的3小时访谈：鲶鱼、曾国藩、Waymo与Momenta的两面、一只狼与许华哲的离开
episodes.titleEn: 132. A 3-Hour Interview with GALAXEA Founder Gao Jiyang: Catfish, Zeng Guofan, the Two Sides of Waymo and Momenta, a Wolf, and Xu Huazhe's Departure
episodes.summaryEn: This Chinese episode frames Gao Jiyang as a pragmatic counterpoint to the tech-romantic style Xiaojun expected in embodied AI. It explores his engineering-first worldview, the robotics supply chain, full-device manufacturing, data recipes, robot brains, and the signal implied by Xu Huazhe's departure from GALAXEA.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
我一直有一个疑惑，为什么在中国具身智能行业里，至今我尚且没有遇到像梁文锋、杨植麟这样带有浓重的技术浪漫主义色彩的人。这让我有时有点失落。
直到我认识了高继扬，他似乎是技术浪漫的反面，追求极致的效率、生存与工程拆解。
这次的访谈时候，星海图的联合创始人许华哲正要离职。这也许是某种信号。
高继扬告诉我，机器人是一个链条极长的的行业——很大程度上，你要把头伸进泥土里。
接下来，就是我对星海图创始人兼CEO高继扬的访谈。
期待2026年，我们和AI共同进步！

alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Notes-derived case: the episode's phrase about putting one's head into the dirt is the core business insight. Embodied AI may punish investors who search only for elegant model stories while underweighting manufacturing, supply chain, and data recipe grind.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The winner in Chinese embodied AI may be the company that accepts operational messiness earliest.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Back robotics teams that combine robot-brain ambition with owned manufacturing insight, reliable supply-chain control, and data pipelines built for physical variance.
alphaLens.whyNow: The notes mention Xu Huazhe's upcoming departure from GALAXEA and use it as a signal inside a young embodied-AI market.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Embodied AI is moving from lab demos into full-stack industrial execution where hardware, data, and model choices are inseparable.

episodes.titleZh: Yin Qi: On Chairing StepFun, the Seduction of Intelligence, and a Brutal Long-Horizon Game
episodes.titleEn: Yin Qi: On Chairing StepFun, the Seduction of Intelligence, and a Brutal Long-Horizon Game
episodes.summaryEn: Yin Qi discusses his new role as chairman of StepFun while also chairing Qianli Tech. The episode reflects on a new chapter for him and for StepFun, the transition from AI 1.0 to AI 2.0, and the tradeoffs of playing a long, brutal competition in large models.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
Today’s guest is Yin Qi. In addition to serving as Chairman of autonomous driving technology company Qianli Tech, he has recently taken on a new role: Chairman of large-model AI company StepFun.
In this conversation, we talk about his new identity, a new chapter, and his reflections on the transition from AI 1.0 to AI 2.0.
What follows is my interview with Yin Qi. Here’s to growing alongside AI in 2026.
Disclaimer: This content does not constitute investment advice.
---
今天的嘉宾是印奇。他在担任智能汽车技术公司千里科技的董事长之外，最近也迎来一个新的身份：出任大模型公司阶跃星辰的董事长。
这次，我和他聊了聊新身份、新阶段与从AI 1.0到2.0转轨与反思。
接下来是我对印奇的访谈。期待2026年，我们和AI共同进步！
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Today's guest is Yin Qi. In addition to serving as chairman of autonomous driving technology company Qianli Tech, he has recently taken on a new role: chairman of large-model AI company StepFun. In this conversation, we talk about his new identity, a new chapter, and his reflections on the transition from AI 1.0 to AI 2.0. What follows is my interview with Yin Qi. This content does not constitute investment advice, and the discussion is framed as a business and technology conversation.

alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Large-model investing may be entering a governance and endurance phase, not just a benchmark-chasing phase.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Watch late-stage model companies where experienced operators can impose long-horizon discipline without killing technical ambition.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source says Yin Qi recently became StepFun chairman while already chairing Qianli Tech, marking a new chapter for StepFun in 2026.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: The market is moving from AI 1.0 excitement to AI 2.0 strategic endurance and tradeoff management.

episodes.titleZh: 130. 张月光创业两年首次访谈：妙鸭不是AI Native产品、流程到上下文设计、One Way Door和乙女游戏
episodes.titleEn: 130. Zhang Yueguang's First Interview After Two Years of Startup Building: Miaoya Was Not AI Native, From Flow to Context Design, One-Way Doors, and Otome Games
episodes.summaryEn: Zhang Yueguang explains his path from building the viral AI photo product Miaoya to leaving Alibaba and founding Muyan Zhiyu. He argues that Miaoya was not truly AI-native, describes the shift from process design to context design, and discusses Star Sleep, Dokie, one-way-door products, and the organization needed for AI products.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
过去两年，张月光仿佛消失了。
这位产品经理在2023年创造过妙鸭这样现象级AI产品，但在妙鸭走红后，仅3个月他便从阿里辞职。他说，这源于他独自在日本京都的鸭川边，坐了一夜，想清楚了许多重要的人生决定。
2024年初，张月光筹措资金，创业沐言智语。这家公司成为那年AI应用型公司里融资额最高的初创公司之一。
有人说，投资人在没有产品的情况下，对他“盲注”4轮，累计金额近三亿元人民币。也有人开玩笑，张月光是在拿着融资额的利息养公司。
过去两年，我和张月光见了很多次，他在尝试的产品形态每一次都有一些变化，他也并不掩饰自己的困惑，坦言的确有一段时间“看不懂”。转折来自半年前。
一位师承于互联网时代的产品经理，当他转向AI，会经历怎样的试错与求索？又要跨越怎样的认知鸿沟？——让我无比意外的是，张月光说，他后来才意识到，妙鸭根本不是AI Native的产品。
沐言智语现阶段正在做的有两款主力产品：AI乙女游戏《星眠》，和即将要推出的Agent产品Dokie。他说，他的探索期结束了，他终于找到那个他愿意集公司之重力下注的东西。
接下来是我对张月光的访谈。
期待2026年，我们和AI共同进步！
（张月光坐在鸭川边思考人生的长夜）

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Over the past two years, Zhang Yueguang seemed to disappear. In 2023, this product manager created the phenomenon-level AI product Miaoya, but three months after it went viral, he resigned from Alibaba. He says the decision came after sitting alone for a night by the Kamo River in Kyoto and thinking through major life choices. In early 2024, he raised money and founded Muyan Zhiyu, one of the highest-funded AI application startups that year. He now focuses on the AI otome game Star Sleep and the upcoming agent product Dokie.

alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Notes-derived case: Zhang Yueguang's admission that Miaoya was not AI-native is the key signal. The hidden alpha is that true AI applications may be defined by context architecture and one-way-door product choices, not by adding generative features to existing flows.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The next winning AI app may come from product managers who abandon linear workflow thinking rather than from teams with the flashiest model integration.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor AI application teams that design around context, agents, and irreversible product commitments, especially in emotionally rich verticals like otome games.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source says Zhang left Alibaba after Miaoya's viral 2023 moment, raised in early 2024, and now focuses on Star Sleep and Dokie as AI product paradigms move toward agents.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: AI product design moves from deterministic process flow to context-rich, two-stage, taste-driven agent experiences.

episodes.titleZh: Zhipu CEO Zhang Peng: Inside the IPO of China’s First Publicly Listed LLM Company
episodes.titleEn: Zhipu CEO Zhang Peng: Inside the IPO of China's First Publicly Listed LLM Company
episodes.summaryEn: Zhang Peng speaks with Xiaojun around Zhipu's IPO while the company is competing with MiniMax for the title of China's first listed large-model company. The episode uses the image of his broken leg and the idiom break a leg to frame the uncertainty, timing, and symbolic significance of Zhipu's Hong Kong listing.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
On the eve of its IPO, Zhipu’s CEO Zhang Peng broke his right leg during a business trip. When he arrived at the interview, he was leaning on a pair of crutches.
At the time, Zhipu’s listing date had not yet been finalized. The race with MiniMax for the title of “China’s first publicly listed large model company” was still ongoing, and the outcome remained uncertain. Zhang Peng mentioned a Western idiom—“break a leg”—a phrase that usually means wishing someone good luck.
A few days later, the result became clear.
Zhipu confirmed that it would debut on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange on January 8, 2026, becoming China’s first publicly listed large model company—and, by extension, the world’s first public LLM company.
At this singular moment, I sat down with Zhang Peng for this conversation.
---
上市前夕，智谱CEO张鹏在一次出差途中摔断伤了右腿。到达访谈地点时，他拄着一副拐杖。
当时，智谱的上市日期尚未明朗。它与MiniMax之间围绕“中国大模型第一股”的竞争仍在继续，结果并未尘埃落定。张鹏提到一句西方俚语：Break a leg（摔断一条腿），它通常意味着祝你好运。
几天之后，结果揭晓。 智谱确认于2026年1月8日登陆港交所，成为中国首家上市的大模型公司，这也意味着它将是“全球大模型第一股”。
在这个特殊的时刻，我与张鹏有了这场聊天。
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: On the eve of its IPO, Zhipu CEO Zhang Peng broke his right leg during a business trip and arrived at the interview on crutches. At the time, Zhipu's listing date had not yet been finalized, and its race with MiniMax for the title of China's first publicly listed large-model company was still unresolved. Zhang Peng mentioned the Western idiom break a leg, which usually means wishing someone good luck. A few days later, Zhipu confirmed that it would debut on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange on January 8, 2026.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Zhipu's IPO Turns LLM Competition Into A Public-Market Test
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Notes-derived case: the broken-leg anecdote matters because it frames the IPO as symbolic luck and uncertainty, not just a financing event. The alpha is that China's first listed LLM company creates a public valuation reference for a sector previously priced mostly in private rounds.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The most important consequence of Zhipu's listing may be comparability, not capital raised.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Use Zhipu's Hong Kong listing as a live market signal for how public investors value large-model narratives, revenue visibility, and strategic scarcity.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source says Zhipu confirmed it would debut on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange on January 8, 2026 after racing MiniMax for the title.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Large-model companies move from private narrative markets into public-market disclosure, liquidity, and valuation discipline.
evidenceSignals: Zhipu is competing with MiniMax for China's first listed large-model company title.

episodes.titleZh: 129. 全球大模型第一股的上市访谈，和智谱CEO张鹏聊：敢问路在何方？
episodes.titleEn: 129. The IPO Interview for the World's First Public Large-Model Company: Zhang Peng of Zhipu on Where the Road Leads
episodes.summaryEn: This three-hour conversation with Zhipu CEO Zhang Peng took place around the company's IPO, later confirmed for January 8, 2026 on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. Zhang Peng traces Zhipu's path from perceptual intelligence to cognitive intelligence, GPT-3 and ChatGPT shocks, scaling-law changes, open source versus closed source, and his hope that Zhipu will be remembered as an AGI trailblazer.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
上市前夕，智谱CEO张鹏在一次出差途中摔断伤了右腿。到达访谈地点时，他拄着一副拐杖。
当时，智谱的上市日期尚未明朗。它与MiniMax之间围绕“中国大模型第一股”的竞争仍在继续，结果并未尘埃落定。张鹏提到一句西方俚语：Break a leg（摔断一条腿），它通常意味着祝你好运。
几天之后，结果揭晓。
智谱确认于2026年1月8日登陆港交所，成为中国首家上市的大模型公司，这也意味着它将是“全球大模型第一股”。
在这个意味深长的时刻，我与张鹏进行了一场3小时长谈。
如果一百年后，智谱出现在人工智能的历史书中，你希望它以怎样的方式被写下？
“AGI的先行者。”张鹏想了想，说，“一个开路的人。”
这是《张小珺商业访谈录》与大家在2026年见面的第一集节目——祝大家新年快乐！期待在2026年，我们与AI共同进步！

episodes.titleZh: Manus' Final Interview Before the Acquisition: Oh, the Surreal Odyssey of 2025…
episodes.titleEn: Manus' Final Interview Before the Acquisition: Oh, the Surreal Odyssey of 2025...
episodes.summaryEn: This episode was recorded with Manus co-founder and chief scientist Peak Ji before Meta's acquisition had occurred, but released after the announcement. It became Manus' final media interview before the acquisition and reflects on the strange 2025 journey behind the company.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
This episode is truly special.
We recorded it on December 1, 2025, featuring Peak Ji, co-founder and chief scientist of Manus. Just hours before release, Meta announced its full acquisition of Manus - yet during our recording session, this historic deal hadn't yet occurred.
Ultimately, this interview became Manus' final media appearance before acquisition.
Production Note:
The rushed production timeline led to one correction needed - the chapter subtitle "Manus: From 0 to10000M ARR" should read "Manus: From 0 to100 Million ARR".
---
今天这集节目很特殊。
我们的录制时间是2025年12月1日，嘉宾是Manus联合创始人兼首席科学家季逸超（Peak）。就在刚过去的凌晨，Meta宣布全资收购Manus。而在节目录制的彼时，收购事件尚未发生。
最终，这期节目成为了Manus最后的访谈。
这集制作周期非常仓促！
勘误：视频的章节小标题中，“Manus：从0到1亿美金ARR”应该改为：“Manus：从0到1亿Millions ARR”

Ultimately, the interview became Manus' final media appearance before the acquisition. The rushed production also required a correction: the chapter subtitle should refer to Manus going from zero to 100 million ARR, not the erroneous wording in the video.

episodes.titleZh: 127. 大模型季报跨年对谈：和广密预言一场AI War、两大联盟和第三个范式Online Learning
episodes.titleEn: 127. Global Large Model Quarterly New Year Conversation: Guangmi Predicts an AI War, Two Alliances, and Online Learning as the Third Paradigm
episodes.summaryEn: In the eighth Global Large Model Quarterly and the third year-end conversation with Guangmi, the episode maps the AI War across giants, alliances, and model labs. It argues that alternating leadership among GPT, Claude, and Gemini is normal, and that after pre-training and RL, online learning may be the crucial third paradigm.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
这里是《全球大模型季报》的第8集，也是我和广密跨年对谈的第三年了！
这集节目将带你看到，在对AGI开始充斥着现实主义情绪回归的当下，全球AI War形成了怎样的战队、阵营和联盟？各个前沿实验室在探索哪些新范式？硅谷又涌现出了哪些新型的研究实验室？
如果你对全球大模型季报有更多的想法或者建议，欢迎大家在评论区留言，我们都会看到。
2025的最后，期待我们和AI共同进步！
AI War：一场全球巨头都输不起的竞争

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: This is the eighth episode of the Global Large Model Quarterly, and the third year of my cross-year conversation with Guangmi. The episode looks at a moment when realism is returning to talk of AGI: what teams, camps, and alliances are forming in the global AI War, what new paradigms frontier labs are exploring, and what new research labs are emerging in Silicon Valley. At the end of 2025, we hope to keep progressing together with AI, while discussing bubbles, commercialization, GPUs, TPUs, and online learning.

outline.textEn: Starting, inevitably, with the AI bubble

outline.textEn: The speed of OpenAI's commercialization

outline.textEn: Looking across the board: the main drivers and camps in the AI War, NVIDIA GPUs vs. Google TPUs

outline.textZh: Google越强，越会形成反Google联盟，OpenAI越强也会形成反OpenAI联盟
outline.textEn: The stronger Google gets, the more an anti-Google alliance forms; the stronger OpenAI gets, the more an anti-OpenAI alliance forms

outline.textZh: Gemini崛起，大家会担心OpenAI会怎么办？怎么看待这两家的竞争？
outline.textEn: With Gemini rising, should people worry about OpenAI, and how should we view their competition?

outline.textZh: 另一个判断是：最终的最终，ChatGPT会融合传统Search，最终也会吃掉传统Search广告的份额
outline.textEn: Another judgment: eventually ChatGPT will merge with traditional search and take a share of search advertising

outline.textEn: People no longer treat Google as the Nokia of AI, but Google's crisis is not truly over

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Online Learning Becomes The Third Frontier Paradigm
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Notes-derived case: Guangmi's AI War map treats alternating GPT, Claude, and Gemini leadership as normal rather than decisive. The hidden alpha is that the next durable edge may come from online learning after pre-training and RL, while alliances form against whichever giant looks strongest.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Model leaderboard swings may be noise; the structural signal is which camps master online learning and alliance leverage.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Track frontier labs, chip ecosystems, and alliance structures that can support online learning at scale across user interactions and deployed systems.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source frames the end of 2025 as a realistic AGI moment involving bubbles, commercialization, GPUs, TPUs, and online learning.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Frontier AI competition moves from static model releases toward adaptive systems that learn online and are embedded in geopolitical-scale alliances.
evidenceSignals: The episode predicts an AI War with teams, camps, and alliances.
It says alternating leadership among GPT, Claude, and Gemini is normal.
Online learning is described as a crucial third paradigm after pre-training and RL.
NVIDIA GPUs versus Google TPUs are explicitly discussed.
The notes describe anti-Google and anti-OpenAI alliances forming as those companies strengthen.
watchlist: Evidence of frontier labs deploying online-learning loops
Alliance formation around anti-Google or anti-OpenAI positions
GPU versus TPU procurement choices
Commercialization speed versus infrastructure commitments
risks: The source is notes-derived.
Online learning claims may be hard to verify externally.
Alliance narratives can shift quickly with model performance.

thesisTable.evidence: The source says GPT, Claude, and Gemini alternating leadership is normal.
thesisTable.implication: Investors should avoid overreacting to single model releases.

thesisTable.claim: Online learning may define the next edge.
thesisTable.evidence: The episode calls it the crucial third paradigm.
thesisTable.implication: Infrastructure for live adaptation becomes strategically valuable.

episodes.titleZh: 126. 和红杉郑庆生聊：经济史的流量革命、人类行为模式的不可预期，与创始人性格
episodes.titleEn: 126. A Conversation with Sequoia China's Zheng Qingsheng: Traffic Revolutions in Economic History, Unpredictable Human Behavior, and Founder Personality
episodes.summaryEn: Zheng Qingsheng looks back on 20 years of Chinese venture capital through three technology waves: internet, mobile internet, and AI. He frames consumer internet history as a sequence of traffic revolutions, reflecting on Douban, Dianping, short video, podcasts, AI hardware, and why new human behavior patterns are hard to predict.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
在前几集节目，我们的年终回顾系列【站在2025年之外】，收录了金沙江朱啸虎、真格戴雨森、Altimeter Capital Freda Duan的声音。
今天我们将延续这个系列节目，嘉宾是红杉中国合伙人郑庆生。
郑庆生提供了一个更宏大的视角——他把时间尺度拉长到20年，回看中国从互联网、移动互联网到AI的三轮技术浪潮；又进一步，把视角放进了更宏观的经济史中，用“流量革命”来试图寻迹下一代to C流量节点的端倪。
很巧的是，他于2005年入行风险投资业，今年是他做投资人的20年。
在投资人的本职工作外，他也是一名新产品体验官、一位经济史爱好者。

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: This year-end conversation continues the series Looking Beyond 2025, after episodes with Xiaohu Zhu, Yusen Dai, and Freda Duan. Zheng Qingsheng brings a broader lens: he stretches the time scale to 20 years, reviewing China's three technology waves from the internet and mobile internet to AI. He also places that history inside a larger economic-history framework, using the idea of a traffic revolution to search for clues to the next consumer traffic node. By coincidence, he entered venture capital in 2005, making this his twentieth year as an investor.

outline.textZh: 从1984年开始学习编程讲起
outline.textEn: Starting with learning to program in 1984

outline.textZh: 2005年成为投资人以后：从盛大战投到挚信资本再到红杉资本
outline.textEn: After becoming an investor in 2005: from Shanda's strategic investment team to Trustbridge and then Sequoia Capital

outline.textZh: 豆瓣和大众点评对我投资生涯的影响：我对此充满了兴趣，是对人类前沿行为模式的探讨
outline.textEn: How Douban and Dianping shaped my investing career: I was fascinated by them as explorations of frontier human behavior

outline.textZh: 我对阿北（豆瓣创始人）的印象：“他和豆瓣是合一的”
outline.textEn: My impression of Abei, founder of Douban: he and Douban were one

outline.textZh: 我对张涛（大众点评创始人）的印象：“更成熟的有敏感产品洞察力的企业家”
outline.textEn: My impression of Zhang Tao, founder of Dianping: a more mature entrepreneur with sharp product instincts

outline.textZh: 现在回头看，Web2.0是在人类社会第一次数字化之后做了一波线上内容的产品创新，再往后，共享经济是又做了一次线上、线下结合的大规模创新
outline.textEn: In hindsight, Web 2.0 was a wave of online content-product innovation after society's first digitization, followed by the sharing economy's large-scale online-offline innovation

outline.textZh: 一位经济史爱好者眼中的中国创投20年：05年以前、10年以后、15年以后（Pinterest的范式直接影响了后来的内容平台）
outline.textEn: Twenty years of Chinese venture capital through the eyes of an economic-history enthusiast: before 2005, after 2010, and after 2015, with Pinterest's paradigm directly shaping later content platforms

outline.textZh: 站在历史的后视镜看，各个内容平台的诞生、繁荣、陨落
outline.textEn: Looking in the historical rear-view mirror at the birth, prosperity, and decline of content platforms

outline.textZh: 人类天然会进化到和自己本来不需要学习、不需要长时间成本投入，就能认知这个世界的方式，AI是不是也回到这点？
outline.textEn: Humans naturally evolve toward ways of understanding the world that require less learning and less time; is AI returning to that point too?

outline.textZh: 个人视角聊聊抖音、小红书、哔哩哔哩产品和他们的founders
outline.textEn: A personal view of Douyin, Xiaohongshu, Bilibili, and their founders

outline.textZh: 总结：“人类新的行为模式总体是不可预期的”
outline.textEn: Conclusion: new human behavior patterns are generally unpredictable

outline.textZh: 我对于个人投资的审美和反思
outline.textEn: My personal investing taste and reflections

alphaLens.alphaTitle: The Next Consumer Winner Is A Traffic Revolution, Not A Feature
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Notes-derived case: Zheng Qingsheng reads consumer internet history as successive traffic revolutions rather than product categories. The alpha is that AI consumer investing should hunt for the next traffic node where human behavior becomes easier, faster, and less cognitively demanding.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: AI consumer platforms should be judged by whether they create new traffic behavior, not whether they add AI to old content formats.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor founders who can identify frontier human behavior patterns and build AI-native distribution loops before they become obvious to incumbent platforms.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source looks back across internet, mobile internet, and AI after 20 years of Chinese VC history to search for the next consumer traffic node.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Consumer platform competition shifts from content-format innovation to AI-mediated behavior and attention compression.
evidenceSignals: Zheng frames consumer internet history as traffic revolutions.
The episode reviews Douban, Dianping, short video, and podcasts.
The notes ask whether AI is returning to lower-learning, lower-time ways of understanding the world.
AI hardware appears in the episode's keyword list.
Founder personality and unpredictable human behavior are central themes.
watchlist: AI products that create new daily traffic loops
Behavioral shifts toward lower-learning interfaces
AI hardware as a new consumer traffic surface
Founders with strong product-behavior intuition
risks: The source is notes-derived.
Human behavior shifts are hard to predict even with historical analogies.
Incumbent platforms may absorb AI traffic nodes quickly.

thesisTable.evidence: The source uses traffic revolution as its economic-history frame.
thesisTable.implication: New AI platforms need distribution breakthroughs, not only features.

thesisTable.claim: Behavior prediction is the scarce skill.
thesisTable.evidence: The episode emphasizes unpredictable human behavior.
thesisTable.implication: Founder taste and behavioral insight deserve premium diligence.

episodes.titleZh: 125. 与Altimeter合伙人Freda聊：下注OpenAI、Robinhood往事，美国资本坏小孩、算盘与泡沫
episodes.titleEn: 125. With Altimeter Partner Freda: Betting on OpenAI, Robinhood Stories, Silicon Valley's Bad Kids, Spreadsheets, and Bubbles
episodes.summaryEn: Altimeter Capital partner Freda Duan analyzes leading Silicon Valley companies from an investor's seat, including OpenAI, Anthropic, NVIDIA, Snowflake, and Robinhood. The episode weighs AI infrastructure spending, business models, public and private market logic, founder archetypes, and whether the AI boom should be called a bubble.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
在年终对话系列【站在2025年之外】的前两集节目中：
朱啸虎提出“三年不会有泡沫”，“泡沫论调纯属无稽之谈”；
戴雨森则预测，2026年是“Year of R”，将会是一个现实回归之年。
今天推出的是系列第三集节目，嘉宾来自一线的硅谷视角。
1个多月前，在2025年11月初，Sam Altman上了一档由美国基金Altimeter Capital创始人主持的播客节目，在主持人连续追问OpenAI如何为1.4万亿美元级别算力与基础设施承诺买单时，Sam称：“If you want to sell your shares, I’ll find you a buyer. Enough.”（“如果你想卖掉你的股份，我可以帮你找到买家。够了。”）——随后，AI板块整体出现波动，关于AI是否存在泡沫的讨论进一步升温。
我们今天的嘉宾Freda Duan就来自这个名叫Altimeter Capital的基金，她担任合伙人。
Altimeter是一个硅谷科技基金，横跨一二级。在一级市场投资案例有OpenAI、Anthropic、字节跳动等，在二级市场投资案例有NVIDIA、Snowflake、Robinhood等。
这集节目，Freda将深入分析美国这些明星公司，给他们的巨额投入算算账；她也从一线硅谷投资人的视角聊聊，美国资本的新秩序，他们眼中的坏小孩、反叛者、刺猬型和哪吒型创始人，以及泡沫。
2025年，让我们和AI共同进步！
（录制于2025年11月）

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: The third episode in the year-end series brings a front-line Silicon Valley perspective. A little more than a month earlier, Sam Altman appeared on a podcast hosted by Altimeter Capital's founder and was pressed on how OpenAI would pay for infrastructure commitments at the scale of $1.4 trillion. Freda Duan, a partner at Altimeter, joins the show to analyze the economics behind major American technology companies, the new order of U.S. capital, the bad kids and rebels investors admire, and the debate over bubbles.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: AI Infrastructure Commitments Need Public-Market Math
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Notes-derived case: Freda Duan's investor lens turns OpenAI, NVIDIA, Snowflake, Robinhood, and Anthropic into a capital-allocation puzzle rather than a technology ranking. The hidden alpha is that AI winners must reconcile infrastructure spending with revenue pillars under public-market logic.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The AI bubble question is less about hype and more about whether revenue architecture can finance infrastructure commitments.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor AI and AI-adjacent companies where revenue pillars, compute spending, and capital-market access can be modeled rather than hand-waved.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source references Sam Altman being pressed on how OpenAI would pay for infrastructure commitments at the scale of $1.4 trillion.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: AI investing moves from narrative TAM to spreadsheet-level underwriting of compute, revenue, and financing capacity.
evidenceSignals: The episode analyzes OpenAI's business model and four revenue pillars.
It compares OpenAI with Netflix in running the numbers.
Infrastructure commitments at $1.4 trillion scale are a setup point.
The notes identify AI, re-industrialization, and digitization of finance as U.S. equity lines.
NVIDIA, Snowflake, Robinhood, and Anthropic are included in the investor discussion.
watchlist: OpenAI revenue mix and financing disclosures
Compute commitment funding mechanisms
Public-market multiples for AI infrastructure beneficiaries
AI links to re-industrialization and financial digitization
risks: The source is notes-derived.
Private company economics may be opaque or changing.
Infrastructure overcommitment can outpace revenue realization.

thesisTable.claim: AI must be underwritten with spreadsheets.
thesisTable.evidence: The source says Freda runs numbers on OpenAI's model and compares it with Netflix.
thesisTable.implication: Financial discipline becomes a competitive filter.

thesisTable.claim: Infrastructure scale is the central constraint.
thesisTable.evidence: The setup mentions $1.4 trillion infrastructure commitments.
thesisTable.implication: Compute financing can define winners and losers.

episodes.titleZh: 124. 年终对话【站在2025年之外】和戴雨森聊2026年预期、The Year of R、回调、我们如何下注
episodes.titleEn: 124. Year-End Dialogue Looking Beyond 2025 with Yusen Dai: 2026 Expectations, the Year of R, Corrections, and How We Bet
episodes.summaryEn: ZhenFund managing partner Yusen Dai argues that 2026 will be the Year of R: return, research, remember, and multimodal reasoning. He reviews 2025's model and application progress, then predicts a more reality-driven period in which ROI, model-research depth, memory, and practical business models matter more.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
不知不觉，我们来到了2025年的最后一个月，在北京的初雪之中，我们希望和大家一起做一个回顾与展望系列：【站在2025年之外】。
今天的嘉宾是真格基金管理合伙人戴雨森。
在122集节目中，朱啸虎声称，三年之内不会有泡沫，泡沫论调纯属无稽之谈，创业者2026年当全速前进。
雨森今天带来全新的看法。在他看来，2026年的关键词是“The Year of R”——回报与研究会再次变得重要。某种意义上，2026年将是一个现实与回调之年。
02:00 复盘2025年
02:00 从模型侧看进展：
o1为代表的Thinking Time Scaling带来模型能力大幅提升
OpenAI、Anthropic、Google三家的旗舰模型追赶很紧，又各有特点，预期和叙事轮动
中国模型公司一年下来dominate开源生态
28:13 从应用侧看进展：
模型能力带来应用大爆发
应用是有护城河的，开始看到复杂应用在context、environment等层面产生壁垒
模型公司不能没有产品，大家都下场做最重要的第一方应用
中国AI应用出海表现不错
52:31 2025年真格出手了多少项目？20个左右
对比中美AI公司估值，中国公司对于全球来说有很高期权价值：
Thinking Machines天使轮估值在没有产品的情况下已是中国AI公司估值总和
模型公司：Mistral 14b，Kimi 4b，Mistral自己都不怎么做Pre-train了，benchmark也就是和Kimi对标
应用公司：在美国Manus这样一家几个月做到100m ARR，几十个点gross margin，MoM20%增长的公司应该是3-5bn
01:03:15 预测2026年：The Year of R
The Year of R：Return、Research、Remember、多模态Reasoning
01:03:15 Return：
为什么Return很重要？
ROI，过去3年交易的是investment，因为大家被潜在的大return吸引，但现在随着I越来越大，大家对R的落地越来越关注，因为有R才能推动未来的I
为什么我们认为2026年大家会加大对return的关注？
模型：模型能力进步是这一波AI革命最本质的驱动力，但模型的能力进步正在放缓；美国头部labs的投入（Capex，人工等）大了很多，但无法阻止中国模型低成本跟进，Scaling Law不能简单理解成为投入大力出奇迹
应用：AI应用的叙事从无所不能威胁人类的AGI收敛到现在的三种主要商业模式，是从梦想回归现实的过程
订阅制是OpenAI现在的核心商
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: In Beijing's first snow, the show begins a year-end review and outlook series called Looking Beyond 2025. The guest is ZhenFund managing partner Yusen Dai. In episode 122, Xiaohu Zhu argued there would be no bubble within three years and that founders should move full speed ahead in 2026. Dai brings a different view: 2026's keyword is the Year of R, when return and research become important again. In that sense, 2026 may be a year of reality returning and of market correction.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: 2026 Is The Year AI Returns To ROI And Research
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Notes-derived case: Yusen Dai's Year of R is a correction thesis disguised as an outlook. The alpha is that return, research, remember, and reasoning create a practical filter for AI startups after 2025's broad enthusiasm.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The best 2026 AI investments may be less about speed and more about surviving reality checks on ROI, memory, and research depth.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor startups with measurable ROI, differentiated research, memory capability, and multimodal reasoning paths rather than generic AI application narratives.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source frames 2026 as a year of reality returning and market correction after model and application progress in 2025.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: AI markets move from broad acceleration to selective correction based on returns and research substance.

episodes.titleZh: 123. 对ONE2X创始人王冠3小时访谈：生成系统、没有中间商赚差价、内容平台的权力分配
episodes.titleEn: 123. A Three-Hour Interview with ONE2X Founder Wang Guan: Generative Systems, No Middlemen Taking a Cut, and Power Distribution on Content Platforms
episodes.summaryEn: ONE2X co-founder and CEO Wang Guan discusses building Medeo, an AI video generator, after leaving Kimi where he led model products. The conversation covers data as the boundary of intelligence, video generation, generative systems, recommendation systems, AI product metrics, remote organizations, and how power may shift among platforms, creators, and consumers.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
今天的嘉宾是一位创业者，ONE2X联合创始人兼CEO王冠，他们现在的产品是AI视频生成器Medeo。
王冠是一名产品经理型的创业者，我和他认识了很长时间，那时他刚从Kimi离职出来创业，此前他是Kimi模型产品负责人。（哦对了，上一个Kimi产品离职来我们节目的是明超平。）
除了AI时代应用型公司怎么做产品、搭组织之外，由于我也是一名内容创作者，所以我也很好奇地与他讨论了许多关于新时代的内容平台，生成系统，AI、创作者与平台权力分配的话题。
2025年，期待我们和AI共同进步！

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Today's guest is Wang Guan, co-founder and CEO of ONE2X, whose current product is the AI video generator Medeo. Wang is a product-manager type founder, and I have known him for a long time, since he had just left Kimi to start his company; before that he led model products at Kimi. Beyond how AI application companies build products and organizations, I was also curious, as a content creator, to discuss new content platforms, generative systems, and the distribution of power among AI, creators, and platforms.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Generative Systems Shift Power Away From Platform Middlemen
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Notes-derived case: Wang Guan's key idea is that companies will become generative-system companies, not just AI app companies. The hidden alpha is that product-native data and video generation can blur model and application boundaries while changing the power split among platforms, creators, and consumers.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The biggest AI application winners may own generative systems and product-native data rather than rely on incumbent recommendation platforms.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor AI video and content companies that collect product-native data, reduce middleman dependence, and turn generation plus recommendation into one compounding system.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source says Wang left Kimi, founded ONE2X, and is building Medeo as AI reshapes the internet landscape and content-platform power.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Content platforms shift from recommendation systems over human-made inventory to generative systems that create, route, and learn from content.

episodes.titleZh: Xiaohu Zhu: Realism, the AI Feast and Its Bubbles, and a Fifteen-Degree Shift from Consensus
episodes.titleEn: Xiaohu Zhu: Realism, the AI Feast and Its Bubbles, and a Fifteen-Degree Shift from Consensus
episodes.summaryEn: This English-language note introduces the third installment in Xiaohu Zhu's realist AIGC story after earlier updates in March 2024 and February 2025. It frames late-2025 questions about whether AI is in a bubble, whether it will burst, how investors view 2026, and whether Nvidia or OpenAI remain attractive investments.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
In March 2024 and February 2025, I published two updates on Xiaohu Zhu  (Managing Partner of GSR Ventures) and his Chinese Realist AIGC Story.  Now, almost another year has passed.
As we stand at the tail end of 2025, several questions arise:
Is there a bubble in the AI industry? Will it burst? Are investors optimistic about 2026? Is now still a good time to invest in Nvidia or OpenAI?
— This is the third installment of Xiaohu Zhu’s realist story.
Disclaimer: This content does not constitute investment advice.
Copyright belongs to Language is World Studio.
---
2024年3月、2025年2月，我曾两次更新朱啸虎（金沙江创投主管合伙人）的中国现实主义AIGC故事，现在又过去了快1年时间。
站在2025年的尾巴上，AI产业有泡沫吗？泡沫会破吗？投资人对2026年的预期乐观吗？现在还是投资英伟达、OpenAI的好时候吗？——这里是朱啸虎现实主义故事的第三次连载。
2025年的最后一个月，还想和大家说那句：期待我们和AI共同进步！
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: In March 2024 and February 2025, the show published two updates on Xiaohu Zhu, managing partner of GSR Ventures, and his Chinese realist AIGC story. Now almost another year has passed. Standing at the tail end of 2025, the episode asks whether there is a bubble in the AI industry, whether it will burst, whether investors are optimistic about 2026, and whether this is still a good time to invest in Nvidia or OpenAI. This is the third installment of Xiaohu Zhu's realist story.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Realist AI Investing Looks Fifteen Degrees Off Consensus
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Notes-derived case: this short English note frames Xiaohu Zhu's third realist update as a late-2025 bubble check. The alpha is not a blanket bullish or bearish call, but the idea that value appears by shifting slightly away from crowded consensus while still respecting the AI feast.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The right AI trade may be neither bubble avoidance nor consensus chasing, but a small angle away from the obvious names.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Use Zhu's realist frame to evaluate Nvidia, OpenAI, and adjacent AI opportunities by crowding, entry price, and 2026 optimism rather than narrative intensity alone.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source says almost another year has passed since prior updates and asks at the tail end of 2025 whether AI is in a bubble and whether investors are optimistic about 2026.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: AI investing moves from first-order belief in the feast to second-order analysis of crowding, timing, and bubble risk.
evidenceSignals: The note is the third installment of Zhu's realist AIGC story.
It explicitly asks whether the AI industry has a bubble.
It asks whether the bubble will burst.
It asks whether Nvidia or OpenAI remain good investments.
It frames investor optimism for 2026 as a central question.
watchlist: Crowding in Nvidia and OpenAI exposure
AI valuation corrections heading into 2026
Investor behavior around consensus AI deals
Evidence of durable revenue behind AI optimism
risks: The source is notes-derived and short.
The note raises questions but provides limited detailed answers.
Crowding analysis can miss genuinely dominant compounders.

thesisTable.evidence: The note centers on whether AI is in a bubble and whether it will burst.
thesisTable.implication: Timing and crowding matter as much as technology belief.

thesisTable.claim: Obvious winners still need valuation discipline.
thesisTable.evidence: The source asks whether Nvidia or OpenAI remain good investments.
thesisTable.implication: Dominance does not automatically equal attractive entry.

episodes.titleZh: 122. 朱啸虎现实主义故事的第三次连载：人工智能的盛筵与泡泡
episodes.titleEn: 122. The Third Installment of Xiaohu Zhu's Realist Story: The Feast and Bubbles of Artificial Intelligence
episodes.summaryEn: Xiaohu Zhu revisits his realist account of AIGC near the end of 2025, arguing against the idea that an AI bubble will appear within three years. The discussion covers OpenAI's increasing realism, the fight for AI super-entry points, DeepSeek's importance, Nvidia and OpenAI holdings, and the problem of overly concentrated VC consensus.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
2024年3月、2025年2月，我曾两次更新朱啸虎的中国现实主义AIGC故事，现在又过去了快1年时间。
站在2025年的尾巴上，AI产业有泡沫吗？泡沫会破吗？投资人对2026年的预期乐观吗？现在还是投资英伟达、OpenAI的好时候吗？——这里是朱啸虎现实主义故事的第三次连载。
2025年的最后一个月，还想和大家说那句：期待我们和AI共同进步！

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: In March 2024 and February 2025, I twice updated Xiaohu Zhu's Chinese realist AIGC story, and now almost another year has passed. Standing at the tail end of 2025, the questions are whether the AI industry has a bubble, whether that bubble will burst, whether investors are optimistic about 2026, and whether it is still a good time to invest in Nvidia and OpenAI. This is the third serial installment of Zhu's realist story, with the familiar wish that we keep progressing together with AI.

outline.textZh: 越来越现实的OpenAI：你看Sam Altman就知道了，今年几乎不太提AGI了，对吧？
outline.textEn: OpenAI is becoming more realistic: just look at Sam Altman; this year he almost no longer talks much about AGI

outline.textZh: AI的超级入口之争：必然的就是超级入口之争，而且是日活之争、时长之争
outline.textEn: The battle for AI's super entry point: inevitably a fight over the super entry point, daily active users, and time spent

outline.textZh: 三年内看不到泡沫：他们讲的这些论点，我觉得都是无稽之谈
outline.textEn: No bubble visible within three years: I think those arguments are nonsense

outline.textZh: 你持有英伟达、OpenAI吗？
outline.textEn: Do you hold Nvidia or OpenAI?

outline.textZh: 大家低估了DeepSeek：如果没有DeepSeek，可能人类的AI是被几个私有公司控制的
outline.textEn: People underestimated DeepSeek: without it, human AI might have been controlled by a few private companies

outline.textZh: 和共识错开15度，那性价比一下子拉出来了，是吧？
outline.textEn: Shift fifteen degrees away from consensus, and the value-for-money suddenly appears

outline.textZh: 离开大厂三条马路，离开大厂三条马路，对吧？
outline.textEn: Move three blocks away from big tech companies

outline.textZh: 大家知道，我投不投都是十分钟
outline.textEn: As everyone knows, whether I invest or not is decided in ten minutes

outline.textZh: 今天的VC共识太集中了：每个项目上都是Club Deal（俱乐部交易），每个股份比例都很小，那怎么赚钱？——GP赚不了大钱，LP也很不开心
outline.textEn: Today's VC consensus is too concentrated: every deal is a club deal, every ownership stake is small, so how do you make money? GPs cannot make big money, and LPs are unhappy

outline.textZh: 手机游戏打个《王者荣耀》，单独抽半小时、二十分钟就够了
outline.textEn: For a mobile game like Honor of Kings, setting aside half an hour or twenty minutes is enough

alphaLens.alphaTitle: No Bubble Within Three Years Is A Super-Entry-Point Thesis
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Notes-derived case: Zhu's more detailed episode argues that AI's bubble debate should be read through super entry points, daily active users, time spent, DeepSeek's role, and VC consensus concentration. The hidden alpha is that the fight is not only model quality; it is control of the daily AI doorway at a price slightly away from consensus.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The AI bubble may not be imminent if the real prize is the super entry point, but consensus VC ownership structures can still make many deals unattractive.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor AI companies with credible entry-point control, non-consensus pricing, and defensible user time, while avoiding crowded club deals with thin ownership and weak upside.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source stands at the tail end of 2025, argues no bubble is visible within three years, and looks toward 2026 investment choices.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: AI investing shifts from generic model enthusiasm to competition over daily active users, time spent, openness, and ownership economics.
