episodes.titleZh: 121. 对DeepMind谭捷的访谈：机器人、跨本体、世界模型、Gemini Robotics 1.5和Google
episodes.titleEn: 121. An Interview with DeepMind's Jie Tan: Robotics, Cross-Embodiment, World Models, Gemini Robotics 1.5, and Google
episodes.summaryEn: Google DeepMind senior research scientist and tech lead Jie Tan explains robotics from the perspective of foundation models, reinforcement learning, simulation, and multimodal AI. The episode focuses on the robotics data problem, Gemini Robotics 1.5, cross-embodiment transfer, motion transfer, world models, tactile sensing, and the gap between demos and deployment.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
今天的嘉宾是Google DeepMind机器人团队的高级研究科学家兼技术负责人谭捷，他的研究方向是将基础模型和深度强化学习方法应用于机器人领域。
中美在机器人领域一直存在两种叙事：市场普遍认为，中国在硬件上发展更快，美国在机器人大脑设计上更领先。
本期节目中，谭捷将带我们一窥硅谷视角，尤其是Google DeepMind视角下的机器人前沿叙事。
前不久，他们刚发布了新工作 “Gemini Robotics 1.5 brings AI agents into the physical world”（Gemini Robotics 1.5将AI Agents带入物理世界），我们也聊了聊他们的最新发现。
由于嘉宾工作环境的原因，会出现一定程度的中英夹杂，还大家多多包容和支持。
02:00 机器人是在真实世界里做图形学，图形学是在simulation里做机器人
嘉宾小传：小时候喜欢打游戏，读博士读的计算机图形学
从图形学转型机器人的变轨
我在Google的第一篇论文《Sim-to-Real: Learning Agile Locomotion For Quadruped Robots》（从仿真到现实：学习四足机器人敏捷运动），开创了强化学习和seem to real在足式机器人上的应用
Paradigm Shift，过去十年第一个是强化学习，第二个是大语言模型
大语言模型对机器人的影响（大语言模型类似大脑，强化学习类似小脑）
13:06 机器人基座大模型到底是不是一个非常独立的学科？So far, not yet
今天的机器人发展到什么阶段了？
从demo到真正落地，隔十年并不是一个非常夸张的事
从我的角度来说，我不得不承认，最近几年的机器人智能发展主要还是依赖于多模态大模型
但多模态模型缺什么呢？缺少robot action的输出
当你真正有一个generalist model（通用模型）的时候，specialized model（专有模型）就完全不能与之竞争
23:44 Robotics最大问题是数据，它在一个非常复杂的unstructured environment里，可以发生任何事情
最大的问题还是数据问题
但是robotics是在一个非常复杂的unstructured environment（非结构化环境）里，可以发生任何事情
它需要极大量的、非常diverse（多元）的数据，但这些数据现在是不存在的
现在有很多startup叫data factory（数据工厂）
所谓“数据金字塔”包括哪些？
​27:52 Gemini Robotics 1.5：我们有一个
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Today's guest is Jie Tan, a senior research scientist and technical lead on Google DeepMind's robotics team. His work applies foundation models and deep reinforcement learning to robotics. In robotics, China and the United States have long carried two narratives: the market generally believes China develops hardware faster, while the United States leads in designing robotic brains. In this episode, Tan offers a Silicon Valley, and especially Google DeepMind, view of frontier robotics, including their recent work Gemini Robotics 1.5 brings AI agents into the physical world.

outline.textZh: 机器人是在真实世界里做图形学，图形学是在simulation里做机器人
outline.textEn: Robotics is graphics in the real world, while graphics is robotics in simulation

outline.textZh: 机器人基座大模型到底是不是一个非常独立的学科？So far, not yet
outline.textEn: Is a robotics foundation model really an independent discipline? So far, not yet

outline.textZh: Robotics最大问题是数据，它在一个非常复杂的unstructured environment里，可以发生任何事情
outline.textEn: The biggest problem in robotics is data; in a complex unstructured environment, anything can happen

outline.textZh: Gemini Robotics 1.5：我们有一个方法叫motion transfer，这是独门秘诀
outline.textEn: Gemini Robotics 1.5: we have a method called motion transfer, and it is our secret sauce

outline.textZh: 生成极大量仿真数据，是弥补它缺点的一个重要手段
outline.textEn: Generating huge amounts of simulated data is an important way to compensate for its weaknesses

outline.textZh: 世界模型就是Vision-Language-Vision，vision和language in，生成下一帧的图像
outline.textEn: A world model is vision-language-vision: vision and language go in, and the next image frame is generated

outline.textZh: 如果你有灵巧手，触觉就非常重要，之所以我前面觉得触觉不重要，是受限于当时的硬件
outline.textEn: If you have dexterous hands, touch becomes very important; I previously thought touch was unimportant because the hardware was limited at the time

outline.textZh: 一个有使命感的人，他不会容忍说“I’m on a wrong ship”
outline.textEn: A person with a sense of mission will not tolerate being on the wrong ship

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Robot Data Bottlenecks Move Value Toward Cross-Embodiment Software
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes imply that the scarce asset in robotics is not the robot body but reusable behavior across bodies. Gemini Robotics 1.5's emphasis on motion transfer and world models points to a stack where the winning company owns the translation layer between simulated experience, visual prediction, and real robot actuation.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The investable robotics bottleneck may be less about backing the fastest hardware maker and more about backing the model, simulation, and tactile-data layers that make heterogeneous hardware trainable.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Own picks-and-shovels for cross-embodiment learning: simulation infrastructure, robot data engines, tactile sensors, policy evaluation, and deployment tooling that can serve many robot forms rather than one branded machine.
alphaLens.whyNow: The episode frames robotics foundation models as not yet an independent discipline while also naming data, simulation, world models, and touch as the active frontier. That combination creates a pre-standardization window before physical AI converges on common training and transfer recipes.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Robotics is shifting from single-embodiment control engineering toward multimodal foundation-model systems that learn transferable motion and predict the next visual state.
evidenceSignals: The source says the biggest robotics problem is data in complex unstructured environments.
Gemini Robotics 1.5 is described as bringing AI agents into the physical world.
The notes call motion transfer a secret sauce for cross-embodiment robotics.
Simulation is presented as a major way to compensate for real-world data weakness.
Tactile sensing becomes important once dexterous hands enter the stack.
watchlist: Robotics models demonstrating transfer across materially different robot bodies.
Synthetic-to-real benchmarks that measure physical-task completion rather than demo quality.
Tactile sensor adoption in dexterous-hand platforms.
API or developer-access releases for Gemini Robotics-style embodied models.
Partnerships between model labs, simulation vendors, and robot hardware manufacturers.
risks: Notes-derived source does not provide deployment metrics, customer evidence, or cost curves.
Cross-embodiment transfer may remain lab-specific if real-world data diversity is insufficient.
Robotics hardware reliability and safety can delay monetization even when model progress is real.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes explicitly identify robotics data in unstructured environments as the biggest problem.
thesisTable.implication: Infrastructure that generates, labels, evaluates, or transfers robot experience can compound faster than single-purpose hardware.

thesisTable.claim: World models change the control stack.
thesisTable.evidence: The source defines a world model as vision-language-vision next-frame generation.
thesisTable.implication: Prediction quality becomes a core robotics KPI, not just perception accuracy.

thesisTable.claim: Touch creates a new sensor frontier.
thesisTable.evidence: The notes say touch becomes important with dexterous hands.
thesisTable.implication: Dexterous manipulation may pull tactile hardware and datasets into the critical path.

episodes.titleZh: 120. 小鹏新上任的刘先明首次访谈：Language是毒药、拆掉L、简单即美、换帅、小鹏的AI转型
episodes.titleEn: 120. Xiaopeng's Newly Appointed Liu Xianming in His First Interview: Language Is Poison, Removing L, Simple Is Beautiful, Leadership Change, and XPeng's AI Transformation
episodes.summaryEn: Liu Xianming, the new head of XPeng's autonomous driving center, gives his first interview after taking over the role in October 2025. He explains the decision to remove language from large driving models, XPeng's shift toward a physical-AI strategy, the technical and organizational resistance behind those choices, and why AI may become a decisive arena for car companies.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
今天的嘉宾是小鹏汽车自动驾驶中心负责人刘先明。
就在2025年10月9日，小鹏汽车突然宣布，原自动驾驶中心负责人李力耘将卸任，由世界基座模型负责人刘先明接任。
这意味着，刘先明成为小鹏在自动驾驶上，既谷俊丽、吴新宙（现英伟达自动驾驶中国团队负责人）、李力耘之后的第四任负责人。外界对他有诸多的好奇。
这是刘先明上任后首次接受专访。我们访谈的时间是2025年10月30日。这集节目，我们聊了聊他上任后拆掉大模型Language等关键技术决策，以及一家车企的AI战略转型。

恰好Cruise当时是第二名，加入第二名再逆袭的故事永远是令人兴奋的
加入小鹏汽车始末：2024年1月在美国办公室与何小鹏见面1小时

AI对于小鹏汽车意味着什么？“是乘法因子”

人工智能发展为什么一直在经历着拆拆拆的过程？

过去1年做过“头铁”的事情
看起来我性格很好，我也拍过桌子、发过火
“拆L”过程中遇到很大阻力，因为这很反paper里的常识
DeepSeek-OCR的反共识
现在关注的AI前沿方向
回应地平线创始人余凯的观点（“自动驾驶应该交给供应商”）
为什么国内自动驾驶还没有代际差？
AI是车企下一阶段的重要赛点，做不好会被淘汰
何小鹏对于AI的关注时间、方式和最近的3次话题
制造企业和AI企业的基因问题
接下来，对于我的挑战
小鹏智驾一号位，每个人的历史使命
《70. 和何小鹏聊，FSD、“在血海游泳”、乱世中的英雄与狗熊》 (https://www.xiaoyuzhoufm.com/episodes/6695032837236c546e4c2e0f)
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Today's guest is Liu Xianming, head of XPeng Motors' autonomous driving center. On October 9, 2025, XPeng suddenly announced that former autonomous-driving head Li Liyun would step down and Liu, previously responsible for world foundation models, would take over. This made Liu the fourth leader of XPeng's autonomous-driving effort after Gu Junli, Wu Xinzhou, and Li Liyun, creating much outside curiosity. Recorded on October 30, 2025, this is Liu's first interview after taking office, covering his removal of language from large models and the company's AI strategic transformation.

outline.textZh: 人物小记
outline.textEn: A short profile

outline.textZh: 大模型拆Language
outline.textEn: Removing language from large models

outline.textZh: 小鹏汽车向物理AI战略的转型
outline.textEn: XPeng Motors' transformation toward a physical-AI strategy

outline.textZh: 换帅的背后
outline.textEn: Behind the leadership change

alphaLens.alphaTitle: XPeng Treats Driving AI as Physical AI, Not a Chatbot in a Car
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The most important detail is the decision to remove language from large driving models. The notes suggest XPeng is making an architectural and organizational bet that physical-world prediction and control should not be mediated through human-readable language.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Automakers adding more conversational AI may be optimizing the wrong layer; the winning driving model could be less linguistic, more visuomotor, and harder for outsiders to benchmark with ordinary LLM tests.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor automakers and suppliers that can turn vehicle fleets into physical-AI training systems, especially where world models, vision-language-action alternatives, and closed-loop deployment are owned in-house.
alphaLens.whyNow: The leadership change on October 9, 2025 and Liu Xianming's first interview after taking over signal that XPeng is reorganizing autonomous driving around a physical-AI strategy. Official company context later positioned XPeng's AI Day around physical AI, making the episode's internal-change signal timely.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Autonomous driving is moving from feature delivery and smart-cabin AI toward embodied intelligence systems trained for real-world action.
evidenceSignals: Liu Xianming took over XPeng's autonomous-driving center after leading world foundation models.
The episode centers on removing language from large driving models.
XPeng's shift toward a physical-AI strategy is a named topic.
The notes mention technical and organizational resistance behind the decision.
AI is framed as a decisive arena for car companies.
watchlist: Evidence that language-free driving models improve intervention rates or long-tail scenario handling.
XPeng organizational cadence after the autonomous-driving leadership change.
Fleet data loops connecting vehicles, robots, and flying-car projects under one physical-AI stack.
Regulatory milestones for higher-level autonomous features in China and export markets.
Supplier displacement if automakers internalize model and world-model capability.
risks: The notes do not include quantitative driving-performance evidence.
Removing language could reduce interpretability or debugging convenience.
Leadership churn may signal strategic urgency but also execution instability.
Regulation and safety validation can slow commercialization of physical-AI claims.

thesisTable.evidence: The source highlights the decision to remove language from large driving models.
thesisTable.implication: Model design choices may separate AI-native automakers from feature assemblers.

thesisTable.evidence: Liu became the fourth leader of XPeng's autonomous-driving effort.
thesisTable.implication: Investors should watch whether organizational reset converts into faster product cadence.

thesisTable.evidence: The episode frames XPeng's transformation toward a physical-AI strategy.
thesisTable.implication: The auto platform could expand into robotics and mobility software leverage.

episodes.titleZh: 119. Kimi Linear、Minimax M2？和杨松琳考古算法变种史，并预演未来架构改进方案
episodes.titleEn: 119. Kimi Linear, Minimax M2? With Yang Songlin, an Archaeology of Algorithm Variants and a Preview of Future Architecture Improvements
episodes.summaryEn: MIT PhD student Yang Songlin joins to explain current AI algorithm and architecture innovation through Kimi Linear, Minimax M2, and Qwen3-Next. The episode traces attention variants, MoE, sparse attention, linear attention, full attention, Kimi Delta Attention, and why architecture work has become more important as data and compute constraints tighten.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
今天这集节目，我们将讨论一个在当下非常关键的话题：人工智能的算法与架构创新。
嘉宾是我们的往期嘉宾返场，她是MIT在读博士杨松琳，研究方向是线性注意力机制。
我们将从最新发布的几个模型Kimi Linear、Minimax M2、Qwen3-Next切入。松琳参与讨论Kimi Linear和Qwen3-Next的部分工作，是Kimi Linear论文的作者之一。
算法创新为什么在2025年变得尤为重要？
它的背后原因是，数据、算力和算法是驱动人工智能的三驾火车，在数据撞墙的无奈前提下，各个模型公司不得不重新开始“雕模型架构”，以期Scaling Law的魔法继续。而由于中国的算力相对美国有限，这反而让中国的AI算法创新走在了世界前沿。
这集节目你将听到，近几年架构最大突破是DeepSeek的MoE（混合专家模型），它让MoE成为了全球共识；而下一个突破的重要方向可能就是Attention（注意力机制）。
中国公司在Attention展开了不同技术bet（押注）：
•
截至目前已发布模型，DeepSeek正在探索Sparse Attention（稀疏注意力机制）；
•
Kimi正在探索Linear Attention（线性注意力机制）；
•
Minimax在年初的M1版本中探索Linear Attention，而在刚发布的M2版本中又回退到 Full Attention（全局注意力机制）。
节目中，松琳将讲解她参与的这篇《Kimi Linear: An Expressive, Efficient Attention Architecture》的工作，并分析以上这些公司在Attention上的不同抉择；
与此同时，她也将带领大家考古人工智能算法变种史，并预演未来算法与架构的改进方案。
本集比较硬核，会有一些专业难度，大家可以根据自己的实际需要收听嗷:)因为嘉宾的工作环境会出现中英夹杂，希望大家多多理解和支持。
04:00 个人、研究主线与线性注意力机制的探索之路
06:27 松琳做过一个开源库：flash-linear-attention（简称FLA）
07:04 怎么通俗理解Linear Attention的Linear？
11:19 聊聊最近参与的新工作，前几天刚发布的《Kimi Linear: An Expressive, Efficient Attention Architecture》（Kimi Linear：一种具有强表达能力与高效率的注意力架构）
（FLA库的另一个作者Zhang, Yu邀请）
12:20 为什么Kimi在年初开始需要重新设计注意力机制
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: This episode discusses a crucial topic today: innovation in AI algorithms and architectures. The guest is returning guest Yang Songlin, an MIT PhD student whose research focuses on linear attention. The conversation starts from recently released models including Kimi Linear, Minimax M2, and Qwen3-Next. Songlin participated in discussions around parts of Kimi Linear and Qwen3-Next and is one of the authors of the Kimi Linear paper. Behind the renewed importance of algorithmic innovation is the fact that data, compute, and algorithms drive AI, while data is hitting a wall.

outline.textZh: 个人、研究主线与线性注意力机制的探索之路
outline.textEn: Personal background, research thread, and the path of exploring linear attention

outline.textZh: 松琳做过一个开源库：flash-linear-attention（简称FLA）
outline.textEn: Songlin built an open-source library called flash-linear-attention, or FLA

outline.textZh: 怎么通俗理解Linear Attention的Linear？
outline.textEn: How to understand the linear in linear attention in plain language

outline.textZh: 聊聊最近参与的新工作，前几天刚发布的《Kimi Linear: An Expressive, Efficient Attention Architecture》（Kimi Linear：一种具有强表达能力与高效率的注意力架构）
outline.textEn: Discussing recent work she joined: Kimi Linear: An Expressive, Efficient Attention Architecture, released a few days earlier

outline.textZh: 为什么Kimi在年初开始需要重新设计注意力机制？设计的背景和目标
outline.textEn: Why Kimi needed to redesign the attention mechanism at the start of the year: background and goals

outline.textZh: 《Kimi Linear》论文重点讲解：KDA模块（Kimi Delta Attention，增量注意力机制）
outline.textEn: A focused explanation of the Kimi Linear paper: the KDA module, or Kimi Delta Attention

outline.textZh: Kimi内部有一个Scaling Ladder（规模阶梯），在一个规模下面表现好就在下一个规模下面去scale，就像通关
outline.textEn: Kimi has an internal scaling ladder: if something works at one scale, it is scaled at the next, like clearing a level

outline.textZh: Kimi Linear Attention vs DeepSeek Sparse Attention：Kimi走线性注意力路线，DeepSeek走稀疏注意力路线，都想解决长文本decoding（长上下文生成）的效率问题
outline.textEn: Kimi Linear Attention versus DeepSeek Sparse Attention: both try to solve the efficiency problem of long-context decoding

outline.textZh: Minimax从M1到M2的架构变化，从Linear Attention退回到Full Attention，为什么？
outline.textEn: Minimax's architecture change from M1 to M2: why did it retreat from linear attention back to full attention?

outline.textZh: 硅谷的注意力机制方案不方便说，但可以浅聊一下OpenAI有paper的方案
outline.textEn: Silicon Valley attention-mechanism plans cannot be discussed in detail, but OpenAI's published approach can be touched on

outline.textZh: Linear Attention从2020年发明出来开始后的前进线索
outline.textEn: The development path of linear attention since its invention in 2020

outline.textZh: 纯Linear Attention是无效的，混合注意力机制还是有很多全局注意力层，这样下限有保证
outline.textEn: Pure linear attention is ineffective; hybrid attention still keeps many global-attention layers to preserve a floor

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Linear Attention Is a Cost-Curve Bet, Not Just a Model-Architecture Debate
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes connect renewed architecture innovation to data and compute constraints. Kimi Linear, Minimax M2, Qwen3-Next, and KDA are therefore not isolated paper arguments; they are attempts to bend inference and long-context economics when scaling via more data becomes harder.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The next open-model winners may not be the teams with the largest training runs, but the teams with the best internal scaling ladders for testing architecture changes before the market recognizes the cost advantage.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Track companies and tooling that lower long-context decoding cost: linear attention libraries, sparse-attention kernels, MoE routing, KV-cache alternatives, and model-serving stacks that make new architectures production-safe.
alphaLens.whyNow: The episode says architecture work has become more important as data and compute constraints tighten. Recent model releases made attention variants visible to users and developers rather than only to researchers.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: AI competition is moving from pure parameter scaling toward architecture-level efficiency and deployment economics.
evidenceSignals: The source ties algorithmic innovation to data hitting a wall.
Kimi redesigned attention and introduced Kimi Delta Attention.
Kimi Linear and DeepSeek Sparse Attention are both described as attacking long-context decoding efficiency.
Minimax's move from M1 to M2 is framed as a retreat from linear attention back to full attention.
The guest built flash-linear-attention, showing infrastructure follows architecture research.
watchlist: Benchmark gaps between linear, sparse, and full attention under long-context serving loads.
Open-source kernel adoption for flash-linear-attention-style libraries.
Whether Kimi's internal scaling ladder yields repeatable architecture upgrades.
Production reversions from linear attention back to full attention.
Serving-cost disclosures from model labs offering long-context products.
risks: Architecture gains may disappear at larger scale or under real workloads.
Full attention may remain preferred where quality dominates serving cost.

episodes.titleZh: Li Auto Founder Li Xiang: MoE, Liang Wenfeng, VLA, Energy, Memory, Intimacy & Human Intelligence
episodes.titleEn: Li Auto Founder Li Xiang: MoE, Liang Wenfeng, VLA, Energy, Memory, Intimacy, and Human Intelligence
episodes.summaryEn: This full version of the second AI Talk with Li Auto founder and CEO Li Xiang expands a shorter released cut from April 2025. The conversation treats Li Xiang as a CEO large model with technical, strategic, and organizational experts, then moves into AI's relationship with humanity, energy, intimacy, memory, and human intelligence.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
In April 2025, I recorded the second season of AI Talk with Li Auto’s founder and CEO, Li Xiang.
The conversation lasted for hours, though only one hour was released at the time. What you’re watching today is the full version.
This episode comes out later than planned. The past few months have been hectic, and for a while, I even hesitated to release it. But as I revisited the material, I was still deeply moved. It feels like an “archive of thinking at the turning point of AI transformation.”
You can also watch it together with our 3-hour conversation from late 2024, to sense how Li Xiang’s reflections evolved and resonated across the two dialogues.
This time, I treated Li Xiang as a kind of “CEO Large Model.” Imagine him as an MoE (Mixture of Experts) architecture — in the first three rounds, I called upon his three internal “experts”: the technologist, the strategist, and the organization builder.
As the dialogue unfolded, we moved deeper — into themes of humanity, energy, intimacy, memory, and human intelligence.
The core theme of this conversation is “The Relationship Between AI

alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes use Li Xiang as a CEO large model whose experts include technologist, strategist, and organization builder. That metaphor exposes a practical alpha: the AI transition may reward companies whose leaders can dynamically route between technical judgment, product strategy, capital allocation, and human systems.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The scarce asset in AI-era incumbents may not be model access; it may be founder-level routing capacity across technology, organization, and human motivation.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Look for founder-led companies that combine deep product cadence with explicit AI organizational redesign, especially where the CEO can integrate VLA, energy, memory, and user intimacy into one operating model.
alphaLens.whyNow: The full version was released as an archive of thinking at a turning point in AI transformation. The source frames the conversation as a delayed but still relevant map of how AI changes the relationship between companies and people.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Leadership in AI-native companies is shifting from command-and-control management toward expert routing across technical systems and human relationships.

episodes.titleZh: 118. 对李想的第二次3小时访谈：CEO大模型、MoE、梁文锋、VLA、能量、记忆、对抗人性、亲密关系、人类的智慧
episodes.titleEn: 118. A Second Three-Hour Interview with Li Xiang: CEO Large Model, MoE, Liang Wenfeng, VLA, Energy, Memory, Fighting Human Nature, Intimacy, and Human Wisdom
episodes.summaryEn: Li Auto founder and CEO Li Xiang returns in a full-length second AI Talk that treats him as a CEO large model made of technical, strategic, and organizational experts. The episode moves from AI tools, foundation models, and VLA training to strategy, terminal devices in the AGI era, organizational energy, memory programs, intimate relationships, and wisdom.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
2025年4月，我与理想创始人兼CEO李想录制AI Talk第二季。那次对谈持续了很长时间，播出版仅1小时，今天你看到的是完整版。
这一集节目的发布比预期晚了些。过去几个月实在太忙了，我一度犹豫要不要继续放出。但当我重新整理这些内容时，仍然被它打动——这是一份关于人工智能技术变革的“节点式思考存档”。
你可以结合2024年底我们那场3小时谈话一起观看，感受两次对话之间，思考的延展与呼应。
这次，我把李想当作一个“CEO大模型”来提问。
假设他是一种MoE（Mixture of Experts，专家混合）架构的模型，我在对话的前三个回合调用了他的三位“专家”：技术专家、战略专家、组织专家。而当谈话深入到后半程，我们开始讨论人、能量、亲密关系、记忆程序与人类的智慧。
“AI与人的关系”，是本次对话的母题。
（录制于2025年4月）
02:35 第一章：假若你是一个CEO大模型
人类做熵减，AI做熵增
工具的三个分级：“信息工具”、“辅助工具”、“生产工具”
“生产工具”重要的衡量是：你愿意为它付钱
梁文锋极简运用了人类最佳实践
按照最佳实践是反人性的，随心所欲才满足人性
我只能做最好的自己，我一直在自己的长板延长线上
理想为什么还做基座大模型？
当时我们比较担心陈伟团队（基座模型自研团队）怎么想？这个压力挺大的
36:18 第二章：调用MoE之技术专家
李想手把手教你训VLA
达到VLA不是突变的过程，是进化的过程，经历了三个阶段
我给你讲一下VLA是怎么训的，以及VLA自己怎么去工作的
我不会做超长CoT，我的CoT链条一般两步到三步
至少5年内不会有通用Agent，但会有一个Agent OS
要顺着人性去说，逆着人性去做
如果大家不想做前面包子的积累，只想吃第10个包子，很像练《葵花宝典》
黑盒、世界模型和定价逻辑
每1万公里的验证成本，我们做到从最开始18万降到4000块钱
01:25:36 第三章：调用MoE之战略专家
2025年雁栖湖战略会
如果看战略，中间的圈是规模，圈外边有三个变量：用户需求、技术产品、组织能力
具备这四个特点的，就是AGI时代的终端：360度对物理世界感知的能力、认知决策的能力、Action的能力、反思反馈能力
到了AGI时代的终端，对于能力的要求变得不一样了
如果看到2030年，我们希望能够成为全球领先的人工智能终端企业
这是我们未来的3-6年要去解的题
李想的理想会不会太过于理想？
构建3-7人能量体
高维组织兼容低维组织
02:09:26 第四章：智慧是我们和万物的关系
我的记忆程序
创业不容易，但是没必要苦哈哈的
大女儿
我们

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Li Auto's AI Edge Is Organizational Memory, Not Only VLA
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The long-form interview turns Li Xiang's AI thinking into a four-chapter model: CEO, technical expert, strategic expert, and wisdom. The hidden signal is that Li Auto may be trying to convert founder cognition into repeatable organizational memory before AI reshapes the vehicle as a terminal device.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: In the AGI-device era, the vehicle winner may be the company that captures intimate user memory and organizational learning, not simply the company with the flashiest autonomous-driving demo.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor AI-auto platforms that combine VLA training, terminal-device strategy, and durable user memory loops, because the car can become a personal AI surface rather than a transportation-only product.
alphaLens.whyNow: The notes explicitly call the episode an archive at a turning point in AI-driven technological change. The full-length format exposes how Li Auto links AI tools, foundation models, VLA training, strategy, energy, and intimacy into one worldview.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Cars are moving from smart hardware endpoints toward persistent AI companions with memory, emotional context, and strategic platform value.

episodes.titleZh: 117. 开源一段论文探索之旅：模型范式、Infra和数据、语言、多模态的完整变迁史
episodes.titleEn: 117. Open-Sourcing a Journey Through Papers: A Complete History of Model Paradigms, Infrastructure and Data, Language, and Multimodality
episodes.summaryEn: Xie Qingchi, product lead at Meituan's Guangnian Zhiwai, shares a year-long journey of reading more than 200 AI papers and distilling 36 classics into a four-hour walkthrough. The episode presents papers as a doorway into AI history, covering model paradigms, infrastructure and data, language models, and multimodal models.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
今天的嘉宾是谢青池，他是美团光年之外的产品负责人。
一个月前，青池找到我，说他用了一年多的时间一篇一篇地啃完了200多篇AI论文，从开始全然不得要领，到后来逐渐地入门——而他希望将他的论文探索之旅开源给大家。
就这样，我们有了今天这集特别的节目。
他从200多篇论文中精选了36篇经典，4小时讲解，带你穿越AI变迁史。
他说，读论文是“给你打开一扇门”，让你能直接“与这个世界最聪明的头脑对话”。
2025年，期待我们和AI共同进步！

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Today's guest is Xie Qingchi, product lead at Meituan's Guangnian Zhiwai. A month ago, Qingchi told me he had spent more than a year working through over 200 AI papers, moving from total confusion to gradually finding his way in, and he wanted to open-source that journey for everyone. That led to this special episode. From more than 200 papers, he selected 36 classics for a four-hour explanation that takes listeners through the history of AI's evolution and its major turning points.

alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The best AI education products may sell to companies as strategic alignment infrastructure, not to individuals as technical self-improvement.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Invest in platforms that convert research histories into team workflows: canonical paper maps, technical onboarding, roadmap planning, and benchmark-linked learning.
alphaLens.whyNow: The episode exists because a product lead spent more than a year moving from confusion to an organized understanding of AI. That path mirrors what many non-research AI product teams now need at scale.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: AI knowledge work is shifting from individual expert intuition to shared, open-source learning systems.

episodes.titleZh: 116. 吴明辉口述19年史：漫长的沉浮、痛苦急转、企业级Agentic Model、现实世界的数值游戏、IPO
episodes.titleEn: 116. Wu Minghui's Oral History of 19 Years: Long Ups and Downs, Painful Pivots, Enterprise Agentic Models, Real-World Numerical Games, and an IPO
episodes.summaryEn: Minglue Technology founder, CEO, and CTO Wu Minghui tells the 19-year story of a B2B company before its planned Hong Kong listing. The episode moves from early entrepreneurship, mergers, shareholders, recommendation systems, and Miaozhen to Minglue's second startup phase, painful restructuring, enterprise AI, private data, DeepMiner, agents, and the future of enterprise services.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
今天的嘉宾吴明辉是明略科技的创始人、CEO兼CTO，明略科技于2025年8月29日获境外发行上市备案通知书，不久后将于香港上市。
这是一次上市前的访谈，吴明辉口述一家To B公司漫长的19年故事，其间经历了好多次的分分合合、沉浮与急转。你能在这里面找到许多我们节目嘉宾的身影——肖弘、李广密、杨植麟。
我们也聊了聊面向全新的AI时代，企业服务级AI与Agentic Model的前景。
但这个故事的最开始，要从他与峰瑞资本创始合伙人李丰的公司合并聊起。
2025年，我们和AI共同进步！

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Today's guest Wu Minghui is the founder, CEO, and CTO of Minglue Technology, which received overseas listing filing notice on August 29, 2025 and is expected to list in Hong Kong soon. This pre-IPO interview is Wu's oral account of a To B company's long 19-year history, full of splits, reunions, rises, falls, and sharp turns. Many previous guests appear in the story, including Xiao Hong, Li Guangmi, and Yang Zhilin. The conversation also looks at enterprise-service AI and the prospects for agentic models in the new AI era.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Enterprise Agents Need Private Data and Numerical Reality, Not Demos
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Wu Minghui's 19-year B2B history reframes enterprise AI as a survival game through painful pivots, not a clean SaaS wave. The notes suggest that agentic models become investable when they can operate on private enterprise data and real-world numerical games rather than generic chat interfaces.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Enterprise AI may be won by old B2B survivors with messy customer data access, not by the most elegant new horizontal agent startups.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor enterprise AI companies with proprietary private-data integration, domain workflow depth, and measurable numerical outcomes, especially where agents are layered onto existing customer operations.
alphaLens.whyNow: The interview is explicitly pre-IPO and tied to Minglue's overseas listing filing notice on August 29, 2025. That creates a public-market test for whether long-cycle B2B AI assets can be revalued in the agent era.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Enterprise software is shifting from dashboards and recommendation systems toward agentic services that act on private operational data.

episodes.titleZh: 115. 对OpenAI姚顺雨3小时访谈：6年Agent研究、人与系统、吞噬的边界、既单极又多元的世界
episodes.titleEn: 115. A Three-Hour Interview with OpenAI's Shunyu Yao: Six Years of Agent Research, Humans and Systems, the Boundary of Absorption, and a World Both Unipolar and Plural
episodes.summaryEn: OpenAI researcher Shunyu Yao reflects on six years of agent research after his April 2025 essay The Second Half argued that AI's main thread had entered its second half. The conversation starts from the individual, then explores agents as systems, tasks, rewards, interfaces, super apps, code as affordance, and why the boundary of intelligence may be defined by interaction forms rather than a single model.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
今天的嘉宾，我们很开心邀请了OpenAI研究员姚顺雨。
2025年4月，姚顺雨发布了一篇有名的博文《The Second Half》，宣告AI主线程的游戏已进入下半场。这之后，我们与他进行了一场播客对谈。
姚顺雨毕业于清华和普林斯顿大学，开始智能体的研究非常早。在博士期间他意识到语言可能是人类发明的最接近本质的工具，于是转向语言智能体研究，至今已6年。他有许多有代表性的工作。
我们的谈话从个体出发，共同探索由人、组织、AI、人与机器的交互，所抵达的这个世界智能的边界以及人类与机器的全景。
前不久，我刚刚创立了一家新的内容工作室「语言即世界工作室」，顺雨很意外地从另一个角度帮我回答了，我们工作室创立的初心。
为什么我们相信语言是这个世界的本质奥秘？他的表达是：“语言是人为了实现泛化而发明出来的工具，这一点比其他东西更本质。”
（本次访谈发生在2025年5月，访谈为个人观点，与所供职公司无关。）
02:58 第一部分：人
•  感觉我前28年的人生，非常的乖
•  我一直有这个非共识：我想要去做Agent
•  第一年最大收获是，要用GPT，不要用BERT；第二个learning是任务或环境非常重要
•  我的研究有两个核心：一是怎么去做一些有价值、和现实世界更相关的任务和环境；二是怎么去做一些简单、但又通用的方法17:50 第二部分：系统
•  Agent是一个非常古老的概念，任何能进行自我决策、与环境交互，并试图优化奖励的系统，都可以被称为Agent
•  Agent演变的三波兴衰：大家可能更多注意到方法线，容易忽视任务线，但这两条线是相辅相成的
•  Agent发展最关键的两个方向：一个是让它拥有自己的reward（奖励），能自己探索；另一个是Multi-Agent（多智能体），让它们之间能形成组织结构
•  Code有点像人的手，它是AI最重要的affordance（环境给予行动者的可能性）
•  任务的设定
•  泛化的工具
•  奖励的机制48:38 第三部分：吞噬的边界
•  创业公司最大机会是：能设计不同的interface（交互方式）
•  可能模型的能力会产生beyond ChatGPT（超越 ChatGPT）的交互方式，变成Super App
•  拥有一个Super App对于公司是双刃剑，当你有像ChatGPT这样的Super App，很自然你的研究就会围绕这个Super App
•  Assistant、Her，或者像人一样的交互方式，显然是最重要的交互方式之一；不显然的是，我能不能基于不像人的交互方式？
•  这世界是个相互抄的关系，而
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Today's guest is OpenAI researcher Shunyu Yao. In April 2025, Yao published the well-known blog post The Second Half, declaring that the main game of AI had entered its second half, and this podcast conversation followed. A graduate of Tsinghua and Princeton, he began agent research early. During his PhD, he realized that language may be the tool humans invented that comes closest to the essence, and he turned toward language-agent research, a path he has now followed for six years.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Agents Are an Interface Boundary Problem, Not a Model Leaderboard
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Shunyu Yao's framing of the second half of AI shifts attention from model intelligence to systems, tasks, rewards, and interfaces. The hidden alpha is that the boundary of AI value may be set by where agents can absorb workflows through the right interaction form.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The agent market may not consolidate around one universal super app; it may become both unipolar at the model layer and plural at the interface layer.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Back agent platforms that own task environments, reward signals, and interaction surfaces, especially where code acts as an affordance for reliable execution.
alphaLens.whyNow: The episode follows Yao's April 2025 essay arguing AI entered its second half. Official market context around agent launches in 2025 reinforces the note's emphasis on moving from research to action systems.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: AI is moving from answer generation toward interactive systems that absorb tasks through tools, rewards, and user interfaces.
evidenceSignals: The source says the conversation explores agents as systems, tasks, rewards, interfaces, and super apps.
Yao has followed language-agent research for six years.
The notes mention code as affordance.
The boundary of intelligence is framed as interaction forms rather than a single model.
The episode is structured around the human, systems, boundary of absorption, and the human whole.
watchlist: Agent products that expose durable task-completion metrics rather than chat engagement.
Developer platforms where code, browser, and app actions converge.

Interface experiments that expand what users delegate.
Signs of super-app consolidation versus specialized agent families.
risks: Agent reliability may lag user expectations for high-stakes workflows.
A single platform could absorb standalone agent startups.
The notes do not provide technical details from the full interview.

thesisTable.implication: Infrastructure and interface design may capture value beyond base models.

thesisTable.implication: Coding agents may generalize into broader digital-work agents.

episodes.titleZh: 114. 与殷一、欧迪聊聊萨洛蒙：中国意外的增长阀门、小众越野跑与少女故事
episodes.titleEn: 114. Talking Salomon with Yin Yi and Oudi: China's Unexpected Growth Valve, Niche Trail Running, and Girl Stories
episodes.summaryEn: Salomon China general manager Yin Yi and Xiaohongshu fashion and apparel business lead Oudi discuss how a 70-year-old French outdoor brand found unexpected growth in China after 2021. The episode explains how Salomon expanded from a male, specialist skiing and trail-running base toward women and new outdoor consumers through Xiaohongshu, offline stores, product taste, and user-centered brand storytelling.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
今天的嘉宾是萨洛蒙中国区总经理殷一和小红书商业服饰潮流行业负责人欧迪。我们一起来聊聊，这两年比较火的一个户外品牌，萨洛蒙。
萨洛蒙和始祖鸟都属于亚玛芬集团，2019年亚玛芬被安踏收购；2021年以后，萨洛蒙这个70多岁的法国品牌，意外在中国开启了增长之路。
这个最早从滑雪品类起步，逐步扩展到越野跑鞋的小众品牌，在中国的传统消费群体以男性以及专业滑雪、越野跑爱好者为主；但近几年，他们通过在小红书的一系列品牌行为，成功吸引女性消费群体和新户外人群，扩圈增长，而这又进一步反向刺激了男性消费者以及核心运动人群的增长。
希望这些新鲜的品牌知识，也能给你带来新的启发:)

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Today's guests are Yin Yi, general manager of Salomon China, and Oudi, who leads Xiaohongshu's commercial apparel and fashion sector. Together we discuss Salomon, an outdoor brand that has become popular over the past two years. Salomon and Arc'teryx both belong to Amer Sports, which Anta acquired in 2019. After 2021, this more than 70-year-old French brand unexpectedly began a growth path in China. It started in skiing and later expanded into niche trail-running shoes, then attracted women and new outdoor consumers through Xiaohongshu.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Salomon Shows How Niche Outdoor Brands Can Scale Through Taste Networks
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes show that Salomon's China growth was not a simple outdoor boom story; it moved from male specialist skiing and trail running into women, color-sensitive consumers, offline stores, and Xiaohongshu storytelling. The hidden signal is that niche performance brands can scale by preserving product taste while changing the social entry point.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Mass growth does not require diluting a niche brand if the expansion route runs through community taste and gendered discovery rather than discount distribution.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor premium consumer brands that combine specialist credibility, China-local social commerce, offline experience, and product-led storytelling, especially under owners that can compound multi-brand playbooks.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source says Salomon's growth path in China began after 2021 and expanded through Xiaohongshu. Current Amer Sports context shows Salomon has become a major growth engine, making the episode's mechanism useful for consumer alpha.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Consumer-brand scaling is shifting from broad advertising to niche-to-mass community migration through taste platforms.

Overexpansion could weaken scarcity and taste signals.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Kimi K2 Turns Coding Into the First Real Agentic Distribution Wedge
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes say the interview was recorded shortly after Kimi K2's release and focused on development, technical judgment, and two years of large-model entrepreneurship. The hidden insight is that coding is not just a benchmark category for Kimi; it is the path by which a model grows hands in the digital world.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Open-source agentic models may compete first where execution is observable and cheap to verify, making coding a better wedge than general chat or consumer assistants.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Track open agentic models, coding-agent tooling, inference providers, and developer platforms that can convert Kimi-style model capability into workflow adoption.
alphaLens.whyNow: The episode is tied to the Kimi K2 launch window. External context checked during drafting shows Kimi K2 remained a market reference point for open agentic models, but the output thesis is grounded in the notes' focus on K2 and agentic LLMs.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Large models are shifting from passive text systems toward agentic execution systems that manipulate digital environments through code.

episodes.titleZh: 113. 和杨植麟时隔1年的对话：K2、Agentic LLM、缸中之脑和“站在无限的开端”
episodes.titleEn: 113. A Conversation with Yang Zhilin After One Year: K2, Agentic LLMs, Brains in Vats, and Standing at the Beginning of Infinity
episodes.summaryEn: Moonshot AI founder and CEO Yang Zhilin discusses Kimi K2 after its July release, describing it as an open-source MoE coding and agentic large language model that gives the model hands in the digital world. The episode covers test-time scaling, agent reinforcement learning, token efficiency, Muon, open source, multimodality, long context, commercial models, and Yang's state of mind through public controversy and startup turbulence.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
今天的嘉宾是月之暗面创始人兼CEO杨植麟，距离他上一次来我们的节目（《商业访谈录》59集）已经过去1年半。
就在刚刚过去的7月，Kimi K2模型发布，引发了比较广泛的关注。K2是一个基于MoE架构的开源编程和Agentic大语言模型。形象来说，模型借助编程能力走出封闭的“缸中之脑”，长出了“手”，开始操控外部数字世界。
今天这集节目我和杨植麟聊了聊K2的研发和他当下的技术认知、技术判断。
以及，在过去一年的舆论风暴与创业起伏中，作为创始人，他的心情与思考。
01:49 一座无限的山
这有点像我最近在看的一本书：The Beginning of Infinity（无穷的开始）
也许有一天会发现，这座雪山没有尽头，我希望它一直没有尽头
但它还是一个“缸中之脑”：想象一个鱼缸，你把一个脑子放在里面，跟外界没有联系
不管是基于长思考的强化学习，还是Agent的强化学习，都指向同一个东西：test-time scaling（测试时扩展）
还有一个很有意思的趋势是，现在有更多模型公司去做“一方的Agent产品”
L1到L5不一定是串行关系，Claude就bet这一点：它在Reasoning上做得不是特别多，但在Agent上做得非常好
只有当模型参与到开发过程，才能解锁真正的Innovator（L4）阶段
24:58 K2是乔戈里峰
K2的重点有几个：一，我们希望它是一个非常好的基础模型
我们希望能最大化使用每一份数据，就是所谓token efficiency——喂一样多的数据，“脑子”长得更多
我们会对数据做很多Rephrase（改写）操作
我们很关注Muon优化器，它对token efficiency提升很大
二，我们希望K2有好的Agentic能力，对于Agentic模型来讲，最大挑战是模型的泛化
它可能是一个从“缸中之脑”变成可以跟世界交互，因为所谓Agent最重要的特征是，可以多轮地使用工具
人是所谓的universal constructor（万能构造器）
有一种潜在思路，需要用更AI native（原生人工智能）的方式去训练AI
Muon你去训的时候，它会炸
54:08 既简单又复杂的系统
为什么Kimi从闭源转向开源？
模型训练完成，产品也基本完成了，做交互上的改进当然有价值，但那是锦上添花的一步
多模态不损伤“脑子”已经很好了
你可能学出来的多模态是个“傻的多模态”，我们希望它是个“聪明的多模态”
Scaling Law遇到数据墙了，这是客观事实
数据飞轮很依赖外部环境的feedback（反馈），我们不希望feedback有很多噪声，但现在没有把这个问题解
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Today's guest is Moonshot AI founder and CEO Yang Zhilin, returning a year and a half after his previous appearance on episode 59. In July, Kimi K2 was released and attracted wide attention. K2 is an open-source coding and agentic large language model based on an MoE architecture. Metaphorically, through coding ability, the model steps out of the closed brain in a vat, grows hands, and begins manipulating the external digital world. The conversation covers K2's development, Yang's current technical understanding, and his reflections as a founder.

outline.textEn: An infinite mountain

outline.textZh: K2是乔戈里峰
outline.textEn: K2 is the mountain K2

outline.textZh: 既简单又复杂的系统
outline.textEn: A system that is both simple and complex

outline.textZh: 在自己的故事里面
outline.textEn: Inside one's own story

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Moonshot's K2 Bet Is Systems Engineering for Digital Labor
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes describe Kimi K2 as an open-source MoE coding and agentic LLM that gives the model hands in the digital world. That makes the real thesis less about one model release and more about combining MoE, test-time scaling, reinforcement learning, token efficiency, and open source into a digital-labor platform.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Open-source does not necessarily weaken monetization if the strategic asset is the agentic system around the model rather than the weights alone.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Invest around the K2 ecosystem: agentic inference, coding workflow integrations, evaluation harnesses, RL data loops, and enterprise deployment layers that can ride open-model adoption.
alphaLens.whyNow: K2's July release attracted wide attention in the notes, and the episode returns to Yang after major public controversy and startup turbulence. That creates a moment to evaluate whether Moonshot can turn attention into durable system capability.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Model competition is moving from chatbot intelligence toward open agentic systems capable of manipulating software environments.

episodes.titleZh: 112. 和广密聊大模型季报：分化与收敛、全家桶与垂直整合、L4体验与挖矿窗口
episodes.titleEn: 112. Talking with Guangmi About the Global Large-Model Quarterly: Differentiation and Convergence, Full Suites and Vertical Integration, L4 Experiences, and Mining Windows
episodes.summaryEn: The seventh Global Large-Model Quarterly episode argues that model companies are differentiating by domain while consumer products are converging around head products. Guangmi and the host discuss Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, Thinking Machines, Grok, Meta, full-suite products like ChatGPT, vertically integrated ecosystems like Google, product barriers, mining-like product windows, and L4 experiences such as Deep Research and Claude Code.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
在大家的强烈催更下，新一集的《全球大模型季报》终于来了。
这一集有两个关键词。
第一个关键词是分化。硅谷各个模型公司在这个季度，开始分化到各个领域，除了Google Gemini和OpenAI还在做通用的模型；Anthropic分化到Coding、Agentic的模型能力；Mira的Thinking Machines分化到多模态和下一代交互。
第二个关键词是产品。《大模型季报》过去一直把视角放在模型的智能探索上，而广密开始浓墨重彩地聊产品，这还是第一次。
这里是《全球大模型季报》的第7集，如果大家喜欢我们的系列，希望大家多多给我们一些鼓励和支持。你们的夸奖对我们来说，非常的重要。
2025，期待我们和AI共同进步！
03:54 模型在分化
通用各项能力的模型 - Gemini/OpenAI
All in Coding+Agentic 能力 - Anthropic
多模态原生 - Thinking Machines Lab
Grok 今天还在摸索自己生态位置
Meta 原创 0-1 的基因还是很弱
最领先的这几家很像 F1 竞赛
21:37 横向全家桶，纵向垂直整合
C端是一个非常明显的头部收敛趋势，ChatGPT可能在C端会收敛掉很多产品
作为投资人或 AI 创业者，一面兴奋是技术每个月都在进步，另一面有点绝望
横向全家桶的例子是ChatGPT，已经包含了Chat+搜索+Coding+Agent+WorkSpace
纵向垂直整合的例子是 Gemini，从 TPU 芯片，到 Gemini 模型，到上面 Agent 应用，再到 Google 文档/Chrome浏览器/安卓操作系统/YouTube视频，可以做超级集成
33:35 智能和产品都重要
过去 3 年一直是对智能上限的探索极度上头，但在过去两个月开始重视产品了
ChatGPT 身上有很多非技术性壁垒，而 Coding 或模型公司只是技术壁垒
OpenAI 是平衡最好的一家，一边探索智能上限，一边又把智能红利转化成产品流量和品牌心智
38:52 做 AI 产品很像挖矿，保鲜窗口很关键
挖矿：第一个做出来让用户惊叹的体验很重要，哪怕 token 消耗很大，只要你是第一个做出来让用户惊叹的 Magic moments，就等于你起码得到了 5 亿美金的营销费用，比如 Perplexity/Cursor/Manus
但这个窗口期又特别有意思，窗口是逐渐在缩短的：从 2 年、1 年、3 个月
产品公司能赢过模型公司做的产品吗？
44:21 L4 级别的体验
最优秀的俩 Agent 都有了 L4 体验：ChatGPT 的
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: After many listeners urged an update, a new episode of the Global Large-Model Quarterly finally arrives. This episode has two keywords. The first is differentiation: during the quarter, Silicon Valley model companies began diverging into different domains, with Google Gemini and OpenAI still building general models, Anthropic moving toward coding and agentic capabilities, and Mira's Thinking Machines moving toward native multimodality and next-generation interaction. The second keyword is product. The series has long focused on exploration at the frontier of model intelligence, but Guangmi now discusses product in depth.

outline.textEn: Models are differentiating

outline.textEn: Horizontal full suites and vertical integration

outline.textZh: 智能和产品都重要
outline.textEn: Both intelligence and product matter

outline.textZh: 做 AI 产品很像挖矿，保鲜窗口很关键
outline.textEn: Building AI products is like mining; the freshness window is crucial

outline.textZh: L4 级别的体验
outline.textEn: L4-level experiences

outline.textZh: 对Google看法的转变
outline.textEn: A changed view of Google

outline.textZh: 其他话题
outline.textEn: Other topics

alphaLens.alphaTitle: AI Products Are Becoming Mines With Short Freshness Windows
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The episode's strongest alpha is the mining metaphor: AI product opportunities have freshness windows that can close quickly as model companies copy, bundle, or vertically integrate. Differentiation at the model layer and convergence at the product layer mean startups must find windows before full suites absorb them.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The best AI application investments may look unattractive by traditional defensibility standards at first, because speed into a temporary but expanding workflow window can matter more than a static moat.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Back AI products with fast distribution, proprietary workflow data, and clear upgrade paths before horizontal full suites or vertically integrated ecosystems commoditize the surface.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source says model companies began differentiating by domain while consumer products converged around head products. It also highlights L4-level experiences such as Deep Research and Claude Code, suggesting users are starting to recognize step-function product quality.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: AI competition is moving from who has the smartest model to who packages intelligence into full-suite or vertical workflows fast enough to capture user habits.
evidenceSignals: The episode's two keywords are differentiation and product.
Google Gemini and OpenAI are framed as general-model builders, while Anthropic moves toward coding and agentic capabilities.
The notes contrast horizontal full suites with vertical integration.
AI product building is compared to mining with a crucial freshness window.
L4 experiences include Deep Research and Claude Code.
watchlist: AI application retention after incumbent full-suite products add similar features.
Vertical products with proprietary workflow data or distribution.
Google's vertical integration across model, product, and ecosystem.
Anthropic's coding and agentic product depth.
Emergence of new L4 experiences that users describe as categorically different.
risks: Freshness-window opportunities can decay before companies build defensibility.
Model providers may bundle away standalone product margins.
The notes are broad and do not quantify market share or retention.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes compare AI product building to mining and emphasize freshness.
thesisTable.implication: Speed and timing deserve more weight in AI app underwriting.

thesisTable.evidence: The source says Silicon Valley model companies diverged into domains.
thesisTable.implication: Application strategy must account for likely upstream bundling.

thesisTable.evidence: Deep Research and Claude Code are cited as L4-level experiences.
thesisTable.implication: Users may only pay for products that feel discontinuously better.

episodes.titleZh: 111. 李一帆口述激光雷达11年创业史：你仔细想行业的机会来自哪？是国家、民族的机会
episodes.titleEn: 111. Li Yifan's Oral History of 11 Years in LiDAR Entrepreneurship: Think Carefully About Where the Industry Opportunity Comes From - It Is a National and Civilizational Opportunity
episodes.summaryEn: Hesai co-founder and CEO Li Yifan tells an 11-year oral history of building a LiDAR company within China's new-energy-vehicle supply chain. The episode covers stock volatility, 99.5 percent cost reduction, family and early growth, unusual equal equity among three founders, financing tactics, major orders, pricing, entering the automotive mainstream, and old versus new capital.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
过去10年，中国新能源汽车产业从无到有，经历蓬勃发展。大家最熟悉的可能是理想、小鹏、蔚来这些整车品牌，但另一面这场变革背后的产业链企业也在变化。
《商业访谈录》的108集对余凯和本集对禾赛联合创始人和CEO李一帆的3小时访谈，关注的都是汽车产业链上的隐形选手。
这集也是李一帆对他们做激光雷达11年硬核科技创业的一部口述史。
随着中国科技创新从互联网的模式创新，走向硬核科技的前沿创新，中国也许还会出现更多的技术型创业者。禾赛的故事也许能提供一个参考样本。
（本次访谈录制于2025年4月）

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Over the past decade, China's new-energy vehicle industry has gone from nothing to rapid growth. People are most familiar with vehicle brands such as Li Auto, XPeng, and NIO, but the supply-chain companies behind this transformation have also been changing. Episode 108 with Yu Kai and this three-hour interview with Hesai co-founder and CEO Li Yifan both focus on hidden players in the automotive supply chain. This episode is also Li's oral history of 11 years of deep-tech LiDAR entrepreneurship, offering a reference sample as Chinese technology innovation moves from internet-model innovation to frontier hard tech.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: LiDAR's Hidden Alpha Is Industrial Cost Collapse, Not Autonomy Hype
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Li Yifan's oral history centers on an extreme cost reduction and the long path into automotive mainstream adoption. The hidden insight is that LiDAR value emerges when it becomes an industrial supply-chain component with pricing discipline, not when it is marketed as a futuristic sensor.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: LiDAR may be more investable as a scaled manufacturing and automotive-supply-chain story than as a pure autonomous-driving option.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor LiDAR companies with proven cost curves, major OEM orders, production scale, and robotics optionality, while discounting firms still dependent on speculative autonomy timelines.
alphaLens.whyNow: The notes place Hesai inside China's new-energy-vehicle supply-chain transformation and mention stock volatility, cost reduction, major orders, and pricing. Current company context shows LiDAR demand spanning ADAS and robotics, which makes the cost-curve thesis timely.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: LiDAR is moving from exotic autonomy hardware toward mass-produced ADAS and robotics infrastructure.
evidenceSignals: The episode cites a 99.5 percent cost reduction in LiDAR.
A first 20 million yuan order is part of the oral history.
The notes discuss pricing considerations and entering the automotive mainstream.
The source frames Hesai as a hidden player in China's NEV supply chain.
Stock-price volatility is a named topic, showing public-market skepticism.
watchlist: LiDAR unit production scale and gross-margin trends.
ADAS versus robotics revenue mix.
OEM design wins converting into shipped volume.
Pricing pressure from camera-only autonomy narratives.
Capital-market treatment of profitable hard-tech suppliers.
risks: Automakers may reduce LiDAR content if vision-only systems improve.
Customer concentration can create revenue volatility.
Hardware margins may compress as LiDAR standardizes.
The notes do not include full financial statements.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes cite a 99.5 percent LiDAR cost reduction.
thesisTable.implication: LiDAR can become mainstream supply-chain content rather than niche autonomy hardware.

thesisTable.evidence: The oral history includes a first 20 million yuan order.
thesisTable.implication: Commercial inflection matters more than prototype performance.

thesisTable.evidence: The interview begins with stock-price roller-coaster discussion.
thesisTable.implication: Volatility can create opportunity if unit economics keep improving.

episodes.titleZh: 110. 逐段讲解Kimi K2报告并对照ChatGPT Agent、Qwen3-Coder等：“系统工程的力量”
episodes.titleEn: 110. A Section-by-Section Reading of the Kimi K2 Report, Compared with ChatGPT Agent, Qwen3-Coder, and More: The Power of Systems Engineering
episodes.summaryEn: Language-agent researcher Zheng Boyuan joins to read recent technical reports on Kimi K2, ChatGPT Agent, Qwen3-Coder, and a Manus blog post, all connected by agent technology. The episode defines and classifies agents, compares technical routes, examines training through synthetic data, reinforcement learning, and safety, then considers context engineering, future paradigms, and families of agents.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
我们又来读论文啦！！！
今天我们要读的论文是最近几个星期内最值得品读的几篇技术报告，分别是：Kimi K2、ChatGPT Agent、Qwen3-Coder的技术报告，以及Manus的一篇技术博文。他们的相关性是，这几篇内容都和Agent有关系。
今天的嘉宾是俄亥俄州立大学（The Ohio State University）的在读博士郑博元，他的研究方向是Language Agent，他会带我们一起读上述技术报告和博文。
这是《商业访谈录》的“技术之美”系列，期待和你一起读论文，领略科技平权，感受技术之美——做你的赛博组会：）

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: We are reading papers again. Today's papers are among the most worth studying from recent weeks: the technical reports for Kimi K2, ChatGPT Agent, and Qwen3-Coder, plus a technical blog post from Manus. Their common thread is that all relate to agents. The guest is Zheng Boyuan, a PhD student at The Ohio State University whose research focuses on language agents. He guides the show through these reports and posts as part of The Beauty of Technology series, meant to be a cyber reading group for experiencing technological beauty.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Agent Winners Are Built by Systems Engineering, Not Prompt Magic
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The episode reads Kimi K2, ChatGPT Agent, Qwen3-Coder, and Manus as one agent-technology cluster. The hidden insight is that agent quality depends on synthetic data, reinforcement learning, safety, and context engineering stitched into a system, not a single clever model prompt.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Agent startups that obsess over context management and training pipelines may beat flashier products that depend on base-model progress alone.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Back agent infrastructure for synthetic trajectory generation, RL evaluation, safety scaffolding, context engineering, and multi-agent workflow orchestration.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source says the papers were among the most worth studying from recent weeks and all relate to agents. Official 2025 agent launches and Manus's context-engineering blog make the episode's systems-engineering thesis immediately actionable.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Language agents are becoming engineered execution systems with training, memory, safety, and context layers.
evidenceSignals: The episode compares Kimi K2, ChatGPT Agent, Qwen3-Coder, and Manus.
It covers synthetic data, reinforcement learning, and safety as key parts of agent training.
Manus's context-engineering lessons are included as a technical blog post.
The source considers future paradigms and families of agents.
The guest defines and classifies agents before comparing technical routes.
watchlist: Agent benchmarks that evaluate full workflow completion, recovery, and safety.
Context-engineering patterns becoming reusable infrastructure.
Synthetic task data pipelines for coding and browser agents.
Open technical reports revealing RL and safety recipes.
Enterprise adoption of families of specialized agents.
risks: Agent evaluation can be gamed by narrow benchmarks.
Safety failures can block enterprise adoption.
Infrastructure may commoditize if absorbed by model providers.
The source is a reading outline rather than original benchmark data.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes identify synthetic data, RL, and safety as key training parts.
thesisTable.implication: Tooling around training loops can be more durable than agent UI alone.

thesisTable.evidence: The episode includes Manus's context-engineering blog.
thesisTable.implication: Memory and context management may become core agent middleware.

thesisTable.evidence: The source compares Kimi K2, ChatGPT Agent, Qwen3-Coder, and Manus.
thesisTable.implication: Portfolio exposure should span models, tools, and orchestration layers.

episodes.titleZh: Lovart创始人陈冕复盘应用创业这两年（视频）：这一刻就是好爽啊！！哈哈哈哈哈
episodes.titleEn: Lovart Founder Chen Mian Looks Back on Two Years of App Startup Life (Video): This Moment Just Feels So Good!
episodes.summaryEn: This episode interviews Chen Mian, founder of Lovart, an AI design agent that gained global attention after Manus. The notes frame him less as a conventional product CEO than as a combative founder finally enjoying a breakthrough after subsidy wars, product takedowns, failed fundraising, and having only 4,000 yuan left in the company account.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
今天的嘉宾又是一位AI应用创业者，Lovart创始人陈冕。
他的产品成为2025年既Manus之后，另一个在全球斩获一定知名度的Agent。不同的是，Manus是通用Agent，Lovart是垂直Agent，面向设计师使用。
与其说他是做产品的CEO，他的精神状态更贴近一名“战斗型CEO”。
这次访谈发生Lovart火了之后，你能感受到陈冕在过去2年遭遇了补贴战争、产品下架、账上只剩4000块现金的绝境、怎么都融不到资等一系列愤懑之后——此时此刻，充斥着的要溢出的快乐。
这是2025年对一位Agent创业者精神状态的截取。浪潮的大幕才刚刚拉开。
2025，期待我们和AI共同进步！
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Today's guest is another AI application founder, Chen Mian of Lovart. His product became, after Manus, another agent to gain a measure of global recognition in 2025. The difference is that Manus is a general agent, while Lovart is a vertical agent for designers. Rather than a product CEO, his state of mind feels closer to that of a combat CEO. This interview took place after Lovart became popular, and you can sense the overflowing happiness that followed two years of subsidy wars, product takedowns, the desperate moment when only 4,000 yuan remained in cash, and repeated fundraising failures.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Lovart Tests Whether Vertical Agents Can Beat General Agents at Creation
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes contrast Lovart with Manus: Manus is a general agent, while Lovart is a vertical design agent. The hidden insight is that vertical agents may build stronger workflow identity because they own the user's creative context, taste, and output standards.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The next AI app breakout may come from a narrow professional workflow that feels emotionally complete, not from the broadest general-purpose assistant.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor vertical agent startups where output quality, workflow memory, and domain-specific taste create retention before general agents can match the product surface.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source says Lovart gained global attention in 2025 after Manus and that the interview followed its breakout. That marks a window to test whether vertical agents can sustain usage after initial novelty.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: AI applications are moving from generic assistants toward vertical agents that participate directly in professional creative workflows.
evidenceSignals: Lovart is described as a vertical agent for designers.
The notes say it gained global attention after Manus.
The founder is framed as a combat CEO rather than a conventional product CEO.
The company survived subsidy wars, product takedowns, failed fundraising, and only 4,000 yuan in cash.
The interview occurs after Lovart became popular.
watchlist: Retention and paid conversion among designers using Lovart.
Output quality across brand, layout, image, and iteration workflows.
Whether general agents copy Lovart's core design flows.
Fundraising and hiring after the 2025 breakout.
User-generated examples showing professional rather than toy usage.
risks: Breakout attention can fade if usage is novelty-driven.
Creative professionals may resist agents that lack taste or controllability.
General agent platforms may absorb vertical design features.
The notes include no product metrics.

thesisTable.evidence: The source contrasts Lovart as a designer agent with Manus as general.
thesisTable.implication: Domain-specific workflow ownership can create defensibility.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes recount cash lows, failed fundraising, and product setbacks.
thesisTable.implication: Survival through constraints may produce sharper product judgment.

thesisTable.claim: Global attention is only the first test.
thesisTable.evidence: Lovart is said to have gained global recognition in 2025.
thesisTable.implication: Retention and professional usage must validate the breakout.

episodes.titleZh: 109. 机器人遭遇数据荒？与谢晨聊：仿真与合成数据、Meta天价收购和Alexandr Wang
episodes.titleEn: 109. Are Robots Facing a Data Shortage? Xie Chen on Simulation, Synthetic Data, Meta's Pricey Acquisition, and Alexandr Wang
episodes.summaryEn: Xie Chen, founder and CEO of Lightwheel Intelligence and a former simulation lead at Nvidia, Cruise, and NIO, discusses simulation and synthetic data for embodied AI. The episode focuses on the data bottleneck in robotics, practical Sim2Real and Real2Sim workflows, the limits of synthetic data, and where model-layer opportunities may emerge globally.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
今天又是一集机器人专场。嘉宾是光轮智能创始人兼CEO谢晨，他曾在英伟达、Cruise及蔚来汽车担任自动驾驶仿真负责人。我们的话题非常具体，即:仿真与合成数据。
今天的具身智能尚且没有找到scaling law的有效配方，其中，数据是一个关键卡点。我们106集的嘉宾银河通用创始人王鹤就提到，真实数据在他们的训练数据比重仅仅1%，合成数据挑起大梁。
今天这集节目，我与谢晨聊了聊仿真与合成数据的实操细节。

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: This is another robotics-focused episode. The guest is Xie Chen, founder and CEO of Lightwheel Intelligence, who previously led autonomous-driving simulation at Nvidia, Cruise, and NIO. The topic is very specific: simulation and synthetic data. Today's embodied intelligence has not yet found an effective recipe for a scaling law, and data is a key choke point. In episode 106, Wang He of Galbot said real data accounts for only 1 percent of their training data, while synthetic data carries the load. In this episode, Xie Chen and I discuss the practical details of simulation and synthetic data.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Robotics Simulation Becomes the Embodied-AI Data Foundry
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Xie Chen's background across Nvidia, Cruise, and NIO makes the episode a bridge between autonomous-driving simulation and embodied-AI simulation. The hidden alpha is that robotics may need industrial-grade data foundries before scaling laws appear.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The most valuable embodied-AI companies may not look like robot makers; they may look like simulation, synthetic-data, and Real2Sim infrastructure providers.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Back simulation platforms, synthetic-data pipelines, Real2Sim tooling, and evaluation systems that solve the robotics data shortage across multiple embodied-AI companies.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source says embodied intelligence has not found an effective scaling-law recipe and that data is a key choke point. Capital interest in robotics makes infrastructure more attractive because many hardware teams face the same data bottleneck.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Robotics is moving from hand-engineered demos toward data-intensive training loops that require synthetic environments and real-world calibration.
evidenceSignals: The episode asks whether robots are facing a data shortage.
Xie Chen previously led simulation at Nvidia, Cruise, and NIO.
The notes cover Sim2Real, Real2Sim, synthetic data, and their bottlenecks.
Driving is described as a vision game, while embodied AI requires physical interaction.
The source cites Wang He's statement that real data is only 1 percent of Galbot's training data.
watchlist: Robotics teams disclosing synthetic-to-real data ratios.
Real2Sim workflows that reduce manual scene and physics setup.
Benchmarks for simulation success in physical-interaction tasks.
Strategic acquisitions of data or simulation providers by model labs.
Revenue traction for embodied-AI infrastructure versus robot hardware.
risks: The Sim2Real gap may remain too large for dexterous physical tasks.
Synthetic data can create hidden failure modes if poorly validated.
Infrastructure vendors may be squeezed by large labs building in-house.
The notes do not quantify customer traction for Lightwheel Intelligence.

thesisTable.claim: Data shortage is the robotics choke point.
thesisTable.evidence: The source says embodied intelligence lacks a scaling-law recipe and data is key.
thesisTable.implication: Data infrastructure can be a horizontal robotics bet.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes contrast driving as vision with embodied AI as physical interaction.
thesisTable.implication: New simulation tools must model contact, manipulation, and physics.

thesisTable.evidence: The episode covers physical Real2Sim workflow and evaluation.
thesisTable.implication: Capture and validation pipelines may become strategic assets.

episodes.titleZh: 余凯口述30年史（视频）：世界不止刀光剑影，是一部人来人往的江湖故事
episodes.titleEn: Yu Kai's 30-Year Oral History (Video): The World Is More Than Combat, It Is a Story of People Coming and Going
episodes.summaryEn: Horizon Robotics founder and CEO Yu Kai recounts a career that moved through German and American academia, China's internet sector, venture capital, capital markets, and the auto industry. The notes present his entrepreneurial worldview as a reminder that startups are not only about technology, business, and competition, but also about social intelligence and relationships.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
今天的嘉宾是地平线创始人兼CEO余凯博士。
在过去49年人生中，他一路闯关过德美学术圈、中国互联网圈、创投圈、资本圈、汽车圈。在每个圈子和江湖，都从籍籍无名的无名小卒开始升级打怪。到最后，在每个圈子，他混得都不错。
一位与他打过交道的前企业高层评价，余凯是科学家里非常具有社会智慧的一位。
余凯毕业于南京大学和慕尼黑大学，毕业后，先后就职西门子、NEC研究院，于2012年回国加入百度，又于2015年离职创立地平线。
很巧的是，2025年正好是地平线创立10年。今年上半年，我与余凯博士聊了两次，这集节目是他的一部口述史。
随着大语言模型浪潮爆发，更多人工智能科学家从高校系统涌入创业轨道。余凯的创业观，也许能给大家一些启示——创业不仅是技术和商业，也不仅仅有刀光剑影，更是一部人来人往的江湖故事。
就像电视剧《少帅》张作霖的台词：“江湖不是打打杀杀，江湖是人情世故。”
2025年，我们和AI共同进步！
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Today's guest is Dr. Yu Kai, founder and CEO of Horizon Robotics. Over the past 49 years, he has passed through German and American academia, China's internet world, venture capital, capital markets, and the auto industry. In each circle, he began as an unknown outsider and worked his way up; in the end, he did reasonably well in all of them. A former senior executive who dealt with him once said Yu Kai is one of the scientists with unusually strong social wisdom. He graduated from Nanjing University and the University of Munich, then worked at Siemens and NEC Research before joining Baidu in 2012 and founding Horizon in 2015.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Horizon Robotics Shows Social Intelligence as a Deep-Tech Moat
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The video notes position Yu Kai as a scientist with unusually strong social wisdom, not just technical competence. The hidden alpha is that automotive AI suppliers need relationship intelligence because their products sit inside complex OEM, capital-market, and supply-chain networks.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: In hard-tech AI, founder social skill can be as important as model or chip capability because design wins require trust across conservative industrial buyers.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor deep-tech founders who combine credible technical history with OEM relationship-building and capital-market navigation, especially in automotive AI where cycles are long.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source places Yu Kai across academia, internet, venture capital, capital markets, and the auto industry. That range is relevant as AI scientists increasingly leave labs to build companies that must sell into industrial systems.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: AI entrepreneurship is shifting from lab-to-product translation toward ecosystem orchestration across capital, customers, and supply chains.
evidenceSignals: Yu Kai moved through German and American academia, China's internet sector, venture capital, capital markets, and the auto industry.
He founded Horizon Robotics in 2015 after joining Baidu in 2012.
The notes cite a former executive calling him a scientist with strong social wisdom.
The source says startups are not only about technology, business, and competition, but also relationships.
The episode frames the world as people coming and going rather than only combat.
watchlist: Horizon Robotics OEM design wins and repeat orders.
Founder-led partnership announcements with automakers and Tier 1 suppliers.
Public-market disclosures that show relationship depth converting to revenue.
Technical hiring from academia into automotive AI startups.
Governance and succession planning around founder relationship capital.
risks: Relationship-driven moats can be hard to measure and may not scale beyond the founder.
Automotive supply cycles can delay revenue recognition.
The video notes lack product-level performance evidence.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes emphasize Yu Kai's unusually strong social wisdom.
thesisTable.implication: Founder relationship capital can improve hard-tech commercialization odds.

thesisTable.evidence: The source spans academia, internet, VC, capital markets, and autos.
thesisTable.implication: Cross-domain credibility helps navigate industrial AI adoption.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes say startups are also about relationships and human dealings.
thesisTable.implication: Investment diligence should include customer and partner trust, not only technical specs.

The notes emphasize his mix of technical background and social intelligence, including stories about Hinton, LeCun, Andrew Ng, Richard Sutton, Nvidia, Tesla, and the hard second round of financing for Horizon.

03:06 初入学术江湖
一开始在学术圈籍籍无名，算命先生说我24岁前“籍籍无名，劳而无功”
发表过100篇论文，我很陶醉，夜深人静都会翻我以前的paper自我欣赏
结识Geoffrey Hinton、Yann LeCun、吴恩达的故事
我这边的对面坐了一个人特别的沉默，没人搭理他，一个人在那吃闷饭——这个人叫Richard Sutton，前段时间拿了图灵奖

我为了小概率能赢，抢先第一个出价，1200万美金

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Today we speak with Dr. Yu Kai, founder and CEO of Horizon Robotics. In the past 49 years he has moved through German and American academia, China's internet world, venture capital, capital markets, and the auto industry, starting from obscurity in each new circle. This episode is an oral history recorded in the tenth year of Horizon's founding. As more AI scientists move from universities into startups during the large language model wave, Yu Kai's view of entrepreneurship may offer a useful lesson: startups are not only technology and business, nor only clashes and combat, but also a world of relationships and human dealings.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Automotive AI Startups Need Capital-World Fluency as Much as Algorithms
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The full Yu Kai oral history emphasizes entry into academia, the internet, startups, capital, and automobiles. The hidden insight is that automotive AI is a sequence of institutional translations, and founders who can translate between Hinton-era research, Baidu-scale AI, Nvidia/Tesla narratives, and OEM procurement can build durable companies.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Scientific founders entering AI startups may fail less from weak algorithms than from weak capital and relationship navigation.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Back automotive AI suppliers whose founders can bridge research credibility, financing, OEM design cycles, and public-market storytelling.
alphaLens.whyNow: The notes explicitly say Yu Kai's view is useful as more AI scientists move from universities into startups during the large-language-model wave. The episode offers a historical template for founder transition risk.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: AI founder risk is expanding from technical invention risk to ecosystem translation risk.
evidenceSignals: The episode is recorded in Horizon's tenth year.
Yu Kai's path includes academia, Baidu, Horizon Robotics, venture financing, and the auto industry.
The notes reference Hinton, LeCun, Andrew Ng, Richard Sutton, Nvidia, and Tesla.
A hard second round of financing is part of the story.
The outline includes moving into the capital world and automotive world as separate chapters.
watchlist: Horizon Robotics' revenue concentration and design-win conversion.
Scientist-founded AI startups that recruit capital-market operators early.
Automotive AI chip and software attach rates in Chinese OEMs.
Founder narratives that connect research lineage to customer ROI.
Follow-on financing conditions for deep-tech AI companies.
risks: Strong founder narratives can obscure product-market weaknesses.
Automotive AI suppliers face long qualification cycles and price pressure.
The source is oral-history notes and lacks financial detail.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes divide Yu Kai's journey across academic, internet, startup, capital, and auto worlds.
thesisTable.implication: Diligence should test cross-institution credibility.

thesisTable.evidence: The source highlights Horizon's hard second financing round.
thesisTable.implication: Survival through funding gaps can create stronger operators.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes reference leading AI figures and Nvidia/Tesla stories.
thesisTable.implication: Research lineage can support recruiting, partnerships, and narrative power.

episodes.titleZh: 和王鹤聊，具身智能的学术边缘史和资本轰炸后的人为乱象（含字幕）
episodes.titleEn: A Conversation with Wang He on the Marginal Academic History of Embodied AI and the Human-Made Chaos After the Capital Boom (With Captions)
episodes.summaryEn: Peking University assistant professor and Galbot founder Wang He traces embodied AI from its computer-vision origins to its recent rise as a capital-market favorite. The conversation covers the relationship between vision, language, and intelligence, the difficulty of data collection, synthetic data, and why Wang describes embodied AI as "productivity as product."
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
今天继续《商业访谈录》的机器人专场，嘉宾是北京大学助理教授、银河通用创始人兼CTO王鹤。
王鹤毕业于清华和斯坦福大学。他给我们从“具身智能”的学术缘起开始聊起，这是一个学术流派从一个学科中萌芽到边缘再到主流渗透的全过程。
而随着ChatGPT诞生，“具身智能”这个小众概念，在过去2年成了新的资本宠儿——但一时间，也带来了新的乱象。
我们探讨了一些具身智能产业界关键问题：
1/具身智能起源于计算机视觉的学术流派，视觉、语言、智能的关系是什么？为什么VLM（视觉语言模型）的表现显著弱于LLM（大语言模型）？
2/具身智能的最大困境之一是数据采集，合成数据是正解吗？具体应该怎么做？
3/如果大模型提倡的是“智能即产品”，那么具身智能呢？王鹤的回答是“生产力即产品”。
去年底，英伟达创始人黄仁勋来华访问。答谢宴上，王鹤不仅和黄仁勋同桌，而且就在做黄仁勋旁边（挨着坐）。在节目最后，我们也聊了聊这个有趣的插曲——他提到，那晚黄仁勋吃了不少水煮肉片。
2025，我们和AI共同进步！

05:58 语言不是智能的本质，而是“一次跃变”
“具身智能”和“机器人”是不同学术流派
“具身智能”起源于“计算机视觉”的研究流派
视觉有智能吗？纯视觉智能的可解释性差，是端到端的
语言不是智能的本质，不能说没有语言就没有智能
智能的本质是什么？“一种视情况对环境做出反应的能力”
语言是人类能产生这么高智能的“一次跃变”
视觉的本质是一种非常强的sensor（传感器）
25:08 具身智能的学术边缘史
具身智能最早兴起的task（任务）是，导航
加入视觉模态，强调Perception–Action Loop（感知-动作循环），成为具身智能研究流派能立起来的核心叙事
标志性事件：“具身智能是计算机视觉未来的三颗北极星之一”（李飞飞）
我和Skild创始人Deepak Pathak在Facebook人工智能实验室FAIR打过交道
41:15 我的学术之路
2016年，博士第一个项目：从人类视频里学多步的人与物体交互过程的生成（动画领域）
在Stanford博士第一年，在不喜欢的方向非常挣扎，后来换组、换方向
Stanford是高度自由的市场：你可以随时踢你老板，你老板可以随时踢你
第一篇论文憋了很久，很绝望
完全从视频中学习，学习世界模型，还没成为当下能推进具身智能的技术
我的第二个项目：位姿估计和合成数据相关
2020年李开复曾在湾区丽思卡尔顿组织brunch，观点分歧
回国坚定以家庭机器人为目标推进research，根本没有allies（盟军）
01:25:08 具身
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: This robotics special features Wang He, assistant professor at Peking University and founder and CTO of Galbot. A graduate of Tsinghua and Stanford, he starts from the academic origins of embodied intelligence and describes how a research school sprouted inside one discipline, moved to the margins, and then penetrated the mainstream. After ChatGPT appeared, the once niche concept of embodied intelligence became a new favorite of capital over the past two years, but it also produced new chaos. The episode asks how vision, language, and intelligence relate, why VLMs lag LLMs, and whether synthetic data is the answer to data collection.

outline.textEn: Embodied intelligence means productivity as product

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Embodied AI's Product Is Productivity, Not a Humanoid Demo
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Wang He's framing of embodied intelligence as productivity as product cuts through the capital boom. The hidden alpha is that useful robotics companies should be evaluated by productivity loops and data generation, not by whether they perform impressive one-off demos.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The embodied-AI bubble may misprice companies with boring productivity use cases while overvaluing teams optimized for humanoid spectacle.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Prioritize embodied-AI companies with clear productivity endpoints, synthetic-data strategy, and software-hardware co-evolution rather than capital-market storytelling.
alphaLens.whyNow: The notes say embodied intelligence became a capital-market favorite after ChatGPT and produced new chaos. That is precisely when disciplined productivity-based underwriting can separate durable companies from hype.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Robotics is moving from academic vision-language research into commercial productivity systems under heavy capital attention.
evidenceSignals: Wang He traces embodied AI from computer-vision origins to capital-market popularity.
The episode asks why VLMs lag LLMs and whether synthetic data solves data collection.
Software and hardware are described as rising in a spiral.
The notes include pitfalls to avoid after capital bombardment.
Embodied intelligence is defined as productivity as product.
watchlist: Robotics startups reporting productivity metrics per deployment site.
Synthetic-data dependency and validation quality.
Hardware-software iteration cycles that improve task economics.
Capital inflows into embodied AI relative to real customer deployments.
Commercial traction in non-glamorous productivity use cases.
risks: Capital hype can inflate valuations before technical readiness.
Synthetic data may fail to transfer to physical productivity.
The notes do not include Galbot customer metrics or revenue.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes explicitly state embodied intelligence means productivity as product.
thesisTable.implication: Revenue diligence should start with labor or throughput improvement.

thesisTable.evidence: The source traces embodied AI from computer vision to the mainstream.
thesisTable.implication: Technical roots can reveal which claims are mature versus fashionable.

thesisTable.evidence: The episode references human-made chaos after capital bombardment.
thesisTable.implication: Valuation discipline is crucial in robotics cycles.

episodes.titleZh: 107. 和梦秋的catch-up：创投挺无聊，也聊聊旅行读书和女性主义
episodes.titleEn: 107. A Catch-Up with Meng Qiu: Venture Capital Is Pretty Boring, Plus Travel, Books, and Feminism
episodes.summaryEn: ClearVue Partners founding partner and former Baidu technical VP Meng Qiu returns for a relaxed annual check-in. The episode moves from the state of venture capital, AI applications, agents, embodied intelligence, wearables, and large model companies to her recent travel, reading, film industry work, and views on feminism.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
这集没有宏大叙事，相当随性。嘉宾是清流资本创始合伙人、百度前技术VP梦秋。
关注《商业访谈录》比较久的朋友可能知道，梦秋基本每年都会来返场一次，和我们一起catch-up当下的创投水温以及她自己的生活。在狼性文化蓬勃的中国投资人圈里，梦秋一直是很佛系也很道家的存在。
这一集更是松弛，她直言工作挺无聊，所以在聊了正经话题以后，我们也聊了聊读书、旅行、观影和女生的碎碎念。
（本次节目录制在4月底）

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: This episode has no grand narrative and is quite casual. The guest is Meng Qiu, founding partner of ClearVue Partners and former Baidu technical VP. Longtime listeners may know that Meng Qiu usually returns once a year to catch up on the temperature of venture capital and her own life. In China's investment world, where a wolf culture can be intense, she has long been a relaxed, almost Daoist presence. This episode is even looser: she says work has been rather boring, so after discussing serious topics, we also talk about books, travel, films, and women's everyday thoughts.
