episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: This is a year-end special of Business Interviews. I invited Li Guangmi, founder of Shixiang, to give an oral account of the global large-model year: when top business leaders and leading scientists, some of the smartest minds in humanity, spent the year racing with tens of millions of dollars in capital, what did the global large-model industry actually produce in 2023? Can this big experiment in the name of large models bring the world into a new Moore era, much like semiconductors?

outline.textZh: 过去一年，大模型的核心secret藏在这三家公司：OpenAI、Anthropic、Google
outline.textEn: The core secret of the past year in large models lies with three companies: OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google

outline.textZh: 回顾全球大模型这一年，我脑海里闪现的关键产品与瞬间
outline.textEn: Looking back on the global large-model year: the key products and moments that come to mind

outline.textZh: 从OpenAI分裂出去的Anthropic，它的前世今生（OpenAI正以1000亿美元融资，Anthropic正以200亿-250亿美元融资）
outline.textEn: Anthropic, split off from OpenAI: its past and present, with OpenAI raising at $100 billion and Anthropic at $20-25 billion

outline.textZh: 硅谷大模型的融资是巨头在定价和主导了，硅谷VC几乎全体错过
outline.textEn: Silicon Valley large-model financing is being priced and led by giants, while almost all VCs missed it

outline.textZh: 卷了这一年，全球大模型到底卷出了什么？
outline.textEn: After a year of intense competition, what did global large models actually achieve?

outline.textZh: 如果复刻GPT-4是进入决赛圈，目前的3名入围选手和3名高潜黑马
outline.textEn: If reproducing GPT-4 is the final round, the current three finalists and three high-potential dark horses

outline.textZh: 提升模型capability的北极星指标与关键变量？
outline.textEn: The north-star metric and key variables for improving model capability

outline.textZh: 这次是极少数科学家推动的“登月时刻”，天才科学家的聚集效应与分布图
outline.textEn: A moonshot moment driven by a tiny number of scientists: the clustering and distribution of scientific talent

outline.textZh: 模型竞赛很残酷，我们对于终局的推演、猜想
outline.textEn: The model race is brutal: projections and guesses about the endgame

outline.textZh: 中国模型公司现状：技术辨识度/资源集中度/领军人物
outline.textEn: The state of Chinese model companies: technical distinctiveness, resource concentration, and leading figures

outline.textZh: 人类烧尽千亿美金做一场AI实验，赌的是什么？AI能推动未来15年全球GDP翻倍？
outline.textEn: What is humanity betting on by burning through hundreds of billions of dollars on an AI experiment?

outline.textZh: 硅谷巨头和它们分裂的大模型阵营：微软和它支持的OpenAI/Google、Amazon和它们支持的Anthropic/Google自成一派/Tesla和它支持的X.AI/Apple——明年Apple支持谁值得关注
outline.textEn: Silicon Valley giants and their model camps: Microsoft and OpenAI, Amazon and Anthropic, Google, Tesla and X.AI, and Apple's next move

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Large Models Are Recreating Moore's Law as a Capital-Science Race
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The year-end episode frames 2023 large models as a hundred-billion-dollar scientific gamble concentrated among OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google. The hidden insight is that model progress may be judged by a new Moore-like cadence: capability improves while training and inference costs fall on predictable cycles.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: VCs may remain structurally disadvantaged in foundation models because giants price and lead the race while talent clusters around a few frontier labs.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Invest around the frontier-model race through infrastructure, cost-reduction tooling, application layers, and picks-and-shovels exposed to capability gains without requiring ownership of the base model winner.
alphaLens.whyNow: The episode reviews 2023 after a year of intense competition, financing, talent concentration, and questions about whether large models can create a new Moore era.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: AI progress becomes a capital-intensive scientific production system dominated by a few labs, with downstream opportunities tied to falling cost curves.
evidenceSignals: The source says the core secret lies with OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google.
OpenAI and Anthropic valuations are cited in the notes.
Silicon Valley financing is described as giant-led, with almost all VCs missing it.
The episode identifies a north-star metric and key variables for improving model capability.
It asks whether capability can improve while training and inference costs fall like a new Moore's Law.
watchlist: Capability and cost cadence across frontier model releases.
Talent concentration among top labs and universities.
Infrastructure suppliers benefiting regardless of model winner.
Chinese model companies' technical distinctiveness and resource concentration.
Application categories unlocked by lower inference costs.
risks: A Moore-like cadence may fail if scaling laws weaken.
Capital concentration can leave little room for independent startup returns.
Application value may be captured by model providers.
The episode is a 2023 review and not a current benchmark update.

thesisTable.evidence: The source centers OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google.
thesisTable.implication: Direct base-model investing is difficult outside giant-led rounds.

thesisTable.claim: Cost curves matter as much as capability.
thesisTable.evidence: The episode asks whether model costs fall on a Moore-like cycle.
thesisTable.implication: Applications become investable as inference prices decline.

thesisTable.claim: Talent clustering is a strategic variable.
thesisTable.evidence: The notes emphasize clustering and distribution of scientific talent.
thesisTable.implication: Recruiting access can define model-company survivability.

episodes.titleZh: 53. 从萨特曼的宏大叙事，聊到赴中东掘金的中国人肖像
episodes.titleEn: 53. From Grand Narratives to Portraits of Chinese People Seeking Opportunity in the Middle East
episodes.summaryEn: Next Capital founder Li Li recounts a 16-day Middle East research trip through three countries and four cities, based on more than 100 interviews. The episode sketches different groups of Chinese people pursuing opportunities there, from giants and infrastructure companies to mobile internet firms, supply-chain exporters, students, young workers, and more mixed actors.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
就在前不久，嘉程资本李黎女士带队赴中东考察。16天走过中东三国四城，访谈百余人。这集播客我们通过声音，记录那些他们在途中遇到的赴中东掘金的华人面孔，试图把这个鱼龙混杂世界中的每类人真实的生存状态刻画出来。
片尾曲是 It's Beginning to Look a Lot like Christmas，祝大家圣诞快乐🎅🎅🎁：）
【嘉宾小记】
李黎是嘉程资本创始合伙人。她曾在科技财经媒体10年，曾担任《IT经理世界》副总编，独家专访过亚马逊创始人贝索斯，著有商业财经畅销书《轻公司》。2011年她投身科技早期投资领域，在某知名机构担任管理合伙人。她也是知名创投服务平台《小饭桌》创始人。2017年，李黎创立科技早期投资机构嘉程资本（Next Capital）。她是乐信(NAS:LX)、老虎国际(NAS:TIGR)、PingCAP、元气森林、核桃编程等公司的早期投资人。

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Not long ago, Next Capital's Li Li led a team to the Middle East. Over 16 days they visited three countries and four cities and interviewed more than a hundred people. In this podcast we use sound to record the faces of Chinese people they met on the road, people going to the Middle East to seek opportunity, and we try to portray the real survival conditions of each type of person in this mixed and complicated world of infrastructure, internet, and supply-chain exports.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: The Middle East Is a Relationship Market for China's Globalization 2.0
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Li Li's 16-day trip portrays the Middle East as a layered market where Chinese giants, infrastructure players, mobile internet firms, supply-chain exporters, startups, students, and young workers all search for opportunity. The hidden insight is that success depends less on a single grand narrative and more on etiquette, local segmentation, and not ignoring small accidental orders.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The Middle East opportunity for Chinese companies is not just sovereign wealth or megaprojects; it includes granular startup and supply-chain niches discovered through ground-level relationships.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Back Chinese companies that bring proven supply-chain or technology advantages into Middle Eastern markets while investing in local etiquette, partners, and market-specific operating knowledge.
alphaLens.whyNow: The notes describe more than 100 interviews across three countries and four cities, capturing Chinese companies' current search for overseas growth.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Chinese globalization shifts from broad manufacturing export to multi-layer overseas presence spanning infrastructure, internet, logistics, autonomous driving, and local services.
evidenceSignals: The research trip covered 16 days, three countries, four cities, and more than 100 interviews.
The notes identify waves of state firms, mobile internet firms, and supply-chain exporters.
Interviewed groups include giant-company employees, Chinese-founded startups, students, young workers, and mixed actors.
The episode notes that Middle Eastern regions look different as markets.
A specific tip says not to casually miss a small accidental Middle East order.
watchlist: Chinese startups converting small Middle East orders into repeatable channels.
Local partner quality and business etiquette execution.
Sector fit in logistics, autonomous driving, e-commerce, and infrastructure.
Competitive dynamics between Chinese and Western companies.
Market differences across Middle Eastern countries and cities.
risks: Grand narratives can obscure fragmented local demand.
Business etiquette and relationship requirements can slow scaling.
Geopolitical competition can affect Chinese and Western company positioning.
The episode is qualitative fieldwork notes rather than financial data.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes emphasize 100-plus interviews across multiple cities.
thesisTable.implication: Market entry should begin with local segmentation.

thesisTable.claim: Small orders can reveal real demand.
thesisTable.evidence: The source warns not to miss accidental Middle East orders.
thesisTable.implication: Early weak signals may become export channels.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes cite infrastructure, mobile internet, supply chains, logistics, WeRide, and Pony.ai.
thesisTable.implication: Investors should underwrite by vertical and country, not region-wide hype.

episodes.titleZh: 52.【职场人性】论嫉妒：人类最禁忌的情感，在职场里暗涌
episodes.titleEn: 52. Workplace Humanity: On Envy, the Most Taboo Human Emotion, Running Beneath the Workplace
episodes.summaryEn: This conversation with organizational expert Wang Yajun examines envy as a taboo but pervasive force in business life. It covers workplace envy between ranks, how organizations and status symbols amplify it, when envy can be constructive, and how companies might evaluate or reduce its destructive effects.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
查理·芒格曾说：“世界不受贪婪驱使，它是由嫉妒驱动的。”他察觉到，在人们的争论中，嫉妒似乎是一个“普遍的禁忌”。如果将某个人的某个立场指控为“受嫉妒驱使”，会被认为是对持有者的极度侮辱。
然而，嫉妒总是无孔不入。
这集我和王亚军老师大声谈论嫉妒。尤其是谈论嫉妒在商业世界中暗流时，它的形状、样貌与表现。
近日东方甄选CEO与董宇辉事件，也可以用本期话题来解释。 =_=|||
【嘉宾小记】
王亚军现为境门管理创始人，曾经是龙湖集团首席战略官、宝龙地产执行副总裁、麦肯锡大中华区战略与组织转型业务创始团队成员。出版有《企业即算法》一书，他对如何建设大规模敏捷组织有长期实践和研究。
•  01:41 当听说我们要聊嫉妒…第一反应是啥？?
•  04:06 嫉妒与爱、痛苦、恐惧一样与我们亲密接触，我们却对它避之不及

播客中提到的论文《Workplace Envy》：https://www.annualreviews.org/doi/10.1146/an
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Charlie Munger once said that the world is not driven by greed but by envy. He noticed that envy seemed to be a universal taboo in people's arguments; to accuse someone's position of being driven by envy is considered a serious insult. Yet envy seeps into everything. In this episode, Wang Yajun and I speak out loud about envy, especially the shapes, appearances, and expressions it takes as an undercurrent in the business world, including recent workplace and CEO controversies.

outline.textZh: 当听说我们要聊嫉妒…第一反应是啥？?
outline.textEn: What was your first reaction when you heard we would talk about envy?

outline.textZh: 嫉妒与爱、痛苦、恐惧一样与我们亲密接触，我们却对它避之不及
outline.textEn: Envy is as close to us as love, pain, and fear, yet we avoid it

outline.textZh: 什么令我们嫉妒不已？谁在设计着嫉妒？
outline.textEn: What makes us envious, and who designs envy?

outline.textZh: 如果你试图消除引起嫉妒的一种偏差，人类会立马发明另一种偏差
outline.textEn: If you try to remove one bias that causes envy, humans immediately invent another

outline.textZh: 职场嫉妒（workplace envy）的N种形式：“暗流无处不在”
outline.textEn: The many forms of workplace envy: undercurrents are everywhere

outline.textZh: 上级对下级的嫉妒 vs 下级对上级的嫉妒
outline.textEn: A superior's envy of a subordinate versus a subordinate's envy of a superior

outline.textZh: 嫉妒也有积极意义（消极的嫉妒 vs 积极的嫉妒）
outline.textEn: Envy can also have positive meaning: negative envy versus positive envy

outline.textZh: 公司里，高层/中层/基层都在嫉妒着什么？
outline.textEn: What do executives, middle managers, and frontline staff envy inside companies?

outline.textZh: 我们一边嫉妒，一边创造着“被我们嫉妒的东西”
outline.textEn: While envying, we also create the things that others envy

outline.textZh: 女人嫉妒一切女人，男人只嫉妒一部分男人？
outline.textEn: Do women envy all women while men envy only some men?

outline.textZh: 奢侈品、豪车被发明出来，专门承载人类的妒忌之心
outline.textEn: Luxury goods and sports cars were invented to carry human envy

outline.textZh: 当年终绩效考核引发不公感和嫉妒感，该如何自处？
outline.textEn: How to deal with unfairness and envy triggered by year-end performance reviews

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Status Design as Operating Leverage
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes frame envy as a taboo force that quietly shapes rank, appraisal, and executive behavior. The hidden alpha is that status architecture is an operating variable, not HR background noise.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Companies that explicitly surface and redirect envy may outperform cultures that pretend motivation is purely rational.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor organizational tools, advisory practices, and management systems that make comparison, recognition, and review mechanics legible before they become destructive.
alphaLens.whyNow: The episode links envy to workplace controversies, CEO behavior, rank comparisons, and performance reviews, making it relevant whenever companies redesign evaluation and status systems.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Management advantage shifts from slogans about motivation to deliberate design of comparison environments.
evidenceSignals: The source cites Munger's idea that envy, not only greed, drives behavior.
The discussion treats envy as close to love, pain, and fear but avoided.
The notes distinguish superior-to-subordinate and subordinate-to-superior envy.
The episode separates destructive envy from potentially constructive envy.
The source asks how companies might evaluate or reduce envy effects.
watchlist: Performance-review systems that reduce opaque ranking
Employee sentiment tools focused on status and fairness
Promotion, bonus, and recognition redesigns
Executive controversies where status resentment is visible
Training that converts comparison into aspiration
risks: The source is notes-derived, so examples are not fully developed.
Envy is difficult to measure externally.
Over-managing status can suppress healthy ambition.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes say it appears across ranks and status symbols.
thesisTable.implication: Culture diligence should examine comparison mechanics.

thesisTable.claim: Not all envy is value destructive.
thesisTable.evidence: The episode distinguishes negative and positive envy.
thesisTable.implication: Better systems redirect envy into performance.

episodes.titleZh: 51. 地缘政治与大国汽车：中国跃居第一出口国？别浪费大危机
episodes.titleEn: 51. Geopolitics and Major-Power Automobiles: China Becomes the Top Exporter? Do Not Waste a Great Crisis
episodes.summaryEn: This episode compares China's recent auto-export surge with Japan's earlier expansion into global markets. It argues that auto globalization always carries political and economic risk, but major crises and geopolitical shifts can also create openings for a national auto industry to rise.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
这一集我们聊聊地缘政治与大国汽车工业。
据中汽协最新数据，2023年1月至11月，中国出口汽车441.2万辆，同比增长58.4%，其中新能源汽车出口109.1万辆，同比增长83.5%。今年中国首次超过日本，跻身第一大汽车出口国。中国要想跃居全球汽车工业的下一个区域重心，出海成为一个绕不开的课题。
随着专辑《汽车史话》展开，大家会看到全球汽车工业的区域重心发生过几次重大迁移。前几集我们深入聊了美式汽车的辉煌与衰落，接下来我们会看到，汽车区域重心从欧美转向日本。在当时，日本车企面临着异常坎坷的大出海之路。他们在美国遭遇了第一次重大失败，经历漫长的调整期，最终借助了一场大型全球危机夺得成功。
这集节目我们会对比中国和日本汽车出海之路。你会看到，对于汽车出海，政治风险与经济风险会一路相伴，险象环生；但与此同时，一个国家汽车工业的崛起与地缘政治，甚至历史中的大冲突、大危机、大风波是密不可分的。
【嘉宾小记】
张君毅是奥纬咨询亚太区私募资本与工业品业务主管合伙人。现任飞乐音响、如祺出行、纵目科技独立董事，在复旦等高校担任客座教授。加入奥纬之前，曾与李斌联合创立蔚来资本并担任管理合伙人，在三年内投出10余家科技领域独角兽和上市公司。此外，他曾担任平安集团执行委员会成员兼智慧企业首席运营官、罗兰贝格大中华区合伙人等职。
Part 1: 疫情中爆发的中国汽车出海
•  02:10 中国新能源汽车和燃油车汽车出海的起点
•  05:24 2012-2020年这八年汽车出口总量（含新能源和燃油车）才100万台/年，为什么？
•  1）2005年有中国车企在全德汽车俱乐部（ADAC）碰撞测试中得0分，造成第一次出海失败
•  2）出口地区单一，伊朗是中国很多车企的第一大市场，包括比亚迪/长安/奇瑞都依托当地企业进行属地生产和销售；后因伊朗遭美国制裁，导致出口的一次重大下滑
•  3）欧美法规和技术要求高且繁杂
•  4）海陆运通道运力有限制，仓位紧张
•  07:45 疫情给了中国车企一次意外的增长机会
•  1）2021年中国汽车出口破200万台，超韩国，仅次于日、德，是第三大汽车出口国
•  2）2022年破300万台，超德国，是第二大汽车出口国
•  3）今年数据喜人，到10月份已经423万台，首次超过日本成第一大汽车出口国
•  08:58 细说中国汽车出海爆发的构成要素：
•  1） 产品具有了全球竞争力，包括质量和性价比
•  2） 疫情让国际供给发生中断，中国汽车成为补充
•  3） 海外运力等前期困境慢慢被解决
•  4） 中国电动车产品补充了当地市场空白，如泰国

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: In this episode we discuss geopolitics and the auto industry of major powers. According to the latest data from the China Association of Automobile Manufacturers, from January to November 2023 China exported 4.412 million vehicles, up 58.4 percent year on year, including 1.091 million new-energy vehicles, up 83.5 percent. This year China surpassed Japan for the first time and became the world's largest auto exporter. For China to become the next regional center of the global auto industry, going overseas is unavoidable.

outline.textZh: 中国新能源汽车和燃油车汽车出海的起点
outline.textEn: The starting point for overseas expansion by China's new-energy and fuel-vehicle makers

outline.textZh: 2012-2020年这八年汽车出口总量（含新能源和燃油车）才100万台/年，为什么？
outline.textEn: Why were China's total auto exports from 2012 to 2020 only about one million vehicles a year?

outline.textZh: 疫情给了中国车企一次意外的增长机会
outline.textEn: The pandemic gave Chinese automakers an unexpected growth opportunity

outline.textZh: 细说中国汽车出海爆发的构成要素
outline.textEn: The factors behind the explosion of China's auto exports

outline.textZh: 造车新势力的出海进程：蔚来、小鹏、爱驰很早筹备出海，哪吒在中东、泰国很努力，对出海审慎的理想也开始迈出脚步
outline.textEn: The overseas progress of new EV makers: Nio, Xpeng, Aiways, Neta, and Li Auto

outline.textZh: 新能源汽车是规模化竞争的市场，所以“攘外必先安内”
outline.textEn: New-energy vehicles are a scale-competition market, so you must secure the home front before going abroad

outline.textZh: 出海的挑战与危机将始终存在
outline.textEn: Challenges and crises in overseas expansion will always exist

outline.textZh: 中国汽车出海案例与2012年以前更早期的探索道路Part 2:日车汽车出海史：第一次失败、在意外的大危机中爆发
outline.textEn: Chinese auto export cases and pre-2012 explorations; Part 2: Japanese auto exports, first failure and later breakout in an unexpected crisis

outline.textZh: 全球汽车工业的区域重心从美国转向日本
outline.textEn: The regional center of the global auto industry shifted from the United States to Japan

outline.textZh: 以丰田为例，上世纪50年代第一次出海在美国遭遇了巨大挫折
outline.textEn: Toyota's first postwar overseas attempt in the United States in the 1950s suffered a major setback

outline.textZh: 上世纪70年代石油危机，为小排量的日本车带来机会
outline.textEn: The 1970s oil crisis created an opportunity for small-displacement Japanese cars

outline.textZh: 丰田做出了哪些有效准备？
outline.textEn: What effective preparations did Toyota make?

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Crisis-Led Auto Globalization
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes argue that auto globalization is inseparable from political and economic risk, yet crises can open the route for a national industry to rise. China's export surge is therefore not only a volume story but a test of whether domestic scale can become global industrial influence.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Geopolitics may accelerate regionalization opportunities for Chinese automakers instead of simply capping their expansion.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Prioritize automakers and suppliers that combine home-market scale, export execution, localization capability, and resilience to political shocks.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source states that China exported 4.412 million vehicles from January to November 2023 and surpassed Japan as the largest auto exporter, forcing overseas strategy from optional to unavoidable.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Autos shift from domestic manufacturing competition to geopolitical industrial platform competition.
evidenceSignals: The source reports 58.4 percent year-on-year growth in China's January-November 2023 vehicle exports.
New-energy vehicle exports were reported at 1.091 million, up 83.5 percent.
The episode says China surpassed Japan as the world's largest auto exporter.
The notes compare China with Japan's earlier global expansion and setbacks.
The source says NEVs are a scale-competition market requiring a secure home front.
watchlist: Overseas assembly and localization announcements
Export mix by region and powertrain
Trade barriers, tariffs, and safety investigations
Domestic share stability before overseas pushes
Dealer and service-network quality abroad
risks: Export volume can outrun brand trust and service capacity.
Geopolitical resistance can block high-margin markets.
Domestic price wars can weaken overseas investment capacity.

thesisTable.claim: Scale at home funds global reach.
thesisTable.evidence: The source says NEVs are scale competition and the home front must be secured.
thesisTable.implication: Export winners need domestic durability first.

thesisTable.evidence: The episode links pandemic disruption and Japan's historical breakout to export openings.
thesisTable.implication: Macro shocks can reorder auto leadership.

episodes.titleZh: 50. 95后、00后创业者怎么样了？他们的生猛、高马斯克值与现实
episodes.titleEn: 50. How Are Post-95 and Post-00 Founders Doing? Their Ferocity, High Musk Quotient, and Reality
episodes.summaryEn: Post-95 founder Song Yachen discusses his path through SenseTime, MiniMax, and his AIGC 3D startup VAST. The episode sketches a younger founder cohort that is more Musk-inspired, intense, and playful, but also confronted by financing, management, and media-recognition realities.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
95后、00后创业者是主流媒体之外一群面目模糊的人。
事实上，在今年如火如荼的AI创业潮中，这波新生代创业者已占据相当比例。只是媒体仍将镜头对准那些成功过的创业者（如王慧文、王小川），或学术大咖创立的公司（如智谱AI）。
今天这名嘉宾是一位95后创业者宋亚宸（Simon）。他出生于1997年，还没毕业就在商汤CEO办公室工作，后以001号身份作为MiniMax早期联创参与创建这家公司，去年底他开始独立创业，方向是AIGC 3D。这是宋亚宸接受的第一个访谈。
这集，你可以视作是对以宋亚宸为代表的95后、00后创业群体的速写。
他有一个令我印象很深的描述：这波年轻创业者的“马斯克值”拉满。他们的创业偶像悄然从乔布斯过度为马斯克。
【嘉宾小记】
宋亚宸于1997年出生，2019年毕业于约翰霍普金斯大学。他于2018年开始在商汤CEO办公室实习并工作，2021年6月作为早期联合创始人参与创立AI新兴独角兽Minimax，2022年底他创立AIGC 3D公司VAST，获国内知名基金投资。2023年，宋亚宸在国际图形学顶级会议Siggraph上，与英伟达⻩仁勋、Sony和Unity高管同台发表主题演讲，是首位在该会议上进行主题演讲的中国创业者。

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Post-95 and post-00 founders are a blurred group outside mainstream media. In fact, during this year's vigorous AI startup wave, this new generation of founders has already taken up a sizable share. The media still points its lens at founders who have already succeeded, such as Wang Huiwen and Wang Xiaochuan, or at companies founded by academic stars such as Zhipu AI. Today's guest is a post-95 founder, Song Yachen, born in 1997, who worked in SenseTime's CEO office and later joined MiniMax as an early co-founder.

outline.textEn: When ferocity collides with reality

alphaLens.alphaTitle: The Young Founder Tempo Gap
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes describe post-95 and post-00 founders as under-covered despite taking a sizable role in the AI startup wave. The hidden insight is that their edge may be cultural tempo and product aggression rather than academic prestige.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The most important young AI founders may not look like the media's preferred academic-star or already-successful founder archetype.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Back young AI teams with prior frontier-company exposure, fast creative taste, and explicit support around financing, management, and media pressure.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source says the AI startup wave had already pulled in a sizable younger-founder cohort while mainstream coverage still focused elsewhere.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Founder selection shifts from credential signaling toward iteration speed, ambition density, and resilience when ferocity meets reality.
evidenceSignals: Song Yachen worked in SenseTime's CEO office and joined MiniMax early.
The episode compares AI 1.0 companies such as SenseTime with AI 2.0 companies such as MiniMax.

AIGC 3D product adoption
Seed rounds led by operators rather than academics

Talent retention after first product launch

Media under-coverage makes diligence harder.
Financing cycles can punish ambitious but immature teams.

episodes.titleZh: 49. 闲话蔚小理往事：大佬、出牌、生死时局【汽车史话番外】
episodes.titleEn: 49. Casual Talk on Nio, Xpeng, and Li Auto: Big Names, Moves, and Life-or-Death Moments
episodes.summaryEn: Writer Wang Pan recounts the first eight years of China's new-energy carmaking wave, from the 2014-2015 entry rush to fundraising, production struggles, and the 2019 survival crisis. The episode focuses on the shifting status of Nio, Xpeng, and Li Auto and the current dilemmas of their CEOs.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
王潘是中国最早一批追踪新能源造车的作者之一，见证了这个行业在过去短短八年间，从萌芽、艰难踱步、濒临生死一线，到意外崛起。更引人注目的是，蔚小理三家公司在这段时间里地位的斗转星移。这集，我们一起闲话蔚小理往事。

•  45:10 第六章：新造车穿越生死线
•  48:20 第七章：三位CEO今日困境

更多信息欢迎关注公众号：张小珺
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Wang Pan was among the earliest Chinese writers to track new-energy carmaking. He witnessed the industry over just eight years as it moved from germination, to difficult shuffling, to the brink of life and death, and then to an unexpected rise. More striking is how the positions of Nio, Xpeng, and Li Auto shifted during this period. In this episode, we chat about the past of these three new carmakers, from entering the field to finding people, raising money, launching cars, and crossing survival lines.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Survival Lines Beat Launch Hype
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes recast China's EV startup wave as a sequence of survival thresholds: people, money, production, and the 2019 crisis. The hidden alpha is that the investable signal is crossing these thresholds, not the glamour of entering carmaking.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Among EV startups, the best predictor is not first product buzz but evidence that the company can survive capital and manufacturing shocks.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Underwrite Nio, Xpeng, Li Auto, and adjacent suppliers through balance-sheet resilience, production execution, and CEO adaptability rather than brand narrative alone.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source highlights the current dilemmas of the three CEOs after the first eight years of the Chinese NEV wave.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: The EV startup market moves from fundraising optimism to survivor economics.
evidenceSignals: The source covers the 2014-2015 entry rush.
It tracks the twists of finding people and raising money.
It discusses old giants investing in new giants.
It contrasts successes and failures of getting cars onto the road.
It identifies 2019 as a life-or-death crisis.
watchlist: Cash runway and refinancing access
Production ramp reliability
Strategic investor behavior
CEO pivots in product and channel strategy
Evidence of crossing the next survival line
risks: Price competition can erase survivor advantages.
Capital markets can close before operations mature.
The episode is retrospective and may underweight new entrants.

thesisTable.claim: Survival is the first EV moat.
thesisTable.evidence: The notes center on the 2019 life-or-death crisis and crossing survival lines.
thesisTable.implication: Balance-sheet quality deserves premium weight.

thesisTable.evidence: The source says Nio, Xpeng, and Li Auto shifted status over eight years.
thesisTable.implication: Static brand rankings are low-quality signals.

episodes.titleZh: 48. OpenAI的权力游戏：脸谱、硅谷精英政治与讽刺意味
episodes.titleEn: 48. OpenAI's Game of Power: Character Types, Silicon Valley Elite Politics, and Irony
episodes.summaryEn: Recorded during the OpenAI board crisis, this episode reviews four days of upheaval, key characters, governance design, and the deeper elite politics around Silicon Valley AI. The notes add that Sam Altman's return was confirmed on the day of release, matching the episode's prediction but leaving unresolved organizational wounds.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
距离OpenAI版“权力的游戏”开播已经过去四天四夜，科技圈全员都在追剧。这集节目我和硅星人与品玩创始人骆轶航老师聊聊，截至目前的剧集全回顾、关键人物光谱、权力失控的根源，以及这背后更深层次的硅谷精英政治话题。
骆老师曾担任《第一财经周刊》驻硅谷记者，后来长期联络中国与硅谷，所以他与本剧里一些关键人物有过直接、多次接触，比如Sam Altman。
这集播客的源起是我读了他最近写的《关于OpenAI CEO被出局的事实、猜测和看法》一文，觉得很受益。这篇文章和播客中提到的人名，都会放在show notes里，供大家参考。
就在节目播出的当天，最新消息确认，Sam Altman将回归——这恰好应证了我们在节目里的预测。但这不意味着皆大欢喜。OpenAI后续还有许多有待妥善解决的问题。
理念未达共识，冲突不会结束，创伤远没愈合。
权力的游戏
•  02:38 四天四夜剧集全回顾
•  08:59 这次政变的根源是什么？从理念之争演化成political斗争剧

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Four days and nights have passed since the OpenAI version of Game of Thrones began, and the entire tech circle has been following the drama. In this episode, I talk with Luo Yihang of Guixingren and PingWest about a full review of the events so far, the spectrum of key figures, the roots of runaway power, and deeper questions of Silicon Valley elite politics behind it all. Luo once worked as a Silicon Valley correspondent and has had direct, repeated contact with several key people, including Sam Altman.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Governance as AI Product Risk
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes show OpenAI's board crisis as more than personnel drama: it exposed how governance design can threaten product continuity, partner confidence, and mission credibility. The hidden alpha is to diligence power mechanisms as rigorously as model capability.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: A leading AI lab can become less investable because of control design even while its technical lead remains intact.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor AI platforms with resilient governance, clear partner rights, and talent-retention mechanisms that can withstand safety-versus-commercial conflict.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source was recorded during the four-day crisis and notes that Altman's return resolved leadership but left organizational wounds unresolved.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: AI competition shifts from model benchmarks alone to governance durability under extreme public and partner pressure.
evidenceSignals: The episode reviews four days and nights of OpenAI upheaval.
The source frames the coup as moving from principle clash to political drama.
It maps dropout/startup factions against academic and big-company factions.
It discusses independent directors and a runaway power mechanism.
The notes say Altman's return was confirmed but organizational wounds remained.
watchlist: Board composition and voting rights
Strategic partner observer or veto arrangements
Senior researcher and executive departures
Safety review process credibility
Product release cadence after governance shocks
risks: The episode was recorded during a fast-moving crisis.
Public narratives may omit private governance facts.
Technical momentum can temporarily mask governance fragility.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes tie the crisis to runaway power mechanisms and board design.
thesisTable.implication: AI diligence must include control architecture.

thesisTable.evidence: The source says Altman's return left unresolved organizational wounds.
thesisTable.implication: Post-crisis talent and safety signals matter.

episodes.titleZh: 47. 接班百亿基金的琐碎小事：徐小平和他的门徒
episodes.titleEn: 47. Small Matters in Succeeding a Ten-Billion-Dollar Fund: Xu Xiaoping and His Disciples
episodes.summaryEn: This interview draws from a reported story on ZhenFund's handoff from its founding generation to the next generation, centered on Xu Xiaoping, Anna Fang, and the firm's internal culture. It covers investor letters, power transition, angel-fund management, founder evaluation methods, and the mentoring system behind the fund.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
前不久，我发表了一篇还原真格基金接班故事的文章《徐小平退后一步，方爱之向前一步：中国风投第一起交接故事》。真格或许是中国一线基金里第一个完成从一代到二代交接和传承的风投机构，非常具有典型性。
这集播客就是其中一部分访谈。基金里那些隐秘的交接故事是如何发生的？这个故事揭开了面纱。
本集录制时间于2022年。如果存在一些实效性问题，希望大家多多包涵。

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Not long ago, I published an article reconstructing the succession story at ZhenFund, Xu Xiaoping Steps Back, Anna Fang Steps Forward: China's First Venture Capital Handoff Story. ZhenFund may be the first top-tier Chinese fund to complete a transition and inheritance from one generation to the next, making it highly representative. This podcast is part of those interviews. How did the hidden handoff stories inside the fund happen? This story lifts the veil, though the recording was made in 2022 and may have timing limitations.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: VC Succession as Institution Design
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes portray ZhenFund's handoff as a rare case where a Chinese top-tier VC tried to turn founder charisma into an institution. The hidden insight is that succession depends on mundane systems: letters, decision rights, founder evaluation, and discipleship.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Fund durability may come less from star-GP brand than from codified mentoring and repeatable founder judgment.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Prefer venture platforms that transfer authority, sourcing culture, and evaluation methodology before founding partners fully step back.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source calls ZhenFund's transition highly representative because it may be the first top-tier Chinese VC handoff from one generation to the next.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Chinese venture capital moves from personality-led origin stories toward institutional continuity.
evidenceSignals: The episode centers on Xu Xiaoping, Anna Fang, and ZhenFund's internal culture.
The source explicitly covers the power transition process.
It includes investor letters and angel-fund management.
It discusses quantitative study of founders.
It closes with Xu Xiaoping and his disciples.
watchlist: LP letters explaining succession
Investment committee authority changes
Carry and economics for next-generation partners
Founder-evaluation frameworks
Alumni and mentor network activity
risks: The recording was made in 2022 and may have timing limitations.
VC succession details are often opaque.
Star-founder reputation may still dominate LP decisions.

thesisTable.claim: Succession is built from small systems.
thesisTable.evidence: The notes emphasize letters, power transition, founder study, and disciples.
thesisTable.implication: Platform quality can outlast founding charisma.

thesisTable.evidence: The episode includes quantitative study of founders.
thesisTable.implication: Repeatable selection may become a fund-level moat.

episodes.titleZh: 46. 失去逍遥子的第一个双十一：诡计、大混战与小阳谋
episodes.titleEn: 46. The First Singles' Day Without Xiaoyaozi: Schemes, Platform Melee, and Small Open Strategies
episodes.summaryEn: Former Taobao Live operator Zhao Yuanyuan discusses the 15th Singles' Day and the first one after Daniel Zhang's departure from Alibaba leadership. The episode maps platform tactics, merchant and consumer psychology, live-commerce dynamics, super-influencer cycles, and the history and possible future of Chinese e-commerce.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
在2023年11月11日聊聊，来到第15个年头的双十一，也是失去逍遥子的第一个双十一。
我们谈到在这场年度大戏里的各方心态和诡计，15年双十一背后的中国电商演进史，以及双十一会消失吗？节目里也有很多双十一战役里的平台内部故事。
【嘉宾小记】
赵圆圆是前淘宝直播运营负责人，他参与过孵化薇娅和李佳琦，此前是奥美创意总监。
•  01:45 今年双十一：鸡肋的？割裂的？充满阳谋的？
•  08:23 淘宝、京东、拼多多、抖音、快手、小红书、得物、唯品会……有史以来最浩瀚的平台大乱战

•  39:46 为什么淘宝形成了以薇娅和李佳琦为超头主播的马太效应生态？和抖音生态（反超头）差别那么大的根源是什么？
•  44:21 超头主播的兴衰与临界点

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: On November 11, 2023, we talk about Singles' Day in its 15th year, and also the first Singles' Day after losing Xiaoyaozi. We discuss the mindsets and schemes of all sides in this annual drama, the evolution of Chinese e-commerce behind 15 years of Singles' Day, and whether Singles' Day will disappear. The episode also contains many internal platform stories from Singles' Day campaigns. Guest Zhao Yuanyuan previously led Taobao Live operations and helped incubate Viya and Li Jiaqi before working as an Ogilvy creative director.

outline.textZh: 今年双十一：鸡肋的？割裂的？充满阳谋的？
outline.textEn: This year's Singles' Day: tasteless, fragmented, and full of open strategies?

outline.textZh: 淘宝、京东、拼多多、抖音、快手、小红书、得物、唯品会……有史以来最浩瀚的平台大乱战
outline.textEn: Taobao, JD, Pinduoduo, Douyin, Kuaishou, Xiaohongshu, Dewu, Vipshop, and the largest platform melee ever

outline.textZh: 双十一的内涵演变，它对于各平台/商家/消费者分别意味着什么
outline.textEn: How the meaning of Singles' Day has evolved for platforms, merchants, and consumers

outline.textZh: 消费者精准画像：二线城市25-35岁已婚女性＞学生＞中老年
outline.textEn: A precise consumer portrait: married women aged 25-35 in second-tier cities, then students, then middle-aged and older consumers

outline.textZh: 男女购物习惯的差异根源——“互联网消费环境是为女性设计的，男生敲个边鼓”
outline.textEn: The root difference between male and female shopping habits: the online consumption environment is designed for women

outline.textZh: 空前复杂的双十一是怎么形成的？（老板都头疼，但解决不了）
outline.textEn: How did Singles' Day become so complex, even though bosses have headaches and cannot solve it?

outline.textZh: 商家能从这次双十一挣到钱吗？消费者心理为什么变得冷淡？
outline.textEn: Can merchants make money this Singles' Day, and why have consumers become cooler?

outline.textZh: 最不像双十一氛围的平台排名：拼多多倒数第二，小红书倒数第一
outline.textEn: Ranking the platforms least like Singles' Day: Pinduoduo second from the bottom, Xiaohongshu last

outline.textZh: 各个平台直播带货的表现
outline.textEn: How each platform performed in livestream commerce

outline.textZh: 为什么淘宝形成了以薇娅和李佳琦为超头主播的马太效应生态？和抖音生态（反超头）差别那么大的根源是什么？
outline.textEn: Why Taobao formed a winner-take-most super-host ecosystem around Viya and Li Jiaqi, unlike Douyin's anti-super-host ecosystem

outline.textZh: 超头主播的兴衰与临界点
outline.textEn: The rise, fall, and turning points of super hosts

outline.textZh: 网红的五个阶段和双十一中的李佳琦事件
outline.textEn: The five stages of internet celebrities and the Li Jiaqi incident during Singles' Day

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Singles' Day Becomes a Fragmented Operating System
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes describe the 15th Singles' Day as tasteless, fragmented, and crowded by the largest platform melee. The hidden alpha is that the festival is no longer a single demand spike but an operating system for testing platform power, merchant economics, and host concentration.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Anti-super-host ecosystems may compound better than winner-take-most livestream networks as consumers cool and merchants seek margin.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Back merchant tools and platforms that support multichannel live commerce, attribution, and margin control rather than dependence on mega-events or mega-hosts.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source marks the first Singles' Day after Daniel Zhang's departure and compares Taobao, JD, Pinduoduo, Douyin, Kuaishou, Xiaohongshu, Dewu, and Vipshop in one melee.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Chinese e-commerce shifts from centralized festival GMV theater to fragmented, always-on attention competition.
evidenceSignals: The episode calls the year tasteless, fragmented, and full of open strategies.
It lists the largest platform melee across major Chinese commerce platforms.
The source asks whether merchants can make money and why consumers became cooler.
It contrasts Taobao's super-host ecosystem with Douyin's anti-super-host ecosystem.
It maps platform, merchant, and consumer psychology.
watchlist: Merchant return on promotion spend
Host concentration and bargaining power
Platform subsidy intensity
Consumer repeat purchase after festivals
Livestream conversion and return rates
risks: Promotional complexity can destroy merchant margin.
Regulatory action can alter influencer economics.
Consumer fatigue can make festival data noisy.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes call it fragmented and ask whether it will disappear.
thesisTable.implication: Platform quality should be judged beyond one-day GMV.

thesisTable.evidence: The source contrasts Taobao's super-hosts with Douyin's anti-super-host system.
thesisTable.implication: Influencer concentration is a measurable platform risk.

episodes.titleZh: 45. 漫谈宝洁：隐藏在186年消费品王国里的规则和秘密
episodes.titleEn: 45. A Broad Talk on P&G: Rules and Secrets Hidden in a 186-Year Consumer-Goods Empire
episodes.summaryEn: Allen Wang recounts P&G's internal talent system, brand management culture, and long-lived operating principles from an insider's perspective. The episode explores why a company without flashy hit-product instincts still endured for nearly two centuries through discipline, promotion rules, consumer research, and organizational continuity.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
这一集和Allen聊有186年历史、坐拥3500多亿美金市值的消费品王国——宝洁。
这家公司是怎么扛过近两个世纪枪林弹雨的？令我惊讶的是，它不是一家有强烈爆品思维和创意基因的公司，甚至有点笨笨的。那么，它的人才、原则、方法论、体系如何支撑这个巨无霸军团走到今天？
本集嘉宾Allen（王怀南）上世纪90年代末入职宝洁美国，他是少见地以华人身份掌舵一个美国消费品品牌的人，宝洁历史上的传奇CEO雷富礼曾经是他在内部的coach。Allen以亲历者的视角给我们带来了隐藏在这个王国里面的绵密干货和一手细节。
【嘉宾小记】
王怀南（Allen）的职业生涯贯穿了上世纪90年代到现在。从清华半导体+英文系起步，到纽约的哥大社会学系求学，自此迈入美国的社会大熔炉。凭借着对社会的兴趣，毕业后在美国走了一条很少有华人走的路——做咨询、市场这些广泛与文化、人打交道的工作。他先后加入麦肯锡咨询、宝洁、Yahoo、Google，横跨美国最具有时代标志性的企业。直到2006年回国创业，是上市公司宝宝树、初创公司米茶科技创始人，也是陆奇创办的奇绩创坛合伙人、导师。
•  02:05 90年代末的美国，哪家公司最难进？美林、通用电气、宝洁
•  04:15 宝洁的一个严肃传统：不喜欢被拒绝offer，不允许第二次敲他的门
•  06:52 宝洁曾经有巨大的食品和饮料部门，但在过去十几年统统卖掉
•  09:18 “任何人可以伪装，但你不可能伪装十次”
•  10:45 宝洁在中国有6000+毕业生，知名校友如蔚来汽车秦力洪
•  11:34 宝洁美国最喜欢的人才：本科军校，硕士商学院，中间最好在军队作战
•  12:26 无数宝洁CEO从“品牌助理经理”出发——美国人有一个梦想，有一天成为美国总统；宝洁品牌经理有一个梦想，从助理品牌出发，有一天成为宝洁CEO
•  13:48 宝洁原则之一：Promotion From Within（只从内部提拔）
•  14:50 宝洁原则之二：Up Or Out（不提升，就滚蛋）
•  15:35 始料未及的白热化竞争：你们中只能活1-2个
•  17:54 我从品客薯片的品牌助理经理出发，如何在那届KO了所有人？而且，收获了宝洁一任伟大的CEO雷富礼（A.G. Lafley）做我的教练
•  21:40 这家近190年的公司会孜孜不倦地做美国家庭入户调研
•  24:55 消费品王国的起点：德国的后裔、移民、战争、象牙肥皂——秉持了德意志民族的精神
•  27:52 宝洁每30多年遇到一次大周期、大挑战（近十年在中国就是其中之一）
•  28:49 从家族
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: In this episode, Allen and I talk about P&G, a consumer-goods empire with 186 years of history and a market value of more than $350 billion. How did this company survive nearly two centuries of gunfire and turbulence? What surprised me is that it is not a company with a strong hit-product mindset or creative gene, and even seems a bit clumsy. So how did its talent, principles, methodology, and systems support this giant army until today, including its promotion and brand-manager rules?

outline.textZh: 90年代末的美国，哪家公司最难进？美林、通用电气、宝洁
outline.textEn: In late-1990s America, which companies were hardest to get into? Merrill Lynch, General Electric, and P&G

outline.textZh: 宝洁的一个严肃传统：不喜欢被拒绝offer，不允许第二次敲他的门
outline.textEn: A serious P&G tradition: it dislikes rejected offers and does not allow you to knock a second time

outline.textZh: 宝洁曾经有巨大的食品和饮料部门，但在过去十几年统统卖掉
outline.textEn: P&G once had huge food and beverage divisions, but sold them all over the past decade-plus

outline.textZh: “任何人可以伪装，但你不可能伪装十次”
outline.textEn: Anyone can pretend once, but you cannot pretend ten times

outline.textZh: 宝洁在中国有6000+毕业生，知名校友如蔚来汽车秦力洪
outline.textEn: P&G has more than 6,000 alumni in China, including Nio's Qin Lihong

outline.textZh: 宝洁美国最喜欢的人才：本科军校，硕士商学院，中间最好在军队作战
outline.textEn: The type of talent P&G America likes most: military academy undergraduate, business-school master's, and ideally combat experience in between

outline.textZh: 无数宝洁CEO从“品牌助理经理”出发——美国人有一个梦想，有一天成为美国总统；宝洁品牌经理有一个梦想，从助理品牌出发，有一天成为宝洁CEO
outline.textEn: Countless P&G CEOs started as assistant brand managers, dreaming of one day becoming P&G CEO

outline.textZh: 宝洁原则之一：Promotion From Within（只从内部提拔）
outline.textEn: P&G principle one: promotion from within

outline.textZh: 宝洁原则之二：Up Or Out（不提升，就滚蛋）
outline.textEn: P&G principle two: up or out

outline.textZh: 始料未及的白热化竞争：你们中只能活1-2个
outline.textEn: Unexpectedly fierce competition: only one or two of you can survive

outline.textZh: 我从品客薯片的品牌助理经理出发，如何在那届KO了所有人？而且，收获了宝洁一任伟大的CEO雷富礼（A.G. Lafley）做我的教练
outline.textEn: Starting as assistant brand manager for Pringles, defeating everyone in that cohort, and gaining A.G. Lafley as coach

outline.textZh: 这家近190年的公司会孜孜不倦地做美国家庭入户调研
outline.textEn: This nearly 190-year-old company tirelessly conducts in-home research with American families

alphaLens.alphaTitle: The Boring Talent System Moat
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes argue that P&G endured not because it was flashy, but because it made disciplined talent and brand-management systems repeatable. The hidden alpha is that clumsiness can be resilience when promotion rules and consumer research outlast product cycles.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: A company without a strong hit-product gene can still be structurally advantaged if its talent system compounds.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Value consumer incumbents and P&G-like alumni networks where internal promotion, brand-manager discipline, and portfolio pruning remain intact.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source uses P&G's 186-year history, promotion from within, up-or-out rules, and China alumni network to explain durability in a volatile consumer market.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Consumer-goods alpha moves from creative heroics toward repeatable talent pipelines and portfolio discipline.
evidenceSignals: The source says P&G is not a strong hit-product or creative-gene company.
It highlights promotion from within as a core principle.
It highlights up-or-out competition.
The notes say many P&G CEOs began as assistant brand managers.
The episode mentions more than 6,000 P&G alumni in China.
watchlist: Internal promotion ratios
Brand-manager rotation and training quality
Portfolio divestitures and focus
P&G alumni in Chinese consumer and auto companies
Consumer research investment discipline
risks: Process discipline can become bureaucracy.
The notes rely on insider perspective rather than full financial analysis.
Hit-driven challenger brands can still disrupt categories.

thesisTable.evidence: The source emphasizes promotion from within and CEOs starting as assistant brand managers.
thesisTable.implication: Management pipelines deserve valuation weight.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes mention P&G selling food and beverage divisions.
thesisTable.implication: Portfolio discipline supports longevity.

episodes.titleZh: 44. 谈诺贝尔经济奖与40+消失在黄金阶梯上的女人
episodes.titleEn: 44. On the Nobel Prize in Economics and Women Over 40 Disappearing From the Golden Ladder
episodes.summaryEn: Economist Zhao Yaohui discusses Claudia Goldin's Nobel-recognized research on women and labor markets, then connects it to Chinese workplace realities. The episode examines early female career advantage, motherhood penalties, retirement-age discrimination, later marriage and childbirth, and what systems might help women pursue both work and family.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
北京时间10月9日，瑞典皇家科学院宣布，2023年诺贝尔经济学奖授予给哈佛经济系教授Claudia Goldin，表彰她“增进了我们对女性劳动力市场结果的理解”。
本集我邀请北大教授、经济学家赵耀辉女士做客。我们从Goldin和她的研究聊起，引申到很多中国女性面临的现实话题：
1. 我们观察到，现在在一线城市的投资/咨询/高校等职业中，站在事业起点上20+女性表现大大优于男性。2. 但与此同时，很多20+闪闪发光的事业女性，在40+消失在了黄金阶梯上。3. 对当代女性，晚婚婚育会不会是更优解？4. 社会怎么完善制度，帮助女性同时获得事业和家庭？
这集能给职场女生有很多启发。
【嘉宾小记】
赵耀辉是北京大学国家发展研究院经济学教授。1995年于美国芝加哥大学获得经济学博士学位，曾任教于美国乔治华盛顿大学，1996年开始任教于北京大学国家发展研究院/中国经济研究中心，长期专注劳动经济学和老年经济学的研究与教学。赵耀辉教授曾于2006年入选教育部“新世纪优秀人才支持计划”，也是中国健康与养老追踪调查（CHARLS）项目组负责人，《The Journal of the Economics of Ageing》副总编，曾任北京大学中国女经济学者研究培训项目联合主任。
•  02:55 Claudia Goldin教授和她的研究和书（《事业还是家庭》）：把女性追求平等的百年历程，分成5组做实证研究
•  07:35 “性别歧视”在历史轨迹中的演变
•  09:30 避孕药带来的“quiet revolution”(安静的革命）
•  15:30 歧视少了，但女性面临的“生育惩罚”/“母职惩罚”持续存在
•  18:14 在家庭内部，生孩子之后女性和她的丈夫收入差距拉大
•  19:12 隐藏在我们周围无处不在的“性别歧视”（女性退休年龄比男性要早5年，是一种性别歧视）
•  26:25 初入职场，在许多行业内部，20+的女性表现优于男性
•  30:28 然而，女性优势随着时间被慢慢消磨，以学校、企业、政府为例
•  31:10 上述变化的根源？Goldin给出解释
•  35:09 中国要防止出现Goldin研究中的第四组现象（女性为了事业牺牲家庭）
•  35:58 社会怎么完善制度，能够帮助女性同时获得事业和家庭？
•  37:12 为什么鼓励生育的政策频出，却没有提高生育率？
•  40:39 晚婚晚育值得鼓励吗？这会给社会带来怎样的结构性变化？
•  46:02 给职业女性、事业女性一些建议
【更多信息】
本集首发于腾讯新闻，点击前往音频版 (https://pa
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: On October 9 Beijing time, the Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences announced that the 2023 Nobel Prize in Economics was awarded to Harvard economics professor Claudia Goldin for advancing our understanding of women's labor-market outcomes. In this episode, I invite Peking University professor and economist Zhao Yaohui to join us. Starting from Goldin and her research, we extend the discussion to many real issues faced by Chinese women: young women who shine at the start of their careers, women over 40 who disappear from the golden ladder, and whether later marriage and childbearing may be a better solution.

outline.textZh: Claudia Goldin教授和她的研究和书（《事业还是家庭》）：把女性追求平等的百年历程，分成5组做实证研究
outline.textEn: Professor Claudia Goldin, her research and book Career and Family, and empirical studies of five cohorts in women's century-long pursuit of equality

outline.textZh: “性别歧视”在历史轨迹中的演变
outline.textEn: How gender discrimination evolved along the historical trajectory

outline.textZh: 避孕药带来的“quiet revolution”(安静的革命）
outline.textEn: The quiet revolution brought by the contraceptive pill

outline.textZh: 歧视少了，但女性面临的“生育惩罚”/“母职惩罚”持续存在
outline.textEn: Discrimination has decreased, but the childbirth and motherhood penalty for women persists

outline.textZh: 在家庭内部，生孩子之后女性和她的丈夫收入差距拉大
outline.textEn: Inside families, the income gap between women and their husbands widens after childbirth

outline.textZh: 隐藏在我们周围无处不在的“性别歧视”（女性退休年龄比男性要早5年，是一种性别歧视）
outline.textEn: Gender discrimination hidden everywhere around us, such as women retiring five years earlier than men

outline.textZh: 初入职场，在许多行业内部，20+的女性表现优于男性
outline.textEn: At career entry, women in their 20s outperform men in many industries

outline.textZh: 然而，女性优势随着时间被慢慢消磨，以学校、企业、政府为例
outline.textEn: Yet women's advantages are gradually worn down over time, with schools, companies, and government as examples

outline.textZh: 上述变化的根源？Goldin给出解释
outline.textEn: What explains these changes? Goldin's answer

outline.textZh: 中国要防止出现Goldin研究中的第四组现象（女性为了事业牺牲家庭）
outline.textEn: China should avoid Goldin's fourth-group phenomenon, where women sacrifice family for career

outline.textZh: 社会怎么完善制度，能够帮助女性同时获得事业和家庭？
outline.textEn: How can society improve systems to help women gain both career and family?

outline.textZh: 为什么鼓励生育的政策频出，却没有提高生育率？
outline.textEn: Why have policies encouraging childbirth not raised the birth rate?

alphaLens.alphaTitle: The Over-40 Talent Mispricing
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes use Claudia Goldin's work to show that women can shine early yet disappear from the golden ladder after childbirth, family-care constraints, and age-linked systems compound. The hidden alpha is that firms wasting midcareer female talent are carrying an avoidable productivity leak.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The core investable issue is not entry-level gender discrimination but the career architecture that erodes female advantage after the 20s.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor employers, platforms, and services that reduce motherhood penalties, preserve midcareer progression, and make flexible high-skill work operationally normal.
alphaLens.whyNow: The Nobel recognition elevated Goldin's research while the episode connects it directly to Chinese workplace realities such as early female outperformance and over-40 disappearance.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Gender-equity analysis shifts from hiring access to lifetime career design around family, time, and retirement rules.
evidenceSignals: The source references Goldin's Nobel-recognized work on women's labor-market outcomes.
It discusses childbirth and motherhood penalties.
It notes women retiring five years earlier than men as hidden discrimination.
The episode says women in their 20s outperform men in many industries.
It says women's advantages are gradually worn down over time.
watchlist: Female retention and promotion after childbirth
Midcareer female leadership ratios
Retirement-age policy changes
Childcare and flexible-work adoption
Pay gap after first child
risks: Policy and household norms can change slowly.
The notes do not provide company-level datasets.
Later marriage or childbirth may not solve structural incentives.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes say early female advantages are worn down after childbirth and age effects.
thesisTable.implication: Retention after life events is a productivity signal.

thesisTable.evidence: The episode cites women retiring five years earlier than men.
thesisTable.implication: Policy change can reprice labor participation.

episodes.titleZh: 43.【汽车史话03】从“盛产管理大师”的通用汽车，看美式车企百年荣辱
episodes.titleEn: 43. Auto History 03: General Motors, the Company That Produced Management Masters, and a Century of American Auto Glory and Decline
episodes.summaryEn: The third Auto History episode contrasts General Motors with Ford by focusing on William Durant, acquisitions, outside capital, and the rise of professional management. It traces how GM ended the Model T myth, became a management-school case through Alfred Sloan and Peter Drucker, and later faced crises, labor pressure, and brand-strategy dilemmas.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
今天播出的是《汽车史话》第三集，主角是曾是全球最大的跨国车企通用汽车。
和上集福特公司所展现的家族制、集权和早期坚持做单一车型很不同，通用汽车几乎处处是它的反面，然而正是这样一家公司终结了T型车的神话。
通用有一位特立独行、擅长营销又极度热爱买买买的创始人，这位并购狂人曾3年收购25家公司。而他又总是把公司的管理和财务状况搞得很糟，为了挽救公司他不得不引入财团，陷入权力的博弈，自己也一度被赶出公司。
这些都间接影响通用。财团主导的历史使创始人无法将权力攥在自己手里，反而使这家公司为职业经理人和现代化管理提供了土壤——这让它成为“一家盛产管理学大师的公司”——不管是被誉为20世纪最伟大的CEO斯隆，还是现代管理学之父彼得·德鲁克，都和这家公司有千丝万缕的关系。
这集我们就看通用这个巨人是如何长成，又是如何慢慢倒下的？

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Today is the third episode of Auto History, and the main character is General Motors, once the world's largest multinational automaker. Unlike Ford, which showed family control, centralization, and an early insistence on a single model, GM was almost the opposite in every way, yet it was this company that ended the myth of the Model T. GM had an idiosyncratic founder who was good at marketing and extremely fond of buying companies. This merger maniac acquired 25 companies in three years.

outline.textZh: 先聊聊德国慕尼黑车展见闻：想低调但规格异常高
outline.textEn: First, observations from the Munich auto show: trying to stay low-key despite unusually high specifications

outline.textZh: 今年上半年中国超越日本，成为汽车出口第一大国
outline.textEn: In the first half of this year, China surpassed Japan to become the largest auto exporter

outline.textZh: “China Speed”:从汽车开发流程看，德国车企需要36个月，中国车企只要24个月
outline.textEn: China speed in auto development: German automakers need 36 months, while Chinese automakers need only 24

outline.textZh: 在大众和小鹏、地平线，奥迪和上汽智己接连展开合作的背景下，骄傲的德国人对中国车企的看法？
outline.textEn: How proud Germans view Chinese automakers amid Volkswagen-Xpeng-Horizon and Audi-SAIC IM Motors cooperation

outline.textZh: 通用汽车早期历程：创始人威廉·杜兰特从马车制造商到并购别克
outline.textEn: GM's early history: founder William Durant from carriage maker to acquiring Buick

outline.textZh: 杜兰特善于推销，为别克带回了1000多辆订单结果只造出20辆，遭遇停职
outline.textEn: Durant was good at sales, bringing Buick more than 1,000 orders but producing only 20 cars, leading to suspension

outline.textZh: 喜欢买买买、并并并的杜兰特先生，短短三年收了25家公司，这让通用从第一天就和福特大有不同
outline.textEn: Durant loved buying and merging, acquiring 25 companies in just three years, making GM very different from Ford from day one

outline.textZh: 然而买买买的另一面是，遭遇财务危机，杜兰特引入两家大财团，从被扫地出门到把公司夺回来
outline.textEn: The other side of endless buying: a financial crisis, the introduction of two financial groups, and Durant's ouster and comeback

outline.textZh: 杜兰特和亨利·福特的性格对比
outline.textEn: Comparing the personalities of Durant and Henry Ford

outline.textZh: 独立独行的创始人+创始人被扫地出门+财团主导的架构，为“职业经理人”提供了土壤
outline.textEn: An idiosyncratic founder, founder ouster, and financier-led structure created soil for professional managers

outline.textZh: 通用汽车从创始人向职业经理人治理阶段过渡
outline.textEn: GM's transition from founder governance to professional-manager governance

outline.textZh: 艾尔弗雷德·斯隆被称作20世纪最伟大的CEO，他终结了T型车的神话
outline.textEn: Alfred Sloan, called the greatest CEO of the 20th century, ended the Model T myth

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Professional Management Beats the Founder Myth
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes position GM as Ford's opposite: acquisition-heavy, financier-shaped, and fertile ground for professional managers. The hidden alpha is that founder ouster and outside capital can become strengths when they create a management system rather than chaos.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Founder displacement can be value-creating in autos if it replaces product obsession with brand, capital, and management discipline.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Look for automakers and industrial platforms that combine portfolio architecture, capital discipline, and professional management depth rather than relying on a single founder-product myth.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source connects GM's management history to current auto shifts, including China speed and German-Chinese cooperation.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Auto competition moves from heroic product invention to organized management of brands, capital, labor, and platforms.
evidenceSignals: Durant acquired 25 companies in three years.
The source says financial crisis brought in two financial groups and founder ouster.
It describes GM as the company that ended the Model T myth.
It links GM to Sloan, Drucker, and professional management.
The notes mention later crises, labor pressure, and brand-strategy dilemmas.
watchlist: M&A integration discipline
Brand architecture clarity
Capital allocation after founder transitions
Labor cost and union pressure
Development-cycle speed versus competitors
risks: Historical analogies can overfit current EV conditions.
Professional management can drift into bureaucracy.
Acquisition sprawl can recreate Durant's financial stress.

thesisTable.evidence: The source says GM ended the Model T myth and became a management-school case.
thesisTable.implication: Auto diligence should test operating systems, not only models.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes say financiers entered after Durant's crisis.
thesisTable.implication: Governance can create room for professional managers.

episodes.titleZh: 42.【汽车史话02】集权者福特与全球第二畅销T型车的衰落
episodes.titleEn: 42. Auto History 02: Centralizer Ford and the Decline of the World's Second-Best-Selling Model T
episodes.summaryEn: The second Auto History episode centers on Henry Ford, the Model T, and the organizational consequences of family control and centralization. It compares Ford with GM, discusses vertical integration, the Model T's success and decline, Ford family governance, and lessons for today's automakers.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
《商业访谈录》正在播出一个商业史系列，我和张君毅老师聊聊《汽车史话》，目标是勾勒100多年汽车工业的发展历程，描摹百年汽车史中的那些闪耀瞬间，也希望从历史片段中给今天的中国汽车产业找到借鉴。
今天是第二集，主题是汽车史上第一位亿万富翁亨利·福特先生，以及那个时代的超级垄断车型：T型车。
1908年发生了两件大事。第一件是福特旗下T型车问世，第二件事是通用汽车诞生。这同一时期的两家企业，有两个相反性格的创始人（福特和杜兰特），塑造了两种不同的组织和文化，也选择了相左的两种战略。建议把这一期和我们接下来要推出的关于通用汽车的一集对比收听。
我们也会以历史为镜，把这两家企业和今天的车企进行一些比照。
•  02:51 亨利·福特：从农场主的儿子到汽车史上第一个亿万富翁
•  04:33 奢侈品不是他的目标，他想做让普罗大众能承担的东西
•  05:38 奔驰先生vs福特先生的原生家庭与性格养成
•  10:47 当我们有些车企挣扎于5万/10万销量的时候，T型车已成为全球销售量第二的车型（1500万-1600万）
•  12:16 T型车为什么只做一款车型？为什么是黑色的？
•  14:32 福特是最早做上下游垂直整合的企业家，他和马斯克有变革者的相似性
•  16:32 福特一直希望公司作为家族永续产业，最大遗憾是他的儿子埃德塞尔·福特去世
•  17:18 亨利·福特的孙子威廉·福特起到承前启后的作用，开始引入人才
•  19:20 最大的敌人也是后来的超越者：通用汽车
•  20:23 为什么T型车没落？
•  20:54 老福特的保守与刚愎自用
•  21:58 这些美国公司很注重盈利性，福特内部能做财务的人晋升快——这让他们经受住了金融危机
•  23:39 福特定义了“车轮上的王国”
•  27:32 福特家族对于公司的控制很强，这也直接造成传奇人物李·艾柯卡离开，他后来将克莱斯勒从破产边缘拯救出来
•  30:05 美国汽车产业因何没落？不能在中国站住脚，如今想成为全球领军车企很难
•  31:33 福特犯过的战略失误（单一车型、低价销售、离中国远）
•  33:05 福特先生坎坷的晚年
•  33:42 不愿意退休的一代和憋屈的二代
•  35:35 “家长式的管理”发生在很多创一代的企业
•  36:22 金融危机以后，福特导入了体系外人才救助业务
•  37:06 家族意味着长期性，职业经理人意味着创新性，需要平衡两者关系
•  37:37 战将：李·艾柯卡（曾任福特总裁）和艾伦·穆拉利（现任福特总裁兼CEO）
•  41:38 亨利·福特的太太和
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Business Interviews is airing a business-history series in which Zhang Junyi and I discuss Auto History. The goal is to sketch more than 100 years of automotive development, portray shining moments in a century of auto history, and find lessons from historical fragments for China's auto industry today. Today's second episode is about Henry Ford, the first billionaire in automotive history, and the super-monopoly model of that era, the Model T. In 1908, two big events occurred: Ford launched the Model T and General Motors was born.

outline.textZh: 亨利·福特：从农场主的儿子到汽车史上第一个亿万富翁
outline.textEn: Henry Ford: from farmer's son to the first billionaire in auto history

outline.textZh: 奢侈品不是他的目标，他想做让普罗大众能承担的东西
outline.textEn: Luxury goods were not his target; he wanted to make something ordinary people could afford

outline.textZh: 奔驰先生vs福特先生的原生家庭与性格养成
outline.textEn: Mr. Benz versus Mr. Ford: family backgrounds and character formation

outline.textZh: 当我们有些车企挣扎于5万/10万销量的时候，T型车已成为全球销售量第二的车型（1500万-1600万）
outline.textEn: While some automakers struggle with 50,000 or 100,000 sales, the Model T became the world's second-best-selling car, with 15-16 million sold

outline.textZh: T型车为什么只做一款车型？为什么是黑色的？
outline.textEn: Why did the Model T have only one model, and why was it black?

outline.textZh: 福特是最早做上下游垂直整合的企业家，他和马斯克有变革者的相似性
outline.textEn: Ford was among the first entrepreneurs to vertically integrate upstream and downstream, with reformer similarities to Musk

outline.textZh: 福特一直希望公司作为家族永续产业，最大遗憾是他的儿子埃德塞尔·福特去世
outline.textEn: Ford wanted the company to remain a family enterprise forever, and his greatest regret was the death of his son Edsel Ford

outline.textZh: 亨利·福特的孙子威廉·福特起到承前启后的作用，开始引入人才
outline.textEn: Henry Ford's grandson William Ford linked past and future and began bringing in talent

outline.textZh: 最大的敌人也是后来的超越者：通用汽车
outline.textEn: The greatest enemy and later surpasser: General Motors

outline.textZh: 为什么T型车没落？
outline.textEn: Why did the Model T decline?

outline.textZh: 老福特的保守与刚愎自用
outline.textEn: Old Ford's conservatism and stubbornness

outline.textZh: 这些美国公司很注重盈利性，福特内部能做财务的人晋升快——这让他们经受住了金融危机
outline.textEn: These American companies valued profitability, and finance people inside Ford advanced quickly, helping them survive financial crisis

alphaLens.alphaTitle: The One-Model Trap
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes show that the Model T's extreme success also encoded Ford's later weakness: centralization, family control, and a single-model worldview. The hidden alpha is that vertical integration only compounds while demand stays simple.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The more dominant a one-model system becomes, the more vulnerable it can be when consumer preference fragments.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Back automakers that pair supply-chain integration with model-refresh flexibility, external talent, and governance capable of admitting change.
alphaLens.whyNow: The episode explicitly seeks lessons from Ford's history for today's Chinese new-energy vehicle industry.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Auto advantage shifts from scale-of-one efficiency to portfolio responsiveness under technology transition.
evidenceSignals: The source says the Model T sold 15-16 million units.
It asks why the Model T had only one model and why it was black.
It describes Ford as an early vertical integrator.
It notes Ford wanted the company to remain a family enterprise forever.
It identifies GM as the enemy and later surpasser.
watchlist: Product refresh cadence
Governance openness to outside talent
Degree of upstream and downstream integration
Model mix diversification
Customer preference fragmentation
risks: Integration can still win during supply shortages.
The lesson is historical rather than company-specific.
Portfolio breadth can dilute execution focus.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes link Model T success with its later decline.
thesisTable.implication: Scale must be paired with product renewal.

thesisTable.evidence: The source emphasizes Ford family control and later talent introduction.
thesisTable.implication: Leadership openness is an auto-cycle signal.

episodes.titleZh: 41.【汽车史话01】世界上第一辆燃油车、女车手和慈禧太后
episodes.titleEn: 41. Auto History 01: The World's First Fuel Car, a Female Driver, and Empress Dowager Cixi
episodes.summaryEn: This first Auto History episode starts in the 1880s with Karl Benz, Bertha Benz, Gottlieb Daimler, and the birth of internal-combustion automobiles. It uses early engineering, driving culture, fuel infrastructure, regulation, and even Cixi's car ownership to connect automotive invention with industrial, energy, and social change.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
《商业访谈录》正在启动一个新系列，我邀请了我们的往期嘉宾张君毅老师来聊《汽车史话》，目标是勾勒100多年汽车工业的发展历程，描摹百年汽车史中的那些闪耀瞬间，也希望从历史片段中给今天的中国汽车产业找到一点点借鉴。今天是第一集，我们从遥远的19世纪80年代开始聊起。
张君毅从1996年开始在同济大学攻读汽车专业，然后从事汽车相关咨询和投资快20年。他目前是奥纬咨询董事合伙人，此前是蔚来资本创始和管理合伙人。
这集节目我们聊到的主角人物是，奔驰先生和他的夫人贝莎、戴姆勒先生。
这个系列刚启动，大家对于我们有任何的建议和想法，都欢迎在评论区积极留言。
•  02:22 1886年，第一辆内燃机汽车（三轮车）拿到专利
•  02:35 奔驰先生（卡尔·弗里德里希·奔驰，Karl Friedrich Benz）其人：奔驰汽车创始人，落实了发动机在车上应用，被誉为“汽车之父”
•  03:40 戴姆勒先生（戈特利布·戴姆勒，Gottlieb Daimler）的百年遗憾，他成为了四轮内燃机汽车的发明人
•  04:27 奔驰先生是一位连续创业者，失败的创业逼迫他研究内燃机
•  05:29 汽车品牌创始人很多是工程师背景，他们未必是汽车行业出身，但一定在其他产业有很长时间积淀
•  06:48 作为德国汽车之都，斯图加特哺育了很多品牌（奔驰、博世、保时捷）
•  07:30 世界上第一位车手是女车手，即奔驰先生的夫人（贝莎·奔驰，Bertha Benz），她开着车回娘家
•  08:18 当时没有加油站，汽油是在药房里配的
•  09:05 周围人称呼奔驰夫人为“女巫”
•  10:45 英国曾制定“红旗法案”，车前面有一个人引道，间接影响了当年英国的汽车业发展
•  14:06 在当时汽车是富人的玩具与梅赛德斯品牌名的诞生（金主爸爸女儿的名字）
•  16:00 当动力总成改变的时候，很多技术会改变，很多企业也会因此消亡
•  16:37 对世界上第一辆内燃机汽车的描述
•  21:25 100多年以来，整个汽车产业没有停止过新品牌的诞生，也没有停止旧品牌的衰败
•  25:06 慈禧太后：中国第一位车主
•  29:41 上一次关于内燃机的能源选择
•  35:15 汽车历史的驱动力是汽车文化。汽车是改变世界的机器，它不仅是工业革命，也是能源革命，同时改变了社会结构、经济面貌
•  22:30 车企的“合久必分，分久必合”
•  45:25 聊聊中国新能源汽车创始人和太太

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Business Interviews is launching a new series. I invited our previous guest Zhang Junyi to talk about Auto History, aiming to sketch more than 100 years of automotive development, portray the shining moments in a century of car history, and find a little reference for China's new-energy vehicle industry today. This first episode starts in the distant 1880s. Zhang Junyi began studying automotive engineering at Tongji University in 1996 and has worked in automotive consulting and investment for nearly 20 years.

outline.textZh: 1886年，第一辆内燃机汽车（三轮车）拿到专利
outline.textEn: In 1886, the first internal-combustion automobile, a three-wheeler, received a patent

outline.textZh: 奔驰先生（卡尔·弗里德里希·奔驰，Karl Friedrich Benz）其人：奔驰汽车创始人，落实了发动机在车上应用，被誉为“汽车之父”
outline.textEn: Karl Friedrich Benz: founder of Benz automobiles, who applied the engine to the car and is known as the father of the automobile

outline.textZh: 戴姆勒先生（戈特利布·戴姆勒，Gottlieb Daimler）的百年遗憾，他成为了四轮内燃机汽车的发明人
outline.textEn: Gottlieb Daimler's century-long regret: he became the inventor of the four-wheel internal-combustion automobile

outline.textZh: 奔驰先生是一位连续创业者，失败的创业逼迫他研究内燃机
outline.textEn: Benz was a serial entrepreneur whose failed startup forced him to study the internal-combustion engine

outline.textZh: 汽车品牌创始人很多是工程师背景，他们未必是汽车行业出身，但一定在其他产业有很长时间积淀
outline.textEn: Many auto-brand founders are engineers; they may not come from autos, but they have long experience in other industries

outline.textZh: 作为德国汽车之都，斯图加特哺育了很多品牌（奔驰、博世、保时捷）
outline.textEn: Stuttgart, Germany's auto capital, nurtured many brands including Mercedes-Benz, Bosch, and Porsche

outline.textZh: 世界上第一位车手是女车手，即奔驰先生的夫人（贝莎·奔驰，Bertha Benz），她开着车回娘家
outline.textEn: The world's first driver was a woman, Bertha Benz, who drove home to visit her family

outline.textZh: 当时没有加油站，汽油是在药房里配的
outline.textEn: There were no gas stations then; gasoline was prepared in pharmacies

outline.textZh: 周围人称呼奔驰夫人为“女巫”
outline.textEn: People around her called Mrs. Benz a witch

outline.textZh: 英国曾制定“红旗法案”，车前面有一个人引道，间接影响了当年英国的汽车业发展
outline.textEn: Britain once enacted the Red Flag Act, requiring someone to lead the car from the front and indirectly affecting the British auto industry

outline.textZh: 在当时汽车是富人的玩具与梅赛德斯品牌名的诞生（金主爸爸女儿的名字）
outline.textEn: At the time, cars were toys for the rich, and the Mercedes brand name came from a patron's daughter

outline.textZh: 当动力总成改变的时候，很多技术会改变，很多企业也会因此消亡
outline.textEn: When the powertrain changes, many technologies change and many companies disappear

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Infrastructure Before Adoption
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes make early automobility a system problem: patents, engineers, fuel access, social permission, and regulation all had to align. The hidden alpha is that enabling infrastructure and social proof can matter as much as invention.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Bertha Benz and pharmacy gasoline were not anecdotes; they were early market-formation infrastructure.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: For mobility and energy transitions, invest in enabling layers such as fueling, regulation navigation, supply clusters, and user education alongside vehicles.
alphaLens.whyNow: The episode aims to draw reference points for China's new-energy vehicle industry from the birth of the internal-combustion automobile.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Value migrates from isolated invention to ecosystem coordination.
evidenceSignals: The source starts with the 1886 patent for the first internal-combustion automobile.
It identifies Benz and Daimler as early engineering founders.
It says Bertha Benz was the world's first driver.
It notes gasoline was prepared in pharmacies before gas stations existed.
It discusses the British Red Flag Act and regulatory drag.
watchlist: Charging and refueling-network buildout
Regulatory friction for new mobility categories
Early adopter demographics and advocacy
Supplier clusters around mobility hubs
Public perception and safety narratives
risks: Infrastructure payback can be slow.
Historical parallels may be too broad for specific securities.
Regulation can delay adoption despite technical readiness.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes include fuel access, Bertha Benz's drive, and the Red Flag Act.
thesisTable.implication: Infrastructure layers can capture transition value.

thesisTable.evidence: The source calls Bertha Benz the world's first driver.
thesisTable.implication: User proof can accelerate new-category acceptance.

episodes.titleZh: 40. 和美国科技界人士聊硅谷AI的冷与热：第二波浪潮拉开帷幕
episodes.titleEn: 40. Talking With US Tech Insiders About Silicon Valley AI's Heat and Chill: The Second Wave Begins
episodes.summaryEn: Two US-based Chinese tech insiders, Kevin from GitHub's open-source community and Sophia from a top US VC firm, discuss what American AI looked like 309 days after ChatGPT. The conversation covers the second AIGC wave, consensus that models alone are not products or moats, compute shortages, corporate investment, M&A, copilots, open source, and Washington's role.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
我们做过很多中国AI的节目。自ChatGPT诞生后的309天，美国AI都发生了些什么？他们又达成了哪些新共识？
这集我和两位美国科技界人士聊聊硅谷AI的冷与热。Kevin和Sophia两位是从小移民过去的华人。其中Kevin参与过奥巴们竞选团队，目前在开源社区GitHub；Sophia供职于一家美国Top5的VC机构。他们将从技术和投资视角来分享。

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: We have made many episodes about Chinese AI. In the 309 days since ChatGPT was born, what has happened in American AI, and what new consensus has formed? In this episode, I talk with two people from the US tech world about the hot and cold sides of Silicon Valley AI. Kevin and Sophia are both Chinese immigrants who grew up in the United States. Kevin once participated in Obama's campaign team and now works in GitHub's open-source community; Sophia works at a top-five US venture capital firm.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Models Are Not the Moat
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes capture a second-wave AI consensus: a model is neither a product nor a moat. The hidden alpha is that compute access, workflow embedding, and corporate distribution matter more as the first ChatGPT shock matures.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Non-tech enterprises and corporate venture arms may shape AI commercialization as much as model startups.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Back vertical copilots and infrastructure that combine proprietary workflow data, compute leverage, and distribution through incumbents or corporate partners.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source is set 309 days after ChatGPT and explicitly asks what new Silicon Valley AI consensus had formed.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: AI value moves from model novelty toward workflow products, compute constraints, and ecosystem control.
evidenceSignals: The notes say a model is not a product and not a moat.

risks: Foundation-model providers can absorb application layers.

thesisTable.implication: Product-market fit and distribution deserve primary diligence.

episodes.titleZh: 39. 对凯文·凯利的50问：垄断者与国界、大富翁与人生
episodes.titleEn: 39. Fifty Questions for Kevin Kelly: Monopolists and Borders, Billionaires and Life
episodes.summaryEn: This interview asks Kevin Kelly 50 questions about future monopolists, platforms, network order, AI, national borders in technology, and life advice. The episode is organized into six themed sections, including his prediction that an AI company could become the next monopolist and his warning to young people not to seek a billion dollars.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
“凯文·凯利有一把标志性的络腮胡子。20岁出头，这把胡子就跟着他从美国远赴亚洲游历，他到过中国、日本、韩国，也到过印度、尼泊尔、阿富汗，他拿着摄像机四处拍摄；随后，这把胡子跟着他回了美国，安坐在自家漏雨的后院小棚里创作，花了5年写出《失控》，而后参与创立《连线》杂志；再之后，凯文·凯利意外在华走红，收获了名望与财富，他又带着这把大胡子一次又一次到访中国。”
这段文字是我写的一篇文章的开头，文章记录了最近对于凯文·凯利的访谈。
凯文·凯利是美国知名杂志《连线》创始主编，著有《失控》《科技想要什么》《必然》等书，曾在上世纪90年代预言了后来互联网的走向，因而他被认为是全球科技界的预言家。中国读者喜欢叫他KK。
最近，71岁的KK带着新书《宝贵的人生建议：我希望早点知道的智慧》再次来到中国。他的行程很紧凑，利用间隙，我们向KK提出了50个问题，范围涵盖寡头、平台、网络秩序、人工智能、科技的国界和人生之问。
访谈用英文进行，为了减轻收听英语的压力，我特地把播客分成了6个部分，每个部分有一个小主题。
你也可以在下方链接中找到中文的文字版本。
•  第一部分05:20 预言下一个寡头：我确信，一家人工智能公司会成为下一个垄断者
•  第二部分12:21 预言垄断者与新秩序：成功的故事总来自于外部
•  第三部分20:43 谈科技的国界：当中国最终造出世界级品牌，可能会是电动汽车
•  第四部分26:34 谈科技精英化与安全：把爱因斯坦和老虎关进一个笼子，谁能活？
•  第五部分36:06 答人生之问和亿万富翁的烦恼：我劝年轻人尽一切努力，不要拥有10亿美元
•  第六部分54:18 盲盒问答：回答几位中国读者、粉丝的提问

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Kevin Kelly has a signature beard. In his early 20s, that beard followed him from the United States across Asia, to China, Japan, South Korea, India, Nepal, and Afghanistan, while he carried a camera and filmed everywhere. Later it followed him back to the United States, where he sat in a leaky backyard shed for five years writing Out of Control and then helped found Wired magazine. After that, Kevin Kelly unexpectedly became popular in China and gained fame and wealth, returning again and again.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Outsider Monopolies and Chinese EV Brands
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The transcript excerpt and notes pair two predictions: an AI company could become the next monopolist, and China's first globally desired consumer brand may be an electric car. The hidden insight is that outsiders can reset monopoly structure when technology crosses borders unevenly.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The next Chinese global brand may emerge from electric vehicles rather than the consumer internet, while the next monopoly may emerge from AI rather than today's biggest platforms.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Track outsider AI platforms and Chinese EV brands that can escape incumbent gravity through brand, distribution, and cross-border product desirability.
alphaLens.whyNow: The interview was organized around post-ChatGPT monopoly questions, technology borders, and a prediction that world-class Chinese consumer branding could come through EVs.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Market power shifts from existing internet incumbents toward new AI and EV category creators.
evidenceSignals: The notes say Kelly is certain an AI company will become the next monopolist.
The transcript says successful stories always come from the outside.
Kelly predicts China's world-class branded consumer product may be an electric car.
The interview asks whether technology is bound by borders.
The notes include warnings about billionaires and life constraints.

episodes.titleZh: 38. 和软银愿景原GP聊聊，大模型全球降温后，人工智能何去何从
episodes.titleEn: 38. With a Former SoftBank Vision Fund GP: Where Does AI Go After the Global Large-Model Cooldown?
episodes.summaryEn: Former SoftBank Vision Fund managing partner Chen Xun frames the large-model cooldown through the lens of technology cycles, arguing that cooling after frenzy can be the best time to build. He discusses AI 2.0 and 3.0, machine intelligence beyond language models, cost-structure changes, model ecosystems, inference scale-up, and his combined investor-founder experience.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
大家可能也注意到，大模型经过上半年的狂欢后，在全球迎来了降温时刻。我们应该如何看待这样的温差呢？本集我邀请陈恂先生来聊聊。
我之前采访过陈恂，他当时反复聊到一个词：技术周期。他认为，人类发展大致有三个周期——经济周期、技术周期和意识形态周期——这里头最容易被忽视的是技术周期。
技术周期相对更特殊。意识形态和经济周期是钟摆性质的，是起伏的；技术周期不同，它是一路往上的，是单向的，浮动的只是进步速度。而这三个周期互相纠缠，互相影响。技术的大进步会推动经济的大发展，但爆炸性发展之后，意识形态和经济往往会出现回调、重整、摇摆。
这次，我们就让他聊聊人工智能时代的技术周期轨迹。从他的经验看，大模型现在这种温度骤降的时刻，恰恰是到了重要且极佳的做事情的时候。
除了这些之外，因为陈恂职业生涯很丰富，不仅创过两次业，而且先后帮银湖和软银愿景组建了中国office，主导投资业务。我们也聊了聊他的事业、选择，以及投资+创业的双重视角，希望能对大家有一些启发。
【嘉宾小记】
青少年时期，陈恂曾代表中国参加物理国际奥林匹克，获银牌。而后求学于北大物理系和斯坦福大学，获电子工程博士学位。
职业生涯早期，陈恂在位于瑞士日内瓦的国际联合研究机构欧洲核子中心从事理论物理研究工作。
此后，他是摩根大通的高级副总裁，负责协调在全球范围内的电子行业的投资和研究工作。
2002年陈恂第一次创业，在硅谷创立睿初科技，2007年该公司被欧洲光刻机巨头阿斯麦并购，他出任阿斯麦高级副总裁和全球执行委员会成员。
这之后他成为职业中后期投资人，2008年以董事总经理的身份加入银湖投资，创建了位于香港、东京和上海的亚洲办公室和亚洲投资及管理团队，负责银湖的亚洲投资业务；2018年他出任软银愿景基金的管理合伙人，负责软银在中国的投资业务。
2016年他联合创立了另一家公司翼方健数，担任联合创始人和董事长，并于2022年孵化了AI应用平台和生态揽睿星舟。
