•  25:13 OpenAI是否会一骑绝尘，取决于能否将技术优势转化成商业壁垒
•  30
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: You may have noticed that after the large-model carnival in the first half of the year, the world has entered a cooling moment. How should we understand this temperature difference? In this episode, I invite Chen Xun to discuss it. I interviewed him before, and he repeatedly returned to one phrase: technology cycles. He believes human development roughly has three cycles: economic cycles, technology cycles, and ideological cycles. The most easily ignored among them is the technology cycle, because it keeps moving upward even when speed changes.

outline.textZh: 个人的创业和投资生涯
outline.textEn: Personal career in entrepreneurship and investing

outline.textZh: 机器智能不会止步语言大模型，现在刚刚开始
outline.textEn: Machine intelligence will not stop at language models; it has only just begun

outline.textZh: 机器智能现在的学习像牛的反刍过程，未来会从世界直接观察和学习
outline.textEn: Machine intelligence now learns like cattle ruminating, but will learn directly from observing the world in the future

outline.textZh: 我在软银愿景一期基金投资的是AI 2.0，面临最大挑战是什么？
outline.textEn: The biggest challenge in the AI 2.0 companies I invested in at SoftBank Vision Fund I

outline.textZh: 2.0出现的优秀企业共性是，找到了单一可放大的市场
outline.textEn: Excellent 2.0 companies shared one trait: they found a single scalable market

outline.textZh: 大模型带来了AI 3.0，最大变化是把可变成本变成固定成本
outline.textEn: Large models brought AI 3.0, whose biggest change is turning variable costs into fixed costs

outline.textZh: 未来模型的世界：全民AI时代、基础大模型、衍生大模型
outline.textEn: The future model world: all-people AI, foundation models, and derivative models

outline.textZh: OpenAI是否会一骑绝尘，取决于能否将技术优势转化成商业壁垒
outline.textEn: Whether OpenAI can run far ahead depends on whether it can turn technical advantage into a business moat

outline.textZh: 怎么看大模型在全球的降温现象？
outline.textEn: How to view the global cooldown in large models

outline.textZh: 从骤然狂热到骤然降温，在历史技术周期中的映照
outline.textEn: From sudden frenzy to sudden cooling, reflected in historical technology cycles

outline.textZh: 狂热后冷静是最重要的时刻，坚持的人能走到最后
outline.textEn: The cooling after frenzy is the most important moment; those who persist can reach the end

outline.textZh: 我们还没完成从模型训练和微调到大量推理和上量的过渡，这是未来三年最值得期待的
outline.textEn: We have not completed the transition from model training and fine-tuning to massive inference and scaling; this is the most promising area of the next three years

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Build During the Model Cooldown
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes frame large-model cooling as part of a technology cycle rather than a dead end. The hidden alpha is that the best builders use cooling periods to find scalable markets and convert model cost structure into a business advantage.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The cooldown after AI frenzy can be a superior entry point for machine-intelligence companies with real markets.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Back AI companies beyond language-only models that prove one scalable market, inference economics, and a path from technical advantage to business moat.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source says the world entered a cooling moment after the first-half large-model carnival and asks how to understand the temperature change.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: AI analysis moves from hype temperature to cycle positioning, inference cost, and model-ecosystem structure.
evidenceSignals: Chen Xun repeatedly frames the issue through technology cycles.
The source says machine intelligence will not stop at language models.
It says excellent AI 2.0 companies found a single scalable market.
It describes AI 3.0 as changing cost structures.
It asks whether OpenAI can turn technical advantage into business moat.
watchlist: Inference cost per task
Evidence of one scalable end market
Non-language model applications
Derivative model ecosystems
Cooling-period financing discipline
risks: Cooling can become a funding drought.
Technical advantage may fail to become a moat.
Model ecosystems can consolidate around a few platforms.

thesisTable.evidence: The source frames frenzy and cooling as normal technology-cycle behavior.
thesisTable.implication: Investor discipline improves after hype breaks.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes say strong AI 2.0 companies found a single scalable market.
thesisTable.implication: Horizontal demos are less valuable than focused demand.

episodes.titleZh: 37. 小冰李笛讲点真心话：只做大模型不追求商业模式是很难生存的
episodes.titleEn: 37. Xiaoice CEO Li Di Speaks Frankly: It Is Hard to Survive by Building Only Large Models Without a Business Model
episodes.summaryEn: Xiaoice CEO Li Di explains why he stayed quiet during the large-model frenzy and offers skeptical views on model size, business models, and China's AI misunderstandings. The episode links Xiaoice's Microsoft origins and emotional-AI history to current debates over OpenAI envy, commercialization, and the desire for Xiaoice to survive long term.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
小冰是2014年由微软推出的一款对话式人工智能产品，是中国最早的对话式AI之一，于2020年独立分拆。但在今年上半年人们对大模型展现出万众痴迷之时，小冰CEO李笛保持着缄默。
考虑到这家公司和OpenAI的关系——同样是做对话式人工智能，也同样属于微软系——这种缄默不免令人感到几分奇怪。
今年8月，我和李笛聊了聊，他对于大模型的许多观点听上去有点反叛。他说，上半年很难与他人交流这些——对狂热者说，“无异于对宗教狂热分子去说一点理性的话”；对冷静者说，“好像是一群loser”。因此他选择沉默和苟活。
【嘉宾小记】
2013年，李笛在微软原全球执行副总裁陆奇的邀请下加入微软，此前是微软（亚洲）互联网工程院副院长，也是微软搜索引擎Bing（亚洲）总经理。
•  02:08 加入微软始末，陆奇对我说的第一句话：我们太需要在中国了
•  10:53 如果没有我们的坚持和情怀，小冰和Bing在中国都不会存在

•  23:59 大、中、小模型的范围界定，模型绝不是越大越好

•  29:50 中国市场对大模型的几个误读

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Xiaoice is a conversational AI product launched by Microsoft in 2014, one of China's earliest conversational AIs, and it was spun off independently in 2020. But in the first half of this year, when people showed mass fascination with large models, Xiaoice CEO Li Di remained silent. Considering the company's relationship with OpenAI - both work on conversational AI and both belong to the Microsoft system - this silence seemed somewhat strange. In August, I spoke with Li Di, and many of his views on large models sounded a bit rebellious.

outline.textZh: 加入微软始末，陆奇对我说的第一句话：我们太需要在中国了
outline.textEn: How I joined Microsoft, and the first thing Qi Lu said to me: we badly need to be in China

outline.textZh: 如果没有我们的坚持和情怀，小冰和Bing在中国都不会存在
outline.textEn: Without our persistence and feeling, Xiaoice and Bing would not exist in China

outline.textZh: 小冰是怎么在微软起步的？为什么做情感？
outline.textEn: How Xiaoice started inside Microsoft, and why it focused on emotion

outline.textZh: 大模型的商业模式问题：大家不要抱有幻想
outline.textEn: The business-model problem of large models: do not hold illusions

outline.textZh: 大、中、小模型的范围界定，模型绝不是越大越好
outline.textEn: Defining large, medium, and small models; bigger is absolutely not always better

outline.textZh: 只做大模型不追求商业模式是很难生存的
outline.textEn: It is hard to survive by building only large models without pursuing a business model

outline.textZh: 中国市场对大模型的几个误读
outline.textEn: Several misunderstandings about large models in the Chinese market

outline.textZh: 国内大模型们目前普遍处于的阶段
outline.textEn: The stage most domestic large-model companies are currently in

outline.textZh: 用大模型来进行竞争：是行业第二名的攻击性武器
outline.textEn: Using large models to compete: an offensive weapon for the industry's second-place player

outline.textZh: 企业发论文没什么帮助
outline.textEn: Publishing papers does not help companies much

outline.textZh: 会羡慕、嫉妒OpenAI吗？我们是很怂的
outline.textEn: Do we envy OpenAI? We are timid

outline.textZh: 做小冰的过程中，对于人、人性和社会的更多认知
outline.textEn: Further understanding of people, human nature, and society from building Xiaoice

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Model Size Is Not a Business Model
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes present Xiaoice's Li Di as skeptical precisely when others were fascinated by large models. The hidden alpha is that commercial survival may favor right-sized models with emotional or vertical fit over parameter-count contests.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: In China AI, restraint about model size and papers may be more investable than joining the foundation-model arms race.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor AI companies with explicit revenue paths, cost-aware model sizing, defensible use cases, and distribution from existing user relationships.
alphaLens.whyNow: The episode comes after the large-model frenzy and explains why building only large models without a business model is hard to survive.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: AI competition shifts from research prestige and model scale to cost-fit commercialization.
evidenceSignals: Xiaoice began inside Microsoft and was spun off in 2020.
The source says Xiaoice focused on emotion.
Li Di argues bigger is not always better.
The episode centers on the business-model problem of large models.
The notes say publishing papers does not help companies much.
watchlist: Paid user retention by use case
Model size versus serving cost
Second-place incumbents using models offensively
China-market AI misunderstandings
Revenue from emotional or conversational AI products
risks: Smaller-model strategies can underperform frontier capabilities.
Large platforms may bundle away vertical revenue.
Commercial discipline may be mistaken for lack of ambition.

thesisTable.evidence: The source says bigger is not always better and model-only companies struggle.
thesisTable.implication: Unit economics should lead AI diligence.

thesisTable.evidence: Li Di stayed quiet during the frenzy and offered skeptical views.
thesisTable.implication: Skeptics may own better AI business models.

episodes.titleZh: 36.【做个生意吧！】跳向后海的年轻人、酒精和下沉年代
episodes.titleEn: 36. Let's Do a Business: Young People Jumping Into Houhai, Alcohol, and an Era of Sinking
episodes.summaryEn: This conversation with Ergou, founder of Tiaohai Bar, follows a post-90s founder through failed ventures, pandemic-era survival, and the accidental rise of a hutong bar into a recognizable business. The episode frames Tiaohai as a place for people standing on the reverse side of mainstream narratives, using alcohol, music, and community to build their own order.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
这是和跳海酒馆创始人二狗的聊天。伴随过去三年疫情，这个从一开始挣扎在北京胡同里的、没有任何商业目的的小酒馆突然火了。
创始人出生于1993年，见证了90前的这波人从走出校园到而立之年的时代幕布——90后创业项目ofo从癫狂走向幻灭，自己的创业项目账面卖了2个亿，但世界急转直下，一分钱也没拿到。自己还是个穷光蛋。
他开始对世界充满了怀疑、追问，在这种强烈情绪的驱使下，他和朋友一起跳向后海。没想到误打误撞，从时代缝隙中找到一条生路。他说跳海代表的就是这样一帮“站在背面”的人。
•  5段创业经历与90后眼中令人唏嘘的世界
•  见过太多“眼看他起高楼，眼看他楼塌了”的故事，要把跳海变成赚钱的项目
•  2018年，我经历的25岁危机
•  2019年，26岁对世界充满了追问，不断跳后海的那个夏天
•  内心有很大情感汹涌，不知道自己能干嘛，只能喝酒、唱歌、打鼓、跳后海——不只是我，整个年代开始下沉了，年轻人的social ladder（社会阶梯）越来越模糊
•  我是谁？我们是谁？创业项目卖了2个亿，但我还是个穷光蛋
•  网红酒馆的两个独特机制：礼宾部和打酒师机制
•  跳海在这三年活下来挺不容易的，账户见底
•  我们这帮人，怀疑现存秩序、想建立自己的秩序
•  一个爱哭的CEO，偶像是王微和张向东，他们已经不在今天主流互联网名单上了
•  2020年，决定让三个合伙人走，在后海一边打鼓一边哭
•  文艺青年算商业账
•  不同城市的酒文化和年轻人
•  怎么看待关于酒贵、没服务的评价？
•  我们的立场是“站在背面”，永远站在阳光照不到的地方呼唤阳光，永远站在宏大叙事的背面对话主流

联络我们：微博@张小珺-Benita (https://weibo.com/u/6486678714) ，小红书@张小珺jùn (https://www.xiaohongshu.com/user/profile/5fede947000000000100603e?xhsshare=CopyLink&appuid=5fede947000000000100603e&apptime=1710049463)
更多信息欢迎关注公众号：张小珺
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: This is a conversation with Ergou, founder of Tiaohai Bar. During the past three years of the pandemic, this small bar, which began in a Beijing hutong with no commercial purpose, suddenly became popular. The founder was born in 1993 and witnessed the curtain of the times for pre-1990s people as they moved from campus into adulthood: the post-90s startup ofo went from frenzy to collapse, and his own startup was sold on paper for 200 million yuan, but when the world turned sharply, he did not receive a cent.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Belonging as a Local Brand Moat
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes describe Tiaohai Bar as a pandemic-era community that began without commercial purpose and became recognizable by serving people outside mainstream narratives. The hidden alpha is that ritual, music, alcohol, and founder story can create a belonging moat in small-format hospitality.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Some local experiential businesses can scale as cultural brands, not just as location arbitrage.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Back niche hospitality and community brands with authentic founder narratives, event cadence, repeat visits, and replication discipline.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source ties Tiaohai's rise to the pandemic years and a younger founder cohort shaped by failed startup wealth narratives.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Consumption shifts from status display toward identity, community, and emotional shelter.
evidenceSignals: The bar began in a Beijing hutong with no commercial purpose.
It became popular during the three pandemic years.
The founder was born in 1993.
The source links the story to ofo's boom and collapse.
The notes frame Tiaohai as a place for people on the reverse side of mainstream narratives.
watchlist: Repeat visit rates
Event and music programming cadence
Replication beyond the original hutong setting
Founder dependency
Per-store margin and alcohol regulation
risks: Authenticity can dilute with expansion.
Hospitality margins are structurally fragile.
Local cultural heat can fade quickly.

thesisTable.evidence: The source says the bar began with no commercial purpose.
thesisTable.implication: Authenticity can lower customer-acquisition cost.

thesisTable.evidence: The bar became popular during the pandemic years.
thesisTable.implication: Stress periods can incubate durable offline brands.

episodes.titleZh: 34. 被忽视的中腰部公司活得还好吗？他们的欲望、挣扎与天命
episodes.titleEn: 34. How Are Overlooked Mid-Tier Companies Living? Their Desires, Struggles, and Fate
episodes.summaryEn: Former Vipshop and TAL strategy executive Wu Shanshan examines mid-tier companies that are often ignored while attention goes to giants and stars. Drawing on Vipshop and TAL, he discusses secondary markets, growth bottlenecks, founder psychology, diversification, culture, resource endowments, and why many mid-tier CEOs resist their apparent fate.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
中腰部公司占据了商业世界很大比例，但我们总习惯性把聚光灯对准巨头明星们，而忽视了这个群体。
中腰部公司活得还好吗？我邀请了先后在两家非常典型的中腰部企业（唯品会、好未来）担任首席战略官的吴杉杉来聊聊这个话题。除了做企业高管，他也在咨询公司有十多年从业经历。
他分享了许多只有内部视角才知道的，中腰部企业真实的赛道瓶颈、创始人看不见的欲望和挣扎，以及他们终究难以抵抗的共同天命。
【嘉宾小记】
吴杉杉，物理学本科和博士毕业后，一脚迈进咨询行业，历任麦肯锡咨询经理、罗兰贝格咨询总监，而后加入唯品会和好未来出任CSO（首席战略官），现在是某全球咨询公司董事总经理，负责高科技互联网业务

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Mid-tier companies occupy a large share of the business world, but we habitually point the spotlight at giant stars and ignore this group. Are mid-tier companies living well? I invited Wu Shanshan, who served as chief strategy officer at two very typical mid-tier companies, Vipshop and TAL, to talk about this topic. Besides being a corporate executive, he also spent more than ten years in consulting. He shares many real track bottlenecks, founder desires and struggles that only an internal perspective can reveal, and their shared fate that is ultimately hard to resist.

outline.textEn: Common traits and fate of mid-tier company CEOs

alphaLens.alphaTitle: The Mid-Tier Fate Discount
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes argue that mid-tier companies are ignored because attention goes to giants and stars, yet their bottlenecks are common across the business world. The hidden alpha is to separate durable niche cash machines from founders trying to escape their natural ceiling.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Accepting a mid-tier destiny can create more value than forcing a giant-company narrative.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Underwrite mid-tier companies through resource endowment, founder psychology, niche defensibility, and cash-return discipline rather than headline growth alone.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source says mid-tier companies occupy a large share of business but are habitually overlooked.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Capital allocation shifts from growth maximalism to realistic track selection and niche durability.
evidenceSignals: The source draws on Vipshop and TAL internal strategy experience.
It discusses secondary markets and growth bottlenecks.
Vipshop is framed as two Wenzhou bosses quietly making money.
TAL is discussed through a silent CEO and legion culture.
The notes emphasize common traits and fate of mid-tier CEOs.
watchlist: Return on invested capital
Founder diversification attempts
Cash return versus reinvestment
Niche customer retention
Succession after founder ambition peaks
risks: Ceilings can limit valuation upside.
Founder ambition can destroy niche economics.
Public markets may keep applying a liquidity discount.

thesisTable.evidence: The source says mid-tier companies occupy a large share of business.
thesisTable.implication: Niche durability can be investable.

thesisTable.claim: Founder psychology is a risk factor.
thesisTable.evidence: The notes focus on founder desires, struggles, and fate.
thesisTable.implication: Diligence should include ambition-path alignment.

episodes.titleZh: 33.【职场人性】企业内斗，究竟在斗什么？
episodes.titleEn: 33. [Workplace Human Nature] What Is Corporate Infighting Really About?
episodes.summaryEn: Returning guest Colin joins the second episode of the Workplace Human Nature series to examine organizational behavior and internal corporate conflict. The conversation distinguishes invisible internal friction from visible infighting, then traces how market pressure, silos, middle management, and executive politics can turn companies inward.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
《商业访谈录》的老朋友Colin，返场啦！
今天推出【职场人性】第二集，探讨的是组织行为学——企业内斗，究竟在斗什么。
【嘉宾小记】
英文名Colin，毕业于复旦大学，职业生涯很长很长，从大学生创业计划“挑战杯”金奖到宝洁到GE通用电气到BCG波士顿咨询到百度公司副总裁再到美的总裁办总监，现在是爱博医疗的持股公司爱博星辰CEO。

•  27:39 请你具备一种能力：组织商
•  28:33 中间层是内斗主发地
•  31:24 高层内斗更高级、辐射范围广，演化成办公室政治
•  33:19 高层怎么看基层吃自己的瓜
•  41:20 旷日持久的被包装的争斗
•  45:50 市场越萎缩，内斗越高发
•  49:30 哪些岗位内斗更易发
•  50:07 个体如何保护自己不陷入泥潭
•  53:12 如何看待企业内“谁是谁的人”——你很可能猜错了
•  01:00:56 高处不胜寒的CEO、掌握权谋之术的CEO
•  01:04:13 一些感触和建议

更多信息欢迎关注公众号：张小珺
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Old friend of Business Interview Colin returns for the second episode of Workplace Human Nature, this time discussing organizational behavior: what exactly are companies fighting over internally? Colin graduated from Fudan University and has had a long career, from winning the Challenge Cup entrepreneurship gold award to P&G, GE, BCG, vice president at Baidu, director in Midea's president office, and now CEO of Aibo Star, the holding company of Eyebright Medical. The episode compares invisible internal friction with visible infighting and asks why organizations sometimes fight simply for the sake of fighting.

outline.textZh: 无形的内耗 vs 有形的内斗
outline.textEn: Invisible internal friction vs. visible infighting

outline.textZh: 什么导致了内斗？为什么为斗而斗？
outline.textEn: What causes infighting, and why do people fight for its own sake?

outline.textZh: 隐形的内耗更恶劣，无异于集体慢性自杀
outline.textEn: Hidden internal friction is worse, like collective slow suicide

outline.textZh: 那些保护伞、老白兔
outline.textEn: Protective umbrellas and the old white rabbits

outline.textZh: 一定程度的内斗被默许甚至被鼓励
outline.textEn: A certain amount of infighting is tacitly allowed or even encouraged

outline.textZh: 当企业内“烟囱林立”
outline.textEn: When corporate chimneys and silos stand everywhere

outline.textZh: 内斗是职场人的必选项么？
outline.textEn: Is infighting unavoidable for working people?

outline.textZh: 基层没机会斗，中层高频地斗，高层低频但高强度地斗
outline.textEn: Frontline staff have little chance to fight; middle managers fight often; top executives fight less often but with higher intensity

outline.textZh: 请你具备一种能力：组织商
outline.textEn: Build one capability: organizational intelligence

outline.textZh: 中间层是内斗主发地
outline.textEn: The middle layer is where infighting most often happens

outline.textZh: 高层内斗更高级、辐射范围广，演化成办公室政治
outline.textEn: Executive infighting is more sophisticated, wider in impact, and evolves into office politics

outline.textZh: 高层怎么看基层吃自己的瓜
outline.textEn: How top leaders view rank-and-file employees following gossip about them

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Internal Friction as Leading Indicator
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes distinguish visible infighting from invisible internal friction, calling the latter closer to collective slow suicide. The hidden alpha is that middle-layer conflict can be an early warning before financial results reveal organizational decay.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Some visible conflict is healthier than silent friction because it at least exposes the coordination failure.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor companies with strong organizational intelligence: clear cross-silo incentives, fast decision cycles, and middle-management accountability.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source links infighting to market pressure, silos, middle management, and executive politics, all of which intensify when growth slows.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Organizational quality becomes a measurable downside-protection factor rather than a soft culture topic.
evidenceSignals: The episode begins with invisible friction versus visible infighting.
The notes call hidden internal friction collective slow suicide.
It discusses corporate chimneys and silos.
The source says middle managers fight often.
It recommends building organizational intelligence.
watchlist: Decision cycle time
Cross-functional escalation frequency
Middle-manager churn
Siloed KPI conflicts
Protected incumbent teams
risks: External observers rarely see internal friction directly.
Some conflict can be productive and misread as dysfunction.
Founder narratives can conceal management drag.

thesisTable.evidence: The source compares it to collective slow suicide.
thesisTable.implication: Investors should seek process indicators, not only culture slogans.

thesisTable.claim: The middle layer is the battleground.
thesisTable.evidence: The notes say infighting most often happens among middle managers.
thesisTable.implication: Managerial span and incentives matter.

episodes.titleZh: 32. 第一个把查理·芒格引入中国的人和出版风云
episodes.titleEn: 32. The First Person to Bring Charlie Munger to China and the Publishing Story Behind It
episodes.summaryEn: Publisher Shi Hongjun recounts the books and publishing waves that shaped his career, including bringing Poor Charlie's Almanack to China when Munger was still little known there. The episode connects publishing history, value investing, Li Lu's books, The Tao of Charlie Munger, and how Munger's thinking changed Shi's own life path.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
你肯定听过这些耳熟能详的书名：《达·芬奇密码》《追风筝的人》《大秦帝国》《穷查理宝典》和李录的《文明、现代化、价值投资与中国》——本集嘉宾施宏俊正是这些风靡一时书籍背后的出版人。
施宏俊是把芒格最早引入中国的人。2009年他第一次见到芒格先生，把这本美国的咖啡书《穷查理宝典》带到中国。那时中国听过芒格这个名字者寥寥。往后的数十年芒格在华持续走红，其知名度甚至高于他在美国的名望。
再之后，施宏俊陆续出版了芒格中国学生李录的图书以及这本刚问世、集纳芒格所有股东会讲话的《芒格之道》。
而作为芒格的中国出版人，施宏俊本人也受到这位老先生思想的强烈启发，彻底改变了生活轨迹。
总之，这是一个人与书互为交错、相互影响的故事。
【嘉宾小记】
施宏俊曾任上海世纪出版集团副总裁、中信出版集团董事总经理，世纪文景、中信大方出版公司创办人，曾策划出版数百种图书，包括《达·芬奇密码》《追风筝的人》《大秦帝国》等现象级图书。
他也是《穷查理宝典》《芒格之道》《文明、现代化、价值投资与中国》的出版人。

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: You have probably heard titles such as The Da Vinci Code, The Kite Runner, The Qin Empire, Poor Charlie's Almanack, and Li Lu's Civilization, Modernization, Value Investment and China. Guest Shi Hongjun is the publisher behind many of these once-phenomenal books. He was the first to introduce Munger to China: in 2009, he met Mr. Munger for the first time and brought the American coffee-table book Poor Charlie's Almanack into the Chinese market. At that time, very few Chinese readers knew Munger's name.

outline.textZh: 出版人生涯和中国出版风云二十年
outline.textEn: A publisher's career and twenty turbulent years in Chinese publishing

outline.textZh: “那真是一个嘈杂的时代啊”
outline.textEn: "That really was a noisy era"

outline.textZh: 黄金十年就这样消逝了，人类缺乏一个抽象的书房
outline.textEn: The golden decade faded, and people lacked a room for abstract thought

outline.textZh: 咖啡书《穷查理宝典》入华始末（登场人物：李录、六六、译者李继宏）
outline.textEn: How the coffee-table book Poor Charlie's Almanack entered China, with Li Lu, Liu Liu, and translator Li Jihong

outline.textZh: 他在美国不算名人
outline.textEn: He was not considered a celebrity in America

outline.textZh: 第一次见面，芒格是不理人的
outline.textEn: At the first meeting, Munger did not pay attention to people

outline.textZh: 今年见芒格，问了他两小时问题
outline.textEn: Meeting Munger this year and asking him questions for two hours

outline.textZh: 芒格让我有勇气离开中信，重启人生
outline.textEn: Munger gave me the courage to leave CITIC and restart my life

outline.textZh: 芒格“富可敌国”的起点
outline.textEn: The starting point from which Munger became extraordinarily wealthy

outline.textZh: 《文明、现代化、价值投资与中国》背后的故事
outline.textEn: The story behind Civilization, Modernization, Value Investment and China

outline.textZh: 谈《芒格之道》和芒格的家庭、秘诀和思维模型
outline.textEn: Discussing The Tao of Charlie Munger, Munger's family, secrets, and mental models

outline.textZh: 向芒格学习交朋友
outline.textEn: Learning how to make friends from Munger

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Curated Thought as Long-Tail IP
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes frame the Chinese introduction of Munger as a publishing arbitrage: a thinker not yet seen as a celebrity became a durable intellectual asset. The hidden alpha is that trusted curators can turn scarce abstract thought into long-lived IP.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Serious publishing can compound even when content investment retreats, if the product is durable thought rather than transient traffic.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Back niche intellectual IP ecosystems with rights control, translation quality, community trust, and long-tail education or membership extensions.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source says the golden decade faded and people lacked room for abstract thought, making trusted curation scarcer.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Publishing shifts from hit-cycle volume to curator-led mental-model distribution.
evidenceSignals: Shi Hongjun brought Poor Charlie's Almanack into China in 2009.
The source says very few Chinese readers knew Munger then.
It connects Munger, Li Lu, value investing, and publishing waves.
The notes say the golden decade faded.
Shi says Munger gave him courage to restart his life.
watchlist: Backlist sales durability
Translation and rights ownership
Author-estate partnerships
Community courses or memberships
Demand for value-investing education
risks: Niche intellectual demand can be cyclical.
Rights concentration can limit scalability.
The source is personal and notes-derived.

thesisTable.evidence: The source says Munger was little known in China before the book introduction.
thesisTable.implication: Discovery and trust can be monetizable.

thesisTable.evidence: The episode connects Munger's thinking with long-term life and investing impact.
thesisTable.implication: Backlist IP can compound slowly.

episodes.titleZh: 31.【趋势2023】和老范从退潮的内容投资谈起
episodes.titleEn: 31. [Trends 2023] With Lao Fan, Starting From the Retreat of Content Investment
episodes.summaryEn: In this crossover with Lao Fan Talks Startups, Fan Weifeng revisits the content industry from the vantage point of a former media worker turned investor. He explains why he now invests less in pure content, why content may be a saddle or whip rather than the horse itself, and what separates creators from content entrepreneurs.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
这集是和播客《老范聊创业》的串台，我和高樟资本创始合伙人范卫锋重新聊聊内容产业。范卫锋曾也是一名媒体人，2001年起在《证券时报》社工作。而后，他转型投资人并于2015年创立高樟资本，投资了许多内容、流量和消费项目（包括饭统戴老板、星球研究所），到今天他投资的流量矩阵统计3亿。
但是，这位凭借着投内容建立起声名的投资人，如今几乎不投纯内容了，取而代之的是自己做内容。他说，内容是马鞍、是鞭子，而他要找的是千里马。2023年了，内容还会是好项目、好生意吗？它会适合哪些人呢？

联络我们：微博@张小珺-Benita (https://weibo.com/u/6486678714) ，小红书@张小珺jùn (https://www.xiaohongshu.com/user/profile/5fede9470000
episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: This episode is a crossover with the podcast Lao Fan Talks Startups. I speak again with Fan Weifeng, founding partner of Gaozhang Capital, about the content industry. Fan was once a media professional, working at Securities Times from 2001 before becoming an investor and founding Gaozhang Capital in 2015. He invested in many content, traffic, and consumer projects, including Fantong Da Boss and Star Atlas Institute, and his invested traffic matrix now totals 300 million. Yet this investor, known for content investments, now almost no longer invests in pure content.

outline.textZh: 最近和小马宋、林少、刘飞聊了聊，一些意外启发
outline.textEn: Recent conversations with Xiao Masong, Lin Shao, and Liu Fei, and some unexpected inspiration

outline.textZh: 从公众号生态发展轨迹看如今的播客生态
outline.textEn: Looking at today's podcast ecosystem through the development of WeChat public accounts

outline.textZh: 爆款能力绝对是天赋
outline.textEn: The ability to make hit content is absolutely a talent

outline.textZh: 我和咪蒙错过的故事
outline.textEn: The story of how I missed Mimon

outline.textZh: 好的内容创作者 vs 好的内容企业家
outline.textEn: Good content creators vs. good content entrepreneurs

outline.textZh: 播客的反流量逻辑 vs 抖音、哔哩哔哩
outline.textEn: The anti-traffic logic of podcasts vs. Douyin and Bilibili

outline.textZh: 踢场子的话：“播客生态没有真正的头部”
outline.textEn: A provocative line: the podcast ecosystem has no real top tier

outline.textZh: 2023年了，内容项目还是好标的吗？
outline.textEn: In 2023, are content projects still good investment targets?

outline.textZh: 内容是马鞍、是鞭子，而投资人在找千里马
outline.textEn: Content is the saddle and the whip, while investors are looking for the fine horse

outline.textZh: 投资人不投纯内容项目了，自己开始做内容
outline.textEn: Investors no longer invest in pure content projects and begin making content themselves

outline.textZh: 我有2个听友群，小马宋说他个人维护93个群
outline.textEn: I have two listener groups; Xiao Masong says he personally maintains ninety-three groups

outline.textZh: 内容成功率远大于绝大多数生意，但它不一定是巨大的生意
outline.textEn: Content succeeds more often than most businesses, but it may not become a huge business

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Content as Distribution, Not Product
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes say Fan Weifeng now invests less in pure content and treats content as saddle or whip rather than the horse. The hidden alpha is that content remains valuable when attached to a monetizable business, but weak when it is the entire business.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The retreat of content investment does not kill creator economics; it forces content to become a distribution layer.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Invest in businesses where content lowers customer-acquisition cost for commerce, software, education, or services, not pure content projects dependent on platform traffic.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source is explicitly framed around 2023 trends and the retreat of content investment.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: The creator economy moves from traffic arbitrage toward product-backed distribution and operator discipline.
evidenceSignals: Fan says he now almost no longer invests in pure content.
The source says his invested traffic matrix totals 300 million.
The episode distinguishes content creators from content entrepreneurs.
It describes the anti-traffic logic of podcasts versus Douyin and Bilibili.
The notes say the podcast ecosystem has no real top tier.
watchlist: Content-to-commerce conversion
Owned audience share
Platform dependency
Creator-operator team structure
Gross margin of products sold through content
risks: Platform algorithm changes can erase traffic.
Creators may resist operational discipline.
Content fatigue can reduce conversion quality.

thesisTable.evidence: The source says Fan almost no longer invests in pure content.
thesisTable.implication: Content must attach to revenue engines.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes distinguish good creators from good content entrepreneurs.
thesisTable.implication: Team composition is a key diligence item.

episodes.titleZh: 30.【职场人性】情商含量越高，公司越平庸？
episodes.titleEn: 30. [Workplace Human Nature] Does More Emotional Intelligence Make a Company More Mediocre?
episodes.summaryEn: Former Longfor chief strategy officer Wang Yajun discusses emotional intelligence as both a personal workplace tool and a potential organizational cost. The episode asks whether high EQ helps promotion, why large companies produce more EQ-driven behavior, and why executives treating EQ as their main tool can be a warning sign.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
在日常工作中你有没有一些感知——随着组织扩大，人的画像不仅变了，而且对于人的情商和在组织里生存能力的要求也大幅提升。
那么，情商是晋级的通行证吗？职业经理人真的是情商越高越好吗？
本集我邀请龙湖前首席战略官王亚军，来聊聊情商这个矛盾又有意思的话题。他分享了非常精彩的观点。很有意思的一点是，他尤其讨厌“职场”这个词，特别是当高管也开始谈职场，这可是危险信号。
【嘉宾小记】
王亚军是境门管理创始人，曾经是龙湖集团首席战略官、宝龙地产执行副总裁、麦肯锡大中华区战略与组织转型业务创始团队成员。出版有《企业即算法》一书，对如何建设大规模敏捷组织有长期实践和研究。
•  02:07 了解嘉宾：清华-麦肯锡-龙湖-宝龙-咨询和投资
•  03:17 “高管都是人精”、“管理都是管人性”，这些是偏见还是事实
•  04:05 当我们谈论情商的时候，在谈什么
•  07:23 极端来说，情绪价值最高的是太监
•  08:40 搭建敏捷组织，要容忍低情商的人
•  12:36 为什么公司越大富有情商的人越多？
•  13:55 情商对个人是低成本解决问题的方法，对组织却是高昂的
•  14:18 情商之于不同职业阶梯上的人：基层、中层、高层
•  17:19 当高管都是玩情商的人 vs 当高管都是低情商的人
•  （提到的人物：苹果乔布斯、中华人民共和国开国大将粟裕）
•  22:06 情商含量与公司发展阶段、市场竞争格局有巨大关系
•  22:36 当有外部危险时，低情商的人更有生存空间
•  25:20 我不喜欢“职场”这个词，意味有边界的游戏
•  27:34 作为顶层设计者，如何塑造更好的“场”
•  31:06 高管不能把情商当作解决问题的主要工具
•  31:59 人类行为的四种动机：性、恐惧、爱、信念
•  34:26 中层难干，应该怎么干？
•  35:22 情商低的人，业务能力强？
•  37:37 情商含量越高，公司越平庸？
•  39:07 当“看破不说破”是生存必要手段，很危险
•  39:47 一些潜台词和隐晦的语言信号
•  42:17 满满现实感的基层 vs 缺乏现实感的高层
•  45:10 内行不看组织架构，坦率说架构怎么拼都行
•  54:14 我们做的不是战略、不是组织，做的是企业——组织难道能单独上市？
•  56:07 从麦肯锡到地产公司开始喝酒了
•  57:34 在企业里，不要告诉我你著作等身
•  59:33 擅长在大公司生存的人来了，瓶颈也来了
•  01:00:15 智商、情商双高的第三类人，就做CEO了

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: In everyday work, have you sensed that as organizations expand, people's profiles change and the requirements for emotional intelligence and survival ability inside the organization rise sharply? Is EQ a passport to promotion? Are professional managers always better when their EQ is higher? This episode invites Wang Yajun, former chief strategy officer of Longfor, to discuss this contradictory and interesting topic. One especially interesting point is that he dislikes the word "workplace," particularly when executives start using it, because that can be a dangerous signal.

outline.textZh: 了解嘉宾：清华-麦肯锡-龙湖-宝龙-咨询和投资
outline.textEn: Getting to know the guest: Tsinghua, McKinsey, Longfor, Powerlong, consulting, and investment

outline.textZh: “高管都是人精”、“管理都是管人性”，这些是偏见还是事实
outline.textEn: "Executives are all shrewd" and "management is about human nature": bias or fact?

outline.textZh: 当我们谈论情商的时候，在谈什么
outline.textEn: What are we talking about when we talk about emotional intelligence?

outline.textZh: 极端来说，情绪价值最高的是太监
outline.textEn: At the extreme, eunuchs provide the highest emotional value

outline.textZh: 搭建敏捷组织，要容忍低情商的人
outline.textEn: To build an agile organization, tolerate people with low EQ

outline.textZh: 为什么公司越大富有情商的人越多？
outline.textEn: Why do larger companies have more emotionally intelligent people?

outline.textZh: 情商对个人是低成本解决问题的方法，对组织却是高昂的
outline.textEn: EQ is a low-cost way for individuals to solve problems but an expensive one for organizations

outline.textZh: 情商之于不同职业阶梯上的人：基层、中层、高层
outline.textEn: EQ at different career levels: frontline, middle, and senior roles

outline.textZh: 当高管都是玩情商的人 vs 当高管都是低情商的人
outline.textEn: When executives all play the EQ game vs. when executives all have low EQ

outline.textZh: 情商含量与公司发展阶段、市场竞争格局有巨大关系
outline.textEn: EQ content is closely tied to company stage and market competition

outline.textZh: 当有外部危险时，低情商的人更有生存空间
outline.textEn: When external danger exists, low-EQ people have more room to survive

outline.textZh: 我不喜欢“职场”这个词，意味有边界的游戏
outline.textEn: I dislike the word "workplace" because it implies a bounded game

alphaLens.alphaTitle: The EQ Tax on Agility
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes argue that emotional intelligence can be cheap for an individual and expensive for an organization. The hidden alpha is to spot when executive EQ games replace candor, speed, and technical judgment.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: A high-EQ executive culture can be a warning sign rather than a mark of maturity.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor companies that tolerate low-EQ high performers, preserve direct feedback, and prevent political emotional labor from becoming the promotion path.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source says as organizations expand, EQ and survival ability requirements rise sharply inside companies.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Culture alpha shifts from pleasant coordination to productive conflict and high-resolution decision-making.
evidenceSignals: The episode asks whether high EQ helps promotion.
It says larger companies produce more emotionally intelligent behavior.
The notes say EQ is low-cost for individuals but expensive for organizations.
The source says agile organizations should tolerate people with low EQ.
It warns when executives start using workplace EQ as a main tool.
watchlist: Promotion narratives emphasizing harmony over results
Retention of blunt high performers
Meeting candor and decision speed
Political language in executive communications
Market-pressure moments that reveal culture
risks: Low EQ can become customer or employee harm.
Candor and toxicity are easy to confuse.
The notes are qualitative and hard to measure externally.

thesisTable.evidence: The source says EQ is cheap for individuals but expensive for organizations.
thesisTable.implication: Culture diligence should test truth flow.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes say agile organizations should tolerate low-EQ people.
thesisTable.implication: Over-smoothing behavior can reduce speed.

episodes.titleZh: 29. 逆全球化思潮来势汹汹，外企CEO何故密集访华？
episodes.titleEn: 29. As Deglobalization Surges, Why Are Foreign CEOs Visiting China So Intensively?
episodes.summaryEn: This business news commentary starts from Elon Musk's 44-hour China visit and expands to the wave of foreign CEOs visiting China in the first half of the year. Guest Wang Wei interprets the political and commercial signals of these visits as a form of reconnection amid deglobalization pressure.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
这是一集商业新闻评述，我们从马斯克44小时闪电访华谈起，聊聊今年上半年的外企CEO来华潮，我们该如何看待这股访问热潮与当今世界形势所呈现的反差？它的政商含义是什么？释放了哪些新信号？
本集嘉宾是对宏观经济有长期观察的王威，目前他是某世界500强企业副总裁和董事会秘书。某种意义上，这次是逆全球化思潮下的一次重新连结。
【嘉宾小记】
王威的职业生涯起步于四大；2009-2017年先后在中化集团负责投资并购和资本运营，参与并主导多起大型海外投资并购案；而后出任A股上市公司梅花生物CFO；现在是某世界500强企业副总裁和董事会秘书。
•  00:11 嘉宾的自我介绍
•  00:41 马斯克这次访华的行程、目的和唯一的特别之处
•  03:00 为什么马斯克不支持民主党而高调表态支持共和党
•  04:53 戏剧的一幕：在华44小时，身价暴涨340亿，重回世界首富的宝座
•  06:09 和马斯克上次来华相比，整个世界已大不一样
•  10:54 全球割裂局势下，商界代表访问意味着什么？
•  12:21 美概股会成为现实吗？
•  16:53 商界大佬海外访问的一系列细节（握手/合影/议题）都有脚本
•  20:14 当马斯克和宁德时代曾毓群照片不经意地流出……
•  21:51 会面时的心理战和信号释放
•  23:43 上半年密集的外企CEO访华潮的长长一串不完全名单
•  27:35 这些外企来华CEO分属全球的几股不同势力
•  30:17 海外访问团和中国访问团有截然不同的意义
•  32:21 访华潮对中国经济有刺激作用吗？
•  34:58 对逆全球化思潮的一次重新连接
【更多信息】

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: This is a business news commentary episode. Starting with Musk's 44-hour lightning visit to China, we discuss the wave of foreign CEOs visiting China in the first half of the year and how to understand the contrast between this visiting boom and today's world situation. What political and business meanings does it carry, and what new signals does it release? The guest is Wang Wei, a long-time observer of macroeconomics who is currently vice president and board secretary of a Fortune 500 company. In a sense, this is a reconnection under the tide of deglobalization.

outline.textZh: 嘉宾的自我介绍
outline.textEn: The guest introduces himself

outline.textZh: 马斯克这次访华的行程、目的和唯一的特别之处
outline.textEn: Musk's China itinerary, purpose, and its one special feature

outline.textZh: 为什么马斯克不支持民主党而高调表态支持共和党
outline.textEn: Why Musk does not support the Democratic Party and publicly backs the Republican Party

outline.textZh: 戏剧的一幕：在华44小时，身价暴涨340亿，重回世界首富的宝座
outline.textEn: A dramatic moment: after 44 hours in China, his net worth rose by 34 billion and he returned to being the world's richest person

outline.textZh: 和马斯克上次来华相比，整个世界已大不一样
outline.textEn: Compared with Musk's last visit to China, the whole world is now very different

outline.textZh: 全球割裂局势下，商界代表访问意味着什么？
outline.textEn: What do business leaders' visits mean in a fragmented global situation?

outline.textZh: 美概股会成为现实吗？
outline.textEn: Will American-concept stocks become a reality?

outline.textZh: 商界大佬海外访问的一系列细节（握手/合影/议题）都有脚本
outline.textEn: The details of business leaders' overseas visits, from handshakes to photos and topics, are scripted

outline.textZh: 当马斯克和宁德时代曾毓群照片不经意地流出……
outline.textEn: When a photo of Musk and CATL's Robin Zeng appears to leak unintentionally

outline.textZh: 会面时的心理战和信号释放
outline.textEn: Psychological maneuvering and signal-sending during meetings

outline.textZh: 上半年密集的外企CEO访华潮的长长一串不完全名单
outline.textEn: A long, incomplete list of foreign CEOs visiting China intensively in the first half

outline.textZh: 这些外企来华CEO分属全球的几股不同势力
outline.textEn: These foreign CEOs in China belong to several different global forces

alphaLens.alphaTitle: CEO Visits as Business Diplomacy
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes treat foreign CEOs' China visits as scripted signals under deglobalization, not ordinary travel. The hidden alpha is that board-level physical presence can preserve options when supply chains and politics fragment.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: In a fragmented world, symbolic executive visits can have real commercial option value even before contracts are announced.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor multinationals and suppliers whose leaders maintain China access, local partner signaling, and supply-chain optionality without ignoring political backlash.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source starts with Musk's 44-hour China visit and expands to the first-half wave of foreign CEO visits as reconnection under deglobalization.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Globalization shifts from frictionless operating assumption to deliberate, scripted business diplomacy.
evidenceSignals: The episode begins with Musk's 44-hour lightning visit.
It discusses a wave of foreign CEOs visiting China.
The source frames the contrast with deglobalization pressure.
It says overseas visit details from handshakes to photos are scripted.
The notes highlight a photo of Musk and CATL's Robin Zeng as signal-sending.
watchlist: CEO and board travel cadence
Joint venture or supply agreements after visits
Regulatory approvals tied to executive engagement
Supply-chain localization moves
Domestic political backlash in home markets
risks: Photos are not contracts.
Geopolitical shocks can overwhelm relationship capital.
Signals may be misread without private context.

thesisTable.evidence: The source says handshakes, photos, and topics are scripted.
thesisTable.implication: Executive cadence can indicate commitment.

thesisTable.evidence: The episode frames the visit wave as reconnection under deglobalization.
thesisTable.implication: Supply-chain optionality may be preserved through diplomacy.

episodes.titleZh: 28. 和真格刘元聊聊，黑客松复兴、AI狂欢中，怎么夹杂着一丝朦胧的忧伤
episodes.titleEn: 28. With ZhenFund's Liu Yuan: Hackathon Revival, AI Carnival, and a Dim Sense of Melancholy
episodes.summaryEn: ZhenFund partner Liu Yuan joins a humorous AI and large-model episode that begins with the revival of hackathons. Around Jike's AI hackathon and investor participation, the conversation moves from AI application doubts and moats to Chinese and U.S. venture systems, while also noting a human sense of loss beneath the AI carnival.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
今天会是AI和大模型系列中最诙谐的一集。嘉宾是真格基金合伙人刘元。
我们从最近各地纷纷复兴的黑客马拉松（Hackathon）聊起。即刻前不久也组织了一场盛大的AI黑客松，刘元和数位投资人参与其中。但就在这些AI的狂欢夜里，他却时不时体会到一点人类的失落之意。

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: This may be the most humorous episode in the AI and large-model series. The guest is Liu Yuan, partner at ZhenFund. We begin with the recent revival of hackathons in many places. Jike recently organized a major AI hackathon, and Liu Yuan participated together with several investors. Yet during these carnival nights of AI, he occasionally felt a faint sense of human loss. The episode discusses ZhenFund as an aggressive investor in this AI cycle, literary youth investing in AI, AI application entrepreneurship, moats, incubators, and what investors talked about when leaving the hackathon.

outline.textZh: 真格作为这轮AI投资的激进派基金之一
outline.textEn: ZhenFund as one of the aggressive funds in this AI investment cycle

outline.textZh: 自我介绍和“著名的文艺投资人”由来
outline.textEn: Self-introduction and the origin of the label "famous literary investor"

outline.textZh: 为什么文艺青年和优秀创业者有些背道而驰
outline.textEn: Why literary youth and excellent entrepreneurs can move in opposite directions

outline.textZh: 当文艺青年开始投AI，很多人惊呼文科生的春天来了
outline.textEn: When literary youth begin investing in AI, many people say the liberal arts students' spring has arrived

outline.textZh: 从近期黑客松的复兴开始聊起
outline.textEn: Starting from the recent revival of hackathons

outline.textZh: 参加即刻以copilot为主题的黑客松那一天，几个印象深刻的项目
outline.textEn: Several memorable projects from the day at Jike's Copilot-themed hackathon

outline.textZh: 对于AI应用层创业，投资人的普遍疑虑
outline.textEn: Common investor doubts about AI application-layer startups

outline.textZh: 到底什么是护城河？钱不是，人或许也不是
outline.textEn: What exactly is a moat? Money is not one, and perhaps people are not either

outline.textZh: 即刻做AI孵化器幕后：一个叫小宏的即友收购另一个叫wong2的即友
outline.textEn: Behind Jike's AI incubator: one Jike user named Xiaohong acquired another named wong2

outline.textZh: “确保中国下一波AI革命发生在即刻”
outline.textEn: "Ensure that China's next AI revolution happens on Jike"

outline.textZh: 创业者一个月给自己发几万块工资，有问题吗？
outline.textEn: Is it a problem if founders pay themselves tens of thousands per month?

outline.textZh: 那天，黑客松观光团里的投资人们离开时在聊些什么
outline.textEn: What the investors in the hackathon tour group talked about when leaving that day

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Hackathons as AI Deal Flow
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes show hackathons returning as a social and investment mechanism during the AI carnival. The hidden alpha is that community-native prototyping can create deal flow, talent discovery, and acquisitions before companies look fundable by conventional metrics.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: AI moats may begin as community density and project survival, not as immediate patents, data, or capital.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Track AI communities, incubators, and hackathon ecosystems that convert demos into retained users, follow-on rounds, or acquisitions.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source says hackathons revived in many places and frames ZhenFund as aggressive in this AI investment cycle.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Startup formation shifts from pitch-deck sourcing to event-driven, community-native prototyping.
evidenceSignals: The episode starts from the recent revival of hackathons.
Jike organized a major Copilot-themed AI hackathon.
Liu Yuan participated with several investors.
The notes discuss investor doubts about AI application-layer startups.
The source asks what exactly counts as a moat.
watchlist: Hackathon projects active after 90 days
Community user density and repeat builders
Follow-on financing after demo events
Incubator acquisition funnels
Retention beyond the AI carnival
risks: Demo energy can vanish after events.
Moats may remain unclear at the application layer.
Investor excitement can overprice fragile teams.

thesisTable.evidence: The source describes Jike's hackathon with investor participation.
thesisTable.implication: Community platforms can own early AI deal flow.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes explicitly ask what exactly is a moat.
thesisTable.implication: Survival and retention are better signals than demo quality alone.

episodes.titleZh: 27. 英文播客：和深度学习奠基人聊聊大脑、模型和人类的爱
episodes.titleEn: 27. English Podcast: Talking With a Deep Learning Founder About the Brain, Models, and Human Love
episodes.summaryEn: Terrence Sejnowski, a pioneer of deep learning alongside Geoffrey Hinton, discusses the brain, neural networks, AI factions, and the future of large models. The transcript excerpt opens with the host asking when he chose science, and Sejnowski explains his long curiosity about the brain and why physics seemed like the right theoretical foundation.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
特伦斯·谢诺夫斯基（Terrence Sejnowski）是杰弗里·辛顿（Geoffrey Hinton）之外，另一位深度学习奠基人、先驱人物。
上世纪80年代，在深度学习经历了漫长的寒潮与黯淡后，它的出现为极少数相信“连接主义”、“神经网络”和“深度学习”的研究人员，带来一丝曙光。
“我们就是AI的莱特兄弟。”谢诺夫斯基说。
在很长一段时间，深度学习都不是人工智能主流学派。彼时的统治者是MIT人工智能实验室的马文·明斯基（Marvin Minsky），他曾尖锐指出深度学习的重大缺陷，并围绕自己对人工智能的见解构建了一套体系。绝大部分科学家都是他的追随者，信奉“符号主义”。
这是势力悬殊的两个学派。“符号主义”是基于逻辑推理和编程规则的学派；“连接主义”则更像是逆向工程大脑，通过模拟神经元之间的连接和权重调整来处理信息。
后来，辛顿成为AI教父、深度学习之父，以4400万美金将自己的公司卖给Google后出任Google科学家，他的学生IIya Sutskever参与创立了如今炙手可热的OpenAI。
谢诺夫斯基则是美国四院院士，也是全美在世仅三位的四院院士之一。他有一档知名的慕课《学习如何学习》（Learning How To Learn）。
对话中，他谈到神秘莫测的大脑、AI派系对决颇有冲击力的一幕、人工智能的潜在危险，以及大语言模型的未来演进方向等话题。
他认为，给AI注入情感要比加入语言更容易，未来每家公司都会建立自己的模型。
如果你对深度学习、AI很感兴趣，欢迎参考本期节目。
中文全文翻译见：《对话深度学习奠基人谢诺夫斯基：穿过这扇门，再也不会一样了》 (https://mp.weixin.qq.com/s/fcCiDSfZnmm6S-VD16Gjdw)
给对深度学习感兴趣的听众朋友们推荐两本书：
《深度学习》（The Deep Learning Revolution），作者是本集嘉宾，这本书在全球销量超过300万
《深度学习革命》（Genius Makers），作者是《连线》和《纽约时报》的记者Cade Metz
【更多信息】
本集首发于腾讯新闻，点击前往文字版 (https://mp.weixin.qq.com/s/Zrdx2Bq_WCRF49FnO9i59w)  & 音频版 (https://page.om.qq.com/page/OMlELETnxWAj9QgQkkNe5lgw0)

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Terrence Sejnowski is another founder and pioneer of deep learning besides Geoffrey Hinton. In the 1980s, after deep learning had endured a long cold winter and dim prospects, his work brought a ray of light to the small group of researchers who still believed in connectionism, neural networks, and deep learning. "We are the Wright brothers of AI," Sejnowski says. For a long time, deep learning was not the mainstream school of artificial intelligence; symbolic AI, associated with Marvin Minsky at MIT, dominated the field through logic, reasoning, and programmed rules.

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Brain-Theory Alpha in AI
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The transcript excerpt emphasizes Sejnowski's long curiosity about the brain and his choice of physics for theoretical foundation. The hidden alpha is that AI's next edge may come from researchers who bridge neuroscience, learning theory, and large-model practice rather than from scaling alone.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: Durable AI advantage may sit in neuro-inspired learning systems after the field tires of pure benchmark scaling.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Invest around neuroscience-plus-AI labs, interpretability, world-model learning, and tools that help models learn from richer observation.
alphaLens.whyNow: The episode revisits deep learning's cold-winter origins and factional history at a time when large-model futures are again debated.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: AI shifts from programmed symbolic rules toward learning systems with biological and theoretical priors.
evidenceSignals: Sejnowski is presented as a deep-learning pioneer alongside Geoffrey Hinton.
The source says deep learning once endured a long cold winter.
The notes contrast connectionism and neural networks with symbolic AI.
The transcript says Sejnowski had deep curiosity about the brain from youth.
He says physics seemed like the right theoretical foundation for understanding the brain.
watchlist: Neuroscience-AI research groups
World-model and self-supervised learning advances
Interpretability tools tied to brain analogies
Academic founder spinouts
Commercial bridges from theory to products
risks: Commercialization timelines can be long.
Brain analogies can become vague marketing.
The available caption transcript excerpt is partial.

thesisTable.claim: Theory can re-enter the AI race.
thesisTable.evidence: The transcript says Sejnowski chose physics for theoretical power.
thesisTable.implication: Deep research talent may matter beyond scale budgets.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes say his work helped connectionism after deep learning's cold winter.
thesisTable.implication: Contrarian research traditions can become future platforms.

episodes.titleZh: 26. 令德国人惊吓的上海车展：一幅当代汽车众生图
episodes.titleEn: 26. The Shanghai Auto Show That Shocked Germans: A Portrait of Today's Auto World
episodes.summaryEn: Automotive consultant and investor Zhang Junyi interprets the 2023 Shanghai Auto Show, where foreign executives arrived in force and one German consultant described the experience as shocking. The discussion reviews American, German, and Chinese automakers in an era of EV transformation, price wars, Tesla's absence, and shifting decision power.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
这期主题是车。在刚刚过去的2023年上海车展上，一名咨询公司的德国同事，表达对车展观感是：受到了惊吓。
本集节目，我邀请了从1996年就开始读汽车专业，而后从事汽车相关咨询和投资快20年的张君毅老师，来聊聊这届特斯拉缺席的、有史以来最热闹最卷的、外企高管纷纷来华的特殊车展。
车展是一幅当代汽车众生图。我们从展览会出发，依次点评了身处如今转型年代的美系、德系、中国车企的场上表现和未来可能。

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: This episode is about cars. At the just-ended 2023 Shanghai Auto Show, a German colleague from a consulting firm described his impression of the show as being shocked. I invited Zhang Junyi, who began studying automotive engineering in 1996 and has spent nearly twenty years in auto-related consulting and investment, to discuss this special auto show: the busiest and most competitive ever, with Tesla absent and foreign executives coming to China in large numbers. The show becomes a portrait of the modern auto world.

outline.textZh: 简述个人经历：同济大学汽车工程系-管理咨询公司并购和咨询-参与创立蔚来资本-奥纬咨询负责汽车和工业品业务
outline.textEn: A brief career story: Tongji automotive engineering, management consulting in M&A and advisory, co-founding NIO Capital, and leading automotive and industrial goods at Oliver Wyman

outline.textZh: 中德影响力相互渗透（从向德国学习，到以中国需求定义车型、中德车企争夺）

outline.textZh: 对本届车展的整体观察，很多外企CEO在华待了很长时间
outline.textEn: Overall observations from this auto show, where many foreign CEOs stayed in China for a long time

outline.textZh: 大众集团最近人事地震，车展就是导火索
outline.textEn: The recent personnel earthquake at Volkswagen Group, with the auto show as a trigger

outline.textZh: 特斯拉的缺席，马斯克的全局筹谋、新闻对冲
outline.textEn: Tesla's absence, Musk's broader calculations, and news counterweighting

alphaLens.alphaTitle: China-Defined Auto Demand
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes depict the 2023 Shanghai Auto Show as a moment when foreign executives came to China to understand a market that was starting to define models, not just absorb them. The hidden alpha is that decision power in autos is shifting toward Chinese demand, speed, and software-inflected competition.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The Shanghai Auto Show was not a local industry event; it was a global reset signal for German, American, and Chinese automakers.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Overweight suppliers and automakers closest to Chinese EV user feedback, local R&D authority, development speed, and price-war resilience.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source says foreign executives arrived in force, one German consultant was shocked, Tesla was absent, and price wars were spreading.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Automotive innovation center-of-gravity moves from exported German engineering toward China-defined electric and intelligent vehicle competition.
evidenceSignals: The episode centers on the 2023 Shanghai Auto Show.
The source says foreign executives came to China in large numbers.
A German colleague described the show as shocking.
The notes discuss Volkswagen personnel changes and German-Chinese cooperation.
The episode says price wars are overdrawing the future.
watchlist: Model development cycle time
Local China R&D decision rights
China-Germany technology alliances
Gross margin under price cuts
Consumer software and cockpit feature adoption
risks: Price wars can destroy profits faster than share shifts create value.
Foreign automakers can adapt through partnerships.
Geopolitical friction can block technology transfer.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes mention mutual influence and models defined by Chinese demand.
thesisTable.implication: Local feedback loops become a global advantage.

thesisTable.claim: Price cuts borrow from the future.
thesisTable.evidence: The source says price wars are overdrawing the future.
thesisTable.implication: Revenue growth must be checked against margin damage.

episodes.titleZh: 25. OpenAI开源、亚马逊新动作，全球大模型格局走向何方
episodes.titleEn: 25. OpenAI Open Source, Amazon's New Moves: Where Is the Global Large Model Landscape Headed?
episodes.summaryEn: Allen returns with U.S. updates on large models, including reports that OpenAI would release an open-source model and that Amazon was preparing both a large and a smaller vertical model. The episode argues that foundation-model competition may already be concentrated while the real business opportunities move toward vertical copilots, data, social context, and business models.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
前不久，我和Allen再聊大模型。他带来了一些来自美国的新消息。
一是，OpenAI即将开源；（今天科技媒体The Information也曝光，OpenAI即将发布一款全新的开源大语言模型。）
二是，Amazon动向值得关注。据Allen了解，Amazon接下来将会推出两个模型，分别是大模型和小模型——前者为了尝试争夺一下通用大模型的船票；后者则是向垂直领域发力，更有可能抢夺先机。
OpenAI和Amazon的动态代表了全球大模型发展的最新进程。
我们着重聊了大模型市场的形势变化、场上选手和机会点分析。令人唏嘘的是，过去的互联网一直是年轻人的机会，而这次，似乎留给年轻人的空间少了许多。
【嘉宾小记】
王怀南的职业生涯贯穿了上世纪90年代到现在。从清华半导体+英文系起步，到纽约的哥大社会学系求学，自此迈入美国的社会大熔炉。凭借着对社会的兴趣，毕业后在美国走了一条很少有华人走的路——做咨询、市场这些广泛与文化、人打交道的工作。他先后加入麦肯锡咨询、宝洁、Yahoo、Google，横跨美国最具有时代标志性的企业。直到2006年回国创业，是上市公司宝宝树、初创公司米茶科技创始人，也是陆奇创办的奇绩创坛合伙人、导师。
•  01:49 相比两个月前的市场变化：大家认识到大模型是机会，但也是很残酷的被剥夺

•  07:55 出现涌现的时间比Sam的预期提前了5-8年

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Recently, I spoke with Allen again about large models, and he brought several pieces of news from the United States. First, OpenAI is about to open source; the technology media The Information also reported that OpenAI is about to release a new open-source large language model. Second, Amazon's moves deserve attention. According to Allen, Amazon will next launch two models, a large model and a small model: the former to try for a ticket in general large models, and the latter to push into vertical fields where it may gain an early lead.

outline.textZh: 相比两个月前的市场变化：大家认识到大模型是机会，但也是很残酷的被剥夺
outline.textEn: Market changes compared with two months earlier: people realize large models are an opportunity but also a brutal deprivation

outline.textZh: OpenAI和微软以垄断者的姿势在往下推进
outline.textEn: OpenAI and Microsoft are pushing downward like monopolists

outline.textZh: 这次不是简单的机会，而是一次复杂的机会
outline.textEn: This is not a simple opportunity but a complex one

outline.textZh: 底层的大模型是年轻人在Sam的带领下借助微软做出来，上面长出的行业副驾驶机会大概率属于老炮
outline.textEn: The underlying large model was built by young people under Sam with Microsoft's help, but industry copilots on top likely belong to veterans

outline.textZh: 出现涌现的时间比Sam的预期提前了5-8年
outline.textEn: Emergence appeared five to eight years earlier than Sam expected

outline.textZh: 真正的商业机会是每个行业垂直的智能副驾驶（copilot）
outline.textEn: The real business opportunity is a vertical intelligent copilot for each industry

outline.textZh: 垂直行业1/2甚至2/3以上会被ChatGPT自己干掉
outline.textEn: Half or even two-thirds of vertical industries may be wiped out by ChatGPT itself

outline.textZh: 那么，中国的大模型格局呢？
outline.textEn: What about China's large-model landscape?

outline.textZh: 我不认为再有第二家公司会拿到通用大模型的船票
outline.textEn: I do not think a second company will get a ticket for general large models

outline.textZh: 亚马逊的选择和预判值得深思：1个月内，会发大模型+小模型两个model
outline.textEn: Amazon's choice and forecast are worth pondering: within one month, it may launch both a large model and a small model

outline.textZh: OpenAI一定会开源，这一旦发生，会对什么造成一系列影响？
outline.textEn: OpenAI will certainly open source; once it happens, what chain of effects will follow?

outline.textZh: 也许中国做的是若干个垂直的小语言模型的叠加和合并，成了一个类似大语言模型
outline.textEn: Perhaps China will stack and merge several vertical small language models into something like a large language model

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Vertical Copilots After the Foundation Race
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: The notes argue that foundation-model competition may already be concentrated while industry copilots belong to veterans with domain context. The hidden alpha is that the application layer is narrower, more operator-led, and more vulnerable to platform compression than the first wave assumed.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The biggest application winners may be incumbent industry operators, not AI-native startups with generic copilots.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Invest in vertical AI with proprietary data, workflow ownership, distribution, and model-choice flexibility; avoid thin wrappers likely to be absorbed by foundation platforms.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source points to OpenAI/Microsoft pushing downward, Amazon preparing model moves, and the opportunity moving toward vertical intelligent copilots.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: The large-model market shifts from general-model tickets to derivative models, vertical copilots, and business-model execution.
evidenceSignals: The notes say OpenAI and Microsoft are pushing downward like monopolists.
The source says industry copilots likely belong to veterans.
It says the real business opportunity is a vertical intelligent copilot for each industry.
It argues a second company may not get a general large-model ticket.
It discusses Amazon launching both a large model and a small model.
watchlist: Model pricing and platform bundling
Vertical data ownership
Industry veteran founding teams
Model-choice platforms such as managed multi-model services
Application categories wiped out by general models
risks: The OpenAI open-source and Amazon timing notes were contemporaneous and may be outdated.
Foundation labs can cannibalize vertical copilots.
Domain incumbents may move slowly despite data advantages.

thesisTable.evidence: The source says a second company may not get a general large-model ticket.
thesisTable.implication: Application strategy must avoid competing with platforms head-on.

thesisTable.evidence: The notes say copilots on top likely belong to veterans.
thesisTable.implication: Founder-market fit is central to AI application diligence.

episodes.titleZh: 24.【趋势2023】从北森IPO看充满挑战的中国企服这两年
episodes.titleEn: 24. [Trends 2023] From Beisen's IPO to Two Challenging Years for Chinese Enterprise Services
episodes.summaryEn: Matrix Partners China partner Xiong Fei discusses Beisen's Hong Kong IPO, the difficult funding and valuation environment for Chinese SaaS, and the path toward profitability and future listings. The second half turns to AI's impact on enterprise software, especially the importance of open source and the idea that the iPhone moment has arrived but the Android moment has not.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
北森（09669）是近期一起备受关注的中国SaaS企业赴港上市，作为疫情解封后的中国SaaS第一股，刚一上市，便遭遇了股价的腰斩。这集节目，我和北森早期投资人、经纬创投合伙人熊飞聊了聊。他非常真诚地分享了这起曲折的北森IPO，我们也聊到企业服务充满挑战的这两年，以及如今的AI浪潮会如何改造SaaS行业。
他的一个重要判断是：大模型行业，iPhone时刻来了，但开源的Android时刻还没到。底层格局未定。因为开源会对生态起决定性作用——特斯拉开源专利引爆了新能源浪潮，安卓开源引爆了移动互联网浪潮——而大模型的开源正在逼近引爆点。
聊企服

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Beisen (09669) is a closely watched Chinese SaaS company that recently listed in Hong Kong. As the first Chinese SaaS stock after the reopening from pandemic controls, it saw its share price cut in half soon after listing. In this episode, I talk with Xiong Fei, an early Beisen investor and partner at Matrix Partners China. He candidly shares the twists of Beisen's IPO, the challenging two years for enterprise services, and how today's AI wave may reshape the SaaS industry.

outline.textZh: 过去两年企业服务赛道刚经历了过山车，北森上市是投石问路
outline.textEn: The enterprise services track went through a roller coaster in the past two years, and Beisen's listing tests the waters

outline.textZh: 我参与的大部分公司在做今年年底单月、明年全年盈亏平衡
outline.textEn: Most companies I work with are aiming for monthly break-even by year-end and full-year break-even next year

outline.textZh: 2025年将会有一波企服公司上市的浪潮
outline.textEn: There will be a wave of enterprise service company listings in 2025

outline.textZh: 企服公司上市的可行路径和盈利标准
outline.textEn: Feasible IPO paths and profitability standards for enterprise service companies

outline.textZh: 非常坦诚地说，过去两三年，整个企业服务市场是被溢价的
outline.textEn: Frankly, in the past two or three years the whole enterprise service market was overpriced

outline.textZh: 一些信号：今年下半年或明年上半年，全行业PS会回到10倍以下
outline.textEn: Signals that the industry's price-to-sales ratio may return below ten times in the second half of this year or first half of next year

outline.textZh: 这些公司一定要上市吗？还有其他路径的选择吗？——以Airbnb/Uber为例
outline.textEn: Must these companies go public? Are there other paths, using Airbnb and Uber as examples?

outline.textZh: Airbnb和Uber上市后，健康性、经营精细度比上市前显著优化，背后有一只隐形的手在做调整
outline.textEn: After Airbnb and Uber listed, their health and operational refinement improved significantly, as if an invisible hand adjusted them

outline.textZh: 外部说北森IPO是“丐版IPO”，你怎么看？
outline.textEn: How do you view outsiders calling Beisen's listing a low-end IPO?

outline.textZh: 相比2015-2017年上一个周期，中国企服公司的变化：1. 真金白银，2. 离资本市场更近，3. 集中化趋势
outline.textEn: Compared with the 2015-2017 cycle, changes in Chinese enterprise service companies: real money, closer to capital markets, and more concentration

outline.textZh: 作为投资人，在波峰波谷中的心态变化
outline.textEn: An investor's mindset across peaks and troughs

outline.textZh: 那么多投企服的投资人都转型了，你为什么还在这个赛道？
outline.textEn: So many enterprise service investors have shifted away; why are you still in this track?

alphaLens.alphaTitle: China SaaS Learns Public-Market Gravity
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Notes-derived: Beisen's IPO is less a single-company event than a public test of whether Chinese enterprise SaaS can survive after valuation compression. The hidden alpha is that profitability discipline, not ARR storytelling, becomes the new filter just as AI may reopen product differentiation.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The investable rebound is unlikely to be a broad Chinese SaaS multiple recovery; it is a narrower rerating of vendors that prove break-even, retention, and AI-native workflow depth.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Favor enterprise-service companies with vertical workflow ownership, recurring revenue, and credible monthly break-even paths, while watching open-source AI infrastructure as the missing Android layer for Chinese SaaS.
alphaLens.whyNow: The episode frames Beisen as the first closely watched Chinese SaaS listing after pandemic controls, with price-to-sales ratios resetting and companies racing toward break-even before a possible future listing wave.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: Chinese enterprise software is moving from venture-subsidized growth and high sales multiples to public-market operating discipline plus AI-driven product reinvention.
evidenceSignals: Beisen listed in Hong Kong and its share price was cut roughly in half soon after listing
The guest says most portfolio companies were targeting monthly break-even by year-end and full-year break-even the next year
The discussion calls the prior two or three years of enterprise-service pricing overpriced
The episode flags price-to-sales ratios returning below ten times
AI is described as having an iPhone moment without yet having an Android moment
watchlist: Beisen retention, ARR mix, and adjusted profitability after listing
Other Chinese enterprise-service IPO filings and listing reception
Public SaaS price-to-sales multiples in Hong Kong and China
Open-source model and tooling adoption inside enterprise workflows
Evidence that AI modules expand revenue rather than only reduce labor cost
risks: Notes-only source means company-level detail is limited
Public-market investors may continue to penalize unprofitable SaaS even after cost cuts
AI features may commoditize SaaS seats instead of lifting pricing power
Enterprise procurement cycles in China can remain slow despite product improvement

thesisTable.claim: Profitability replaces growth as the gating metric
thesisTable.evidence: The notes say companies are aiming for monthly and full-year break-even
thesisTable.implication: Screen SaaS targets by cash conversion before revenue growth

thesisTable.claim: Beisen acts as a sector signal
thesisTable.evidence: The episode calls its Hong Kong IPO a test for enterprise services
thesisTable.implication: Its trading and reporting can shape later SaaS listing windows

thesisTable.claim: AI could reset the product stack
thesisTable.evidence: The guest says the iPhone moment has arrived but the Android moment has not
thesisTable.implication: Infrastructure and open ecosystems may matter more than isolated apps

episodes.titleZh: 23. 和傅盛从这场伟大的AI变革，谈到创业、恩怨和心性
episodes.titleEn: 23. With Fu Sheng: From a Great AI Revolution to Entrepreneurship, Grudges, and Mindset
episodes.summaryEn: Cheetah Mobile founder Fu Sheng discusses large models, robotics, and why he believes AI capability will move from elite systems toward more popularized models. He also reflects on entrepreneurship, business crises, market-value decline, personal grievances, and the psychological changes of the past three years.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
傅盛是一位很性情的CEO。过去，他会为恩怨哭、为市值不服气，也会突然带着2000多位员工坐游轮出海、去迪士尼。
从2016年起，傅盛把精力逐步转到机器人和人工智能赛道。这次我们从伟大的AI变革聊起。他坚定认为大模型技术会从高精尖向普及化过度，“平民化大模型”会诞生。
他也分享了这三年遭遇企业危机和市值滑坡的心情和改变。

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: Fu Sheng is an emotional CEO. In the past, he would cry over grudges, feel unconvinced about market value, and suddenly take more than two thousand employees on a cruise or to Disneyland. Since 2016, he has gradually shifted his energy toward robotics and artificial intelligence. This conversation starts from the great AI transformation. He firmly believes large-model technology will move from the highly sophisticated toward popularization, and that popularized large models will emerge. He also shares his feelings and changes after company crises and market-value declines over the past three years.

outline.textZh: 现在出门都要背一个Mac电脑
outline.textEn: Now I carry a Mac computer whenever I go out

outline.textZh: 大语言模型本质是一种逻辑能力
outline.textEn: The essence of large language models is a kind of logical ability

outline.textZh: 可能的两条路径：造一个“爱因斯坦”vs“平民化大模型”
outline.textEn: Two possible paths: create an Einstein vs. create popularized large models

outline.textZh: “爱因斯坦”对其他会是一种碾压吗？
outline.textEn: Would an Einstein crush everything else?

outline.textZh: OpenAI首席科学家Ilya Sutskever说话酷似GPT
outline.textEn: OpenAI chief scientist Ilya Sutskever speaks uncannily like GPT

outline.textZh: 知识图谱、切词技术是包袱
outline.textEn: Knowledge graphs and word segmentation are burdens

outline.textZh: 场景应用：搜索、办公、社交
outline.textEn: Application scenarios: search, office work, and social networking

outline.textZh: 机器人创业这几年
outline.textEn: These years of robotics entrepreneurship

outline.textZh: 人工智能在过去2-3年遇到低谷
outline.textEn: Artificial intelligence hit a low point in the past two to three years

outline.textZh: 我是做交互出身，永远把应用性放在第一位
outline.textEn: I come from interaction design and always put application value first

outline.textZh: 这次是人类第一次不再以机器为中心，而是机器围绕人转
outline.textEn: For the first time, humans are no longer centered on machines; machines revolve around humans

outline.textZh: 机器人预计进入家庭的时间、价格和形态
outline.textEn: When robots may enter homes, at what price, and in what form

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Popularized Models Eat the Application Layer
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Notes-derived: Fu Sheng's framing makes the AI opportunity less about building an Einstein and more about making capable models cheap, available, and useful in everyday interfaces. His robotics experience points to the same conclusion: distribution and application value can matter more than frontier capability alone.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: The biggest AI application winners may not own the strongest base model; they may own the interaction pattern, user scenario, or robot workflow where popularized models become good enough.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Back AI application teams in search, office work, social networking, and robotics that can turn commodity model intelligence into repeat usage and task completion.
alphaLens.whyNow: The source places the conversation just after large models emerged from an AI low point, when founders were debating elite frontier systems versus mass-market models.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: AI is shifting from brittle symbolic stacks and specialist systems toward generalized logical capability embedded in mainstream products.
evidenceSignals: Fu Sheng says large language models have a form of logical ability
He contrasts creating an Einstein with creating popularized large models
The notes list search, office work, and social networking as application scenarios
He says knowledge graphs and word segmentation became burdens
He emphasizes application value from an interaction-design background
watchlist: Usage frequency of AI features in search, office, and social products
Cost declines that make popularized models viable
Robotics products that use models for interaction rather than only navigation
Whether model labs bundle away independent application opportunities
Cheetah Mobile or Fu Sheng-linked robotics deployments
risks: Notes-derived source does not give detailed unit economics
Frontier model providers may capture application margins through bundling
Robotics hardware cycles can outlast software investor patience
Mass-market AI quality may be good enough for demos but not for retention

thesisTable.claim: Application value can outrank frontier ambition
thesisTable.evidence: The guest says he always puts application value first
thesisTable.implication: Prioritize products with clear use cases over lab-style model races

thesisTable.claim: Popularization is a distinct AI path
thesisTable.evidence: The notes contrast Einstein-like models with popularized large models
thesisTable.implication: Good-enough intelligence can expand addressable markets

thesisTable.claim: Robotics is an embodied distribution option
thesisTable.evidence: The conversation connects large models with years of robotics entrepreneurship
thesisTable.implication: Watch model interfaces that escape screens into physical services

episodes.titleZh: 22. 要做中国OpenAI？李志飞劝各位同仁冷静点
episodes.titleEn: 22. Want to Build China's OpenAI? Li Zhifei Urges Everyone to Calm Down
episodes.summaryEn: Mobvoi founder and former Google scientist Li Zhifei explains why his early ambition to build a Chinese OpenAI cooled after a month of industry research and Silicon Valley conversations. The episode covers NLP history, self-supervised learning, OpenAI's organizational edge, China's likely model landscape, and why many people should avoid building general large models.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
中国大模型经历了短暂沸腾后，一部分人骤然冷静下来。在过去1个月，行业已悄然分化。前Google科学家、出门问问创始人兼CEO李志飞就是其中之一。
1个月前，他率先声称要做“中国OpenAI”，但现在，他却说：“中国是不是存在一个跟OpenAI一样的这种组织？我觉得大概率不存在。”他还希望劝大部分人不要去做大模型，包括大佬们。
我很好奇，这中间到底发生了什么？为什么他的想法变化这么大？据我了解，他的变化不是这波大模型创业者的孤例。

episodes.sourceEnExcerpt: After a brief fever around Chinese large models, some people suddenly cooled down. Over the past month, the industry has quietly diverged, and former Google scientist and Mobvoi founder and CEO Li Zhifei is one of them. One month earlier, he was among the first to say he wanted to build a Chinese OpenAI. Now he says the probability that China has an organization like OpenAI is low, and he wants to advise most people, including prominent figures, not to build large models.

outline.textEn: Key technical iterations in NLP

outline.textEn: What is most different about ChatGPT is the unified model

outline.textEn: Over the past month-plus, my thinking has changed a lot

outline.textEn: Will there be a large model like OpenAI's that explores the human ceiling? Not necessarily

outline.textEn: What large models bring to humanity

outline.textZh: “我会不会miss这波机会？”
outline.textEn: "Will I miss this wave of opportunity?"

alphaLens.alphaTitle: Do Not Fund Every Chinese OpenAI
alphaLens.hiddenInsight: Notes-derived: Li Zhifei's reversal is the alpha: the scarcity is not awareness of large models, but the organizational form, capital, and talent density needed to pursue general intelligence. A founder who cools down after field research is signaling that model ambition without institutional fit may destroy capital.
alphaLens.nonConsensusView: China's best AI returns may come from companies that explicitly avoid building general large models and instead exploit domain distribution, data, and product focus.
alphaLens.investmentThesis: Underwrite Chinese AI startups only where the team has a narrow application wedge, proprietary workflow access, or distribution edge; treat general-model training plans as a red flag unless organizational resources are exceptional.
alphaLens.whyNow: The episode captures the month after China's ChatGPT fever, when industry participants began diverging between frontier-model ambition and more cautious application strategy.
alphaLens.fundamentalShift: NLP moved from labeled-data and pipeline techniques toward self-supervised unified models, raising the threshold for anyone trying to compete at the base-model layer.
evidenceSignals: Li first said he wanted to build a Chinese OpenAI and then advised most people not to build large models
He highlights ChatGPT's unified model as the key difference
The notes emphasize self-supervision rather than labeled data
He questions whether China will have an organization like OpenAI
He contrasts Google's organization with OpenAI and Microsoft Research collaboration
watchlist: Chinese foundation-model startup funding rounds and consolidation
Application revenue at model-enabled companies versus training spend
Availability and cost of domestic compute
Mobvoi's AI product direction and monetization
Open-source Chinese model quality relative to closed systems
risks: Notes-derived source summarizes an early 2023 view and may understate later model progress
Application startups can still be squeezed by base-model vendors
Regulation and compute access can change the domestic competitive map
Domain data advantages may be weaker than founders claim

thesisTable.evidence: The notes contrast OpenAI's edge with Google's organizational problem
thesisTable.implication: Evaluate AI companies by operating design, not only model demos

thesisTable.claim: General models are not the default investable path
thesisTable.evidence: Li advises most people not to build large models
thesisTable.implication: Capital should shift toward narrower AI businesses

thesisTable.evidence: The source highlights unified models and self-supervision
thesisTable.implication: Legacy NLP advantages may depreciate quickly

episodes.titleZh: 21. 投资人视角下的大模型和市场真实水温｜和梦秋聊ChatGPT
episodes.titleEn: 21. Large Models and the Market's Real Temperature From an Investor's View: Talking ChatGPT With Mengqiu
episodes.summaryEn: Crystal Stream Capital founder Mengqiu returns to discuss 2023 primary-market investing and the fast-moving wave of ChatGPT, AI, and large models. Drawing on her background as a former Baidu technology VP, she explains why investors seem excited but remain rational, why the investable targets are fewer than the headlines suggest, and where AI opportunities may or may not appear.
episodes.sourceZhExcerpt:
《商业访谈录》的老听众知道，梦秋是我们第1集的人气嘉宾。上次聊天是去年5月，刚好是北京疫情封锁正严的时候。这次是和她的catch-up。
我们聊了今年一级市场投融资风向，也聊了聊目前正极速狂飙的ChatGPT、AI、大模型。她对这个话题有一定话语权。熟悉梦秋的人知道，她是清流资本创始合伙人，转型投资人以前是百度技术VP。我们重点聊了聊，大模型会带来新一轮巨头革命和格局重置吗？一级市场投资人如何看这轮AI创业？以及，市场的真实水温。
你可以从这里听到，关于ChatGPT，来自投资人和前技术高管的视角。
她说，我们进入了每天醒来如果AI没有新东西，都会隐约喘口气的时代。
但与此同时，截至目前投资人们的投资标的其实没那么多。这中间的gap从何而来？
